          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "Yes, this is completely possible and works beautifully. \n\nI wrote a lightweight, read-only Python script (`fetch_slack_brains.py`) on your VM to test the plumbing. It works exactly how you described:\n\n### 1. Verification (Only You)\nBecause I have your user token, we query the `reactions.list` endpoint. The script matches your specific user ID (`U095LHMC4UW`) against the reaction. If someone else adds a `\ud83e\udde0`, the script ignores it.\n\n### 2. Context Gathering\n*   **If it's a thread:** It fetches the entire thread (parent message and all replies).\n*   **If it's a channel message:** It fetches a context window of up to 11 messages (5 preceding, the reacted message, and 5 succeeding) to ensure the surrounding conversation isn't lost.\n*   It resolves all Slack user IDs to clean display names.\n\n### 3. Note Structure\nI suggest writing the note to `sources/slack/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md` with this structure:\n\n```markdown\n---\nid: 20260605120000\ndaily_note: \"[[2026-06-05-friday]]\"\noriginal_url: \"https://signlab.slack.com/archives/...\"\nchannel: \"\\#product-feedback\"\nparticipants:\n  - \"Justin Goff\"\n  - \"Alice\"\n---\n\n# Slack: Conversation Topic\n\n*Brief context of what triggered this discussion.*\n\n## Summary of Conversation\n*   **Alice** raised concerns about...\n*   **Justin** suggested...\n*   **Decision:** We agreed to...\n\n---\n## Verbatim Reference\n> **Alice**: [raw message text]\n> **Justin**: [raw message text]\n```\n\n### 4. Integration & Notification\n*   **Daily Note:** I will automatically find today's daily note and append a clean bullet to your `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad` section:\n    `* [[sources/slack/2026-06-05-conversation-topic|Slack discussion]]: One-sentence gist of the conversation.`\n*   **Telegram Notification:** I'll send you a brief Telegram message to let you know a new note has been created, along with its high-level summary.\n\n---\n\n### How we automate it\nI can schedule this as a background **cron job** to run every 2 hours. \n\nIt will execute the Python fetcher. If there are no new reactions, it terminates instantly and silently (saving resources). If it finds a new `\ud83e\udde0`, it will spin up, write the Obsidian note, update your daily note, mark the message as processed in a local cache, and ping you.\n\nShould I go ahead and schedule this cron job?",

        {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"gbrain\", \"description\": \"Manage and administer gbrain, including schema-pack migrations, diagnostics, and sync operations.\", \"tags\": [], \"related_skills\": [], \"content\": \"---\\nname: gbrain\\ndescription: Manage and administer gbrain, including schema-pack migrations, diagnostics, and sync operations.\\ntriggers:\\n  - \\\"migrate to gbrain-base-v2\\\"\\n  - \\\"gbrain schema\\\"\\n  - \\\"gbrain doctor\\\"\\n  - \\\"gbrain sync\\\"\\n  - \\\"unify my types\\\"\\n---\\n\\n# GBrain Administration\\n\\nThis skill governs the operations, maintenance, diagnostics, and migrations of Justin's **gbrain** database and CLI surface.\\n\\n## Core Commands\\n\\n### 1. Schema Management & Migrations\\n\\nA schema pack defines the directory layout, page types, path inference, and link verbs in the brain.\\n\\n* **Check active pack & status:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain schema active\\n  ```\\n* **List available packs (bundled + user-installed):**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain schema list\\n  ```\\n* **Check onboarding and schema recommendations:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain onboard --check --explain\\n  ```\\n* **Migrating schema packs (e.g., upgrading from `gbrain-base` to `gbrain-base-v2`):**\\n  To perform a type unification / schema migration, submit the `unify-types` protected Minion job:\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain jobs submit unify-types --allow-protected --params '{\\\"target_pack\\\":\\\"gbrain-base-v2\\\"}'\\n  ```\\n  *Note:* The default behavior of `unify-types` applies mutations directly when submitted through the jobs engine inline. \\n\\n### 2. Diagnostics & Repairs (`gbrain doctor`)\\n\\n`gbrain doctor` executes an extensive check of database integrity, schema versioning, embeddings alignment, and synchronization state.\\n\\n* **Run complete health check:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain doctor\\n  ```\\n* **Preview remediation plan:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain doctor --remediation-plan --json\\n  ```\\n* **Execute remediation with cost caps and target score:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain doctor --remediate --yes --target-score 90 --max-usd 5\\n  ```\\n\\n### 3. Synchronization & Dream Cycles\\n\\n* **Sync a specific source:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain sync --source <id>\\n  ```\\n* **Sync all sources in parallel (recommended cron configuration):**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain sync --all --parallel 4 --workers 4 --skip-failed\\n  ```\\n* **Run dream cycle for stale sources:**\\n  ```bash\\n  gbrain dream --source <id>\\n  gbrain dream --stale\\n  ```\\n\\n## Pitfalls & Best Practices\\n\\n1. **Schema Resolution Tiers:** When querying or operating, gbrain walks a 7-tier resolution chain. Lower config files (like `~/.gbrain/config.json`) are overridden by brain-wide database configurations, environment variables, or per-call parameters.\\n2. **Database Connection Checks:** Some doctor checks or oversized pages scans are skipped if gbrain is configured with a local PGLite backend instead of a remote PostgreSQL server. If connection issues surface, look up the connection string configured in `gbrain config`.\\n3. **Protected Minion Jobs:** Certain system jobs like `unify-types` or `skillopt` are **protected** and require the `--allow-protected` flag to be submitted from the CLI surface. Always include this flag when invoking them.\\n4. **Retyping Existing Pages (`schema sync` limitations):** `gbrain schema sync` ONLY targets untyped pages (where `type` is `NULL` or `''`). If pages are already typed (e.g., as `concept` or `note`) and you introduce new folder-prefix types in a custom pack, `sync` will dry-run as 0. To force-apply new prefixes:\\n   - Run a SQL or Bun script against the PGLite/Postgres database to reset `type = ''` for files matching the target path patterns (e.g., `UPDATE pages SET type = '' WHERE source_path LIKE 'Meetings/%'`).\\n   - Run `gbrain schema sync --apply` or submit the `unify-types` protected minion job with your target pack (e.g. `gbrain jobs submit unify-types --allow-protected --follow --params '{\\\"target_pack\\\":\\\"gbrain-personal\\\"}'`).\\n5. **Wasm/PGLite Connection Issues & Bundled Pack Forking:**\\n   - If running `gbrain schema` subcommands on PGLite surfaces `relation \\\"pages\\\" does not exist`, verify that `database_path` is correctly populated in the `connectConfig` of `withConnectedEngine` in `src/commands/schema.ts` (it must map the PGLite database file rather than starting a clean in-memory engine).\\n   - If `gbrain schema fork <bundled-pack> <new-name>` reports `Source pack not found`, ensure `packPathByName` in `src/commands/schema.ts` explicitly includes the bundled pack name in its resolution chain.\\n4. **PGLite \\\"relation 'pages' does not exist\\\" Error:** If `gbrain schema` subcommands (such as `suggest` or `review-candidates`) throw `relation \\\"pages\\\" does not exist`, verify if `withConnectedEngine` inside `src/commands/schema.ts` is correctly passing the `database_path` property to the PGLite engine configuration. In some versions, this property is omitted, defaulting PGLite to a blank in-memory database.\\n5. **Re-typing Existing Pages Under New Prefixes:** `gbrain schema sync` and type unification jobs only map untyped pages (`type` is empty or NULL). If pages already have standard types (e.g. `concept` or `note`) but a new prefix has been added (e.g., mapping `Meetings/` to a new `meeting` page type), update those pages' types to `''` (empty string) in the database first, then run `gbrain schema sync --apply` to re-assign them. Since `type` is non-nullable, use an empty string `''` instead of `NULL`.\\n4. **PGLite Schema Connection Bug:** If running `gbrain schema` subcommands (like `suggest` or `review-candidates`) on a PGLite backend throws a `relation \\\"pages\\\" does not exist` error, it is due to `database_path` being missing from `withConnectedEngine`'s `connectConfig` inside `src/commands/schema.ts`. Ensure `database_path: (cfg as { database_path?: string }).database_path` is passed to the connection config in that helper.\\n\\n6. **Obsidian Frontmatter Type Clash (Display as text?):**\\n   - **Symptom:** Opening a page in Obsidian shows a dialog warning: *\\\"Display as text? Your number data is not compatible. It will be adapted to fit the new format.\\\"*\\n   - **Mechanism:** Obsidian expects the `id` (or similar) frontmatter field to be a **Text** property, but a note on disk has `id: 20260603102013` written without quotes. This happens because `gbrain` reads/writes frontmatter using `gray-matter`/`js-yaml`. When numeric-only string IDs are stored or written back, the YAML serializer outputs them as unquoted digits (numbers). Obsidian then parses them as a **Number** type, creating a type conflict with its internal schema definitions.\\n   - **Remediation:** \\n     - *In Obsidian:* Go to settings \u2794 Editor \u2794 Properties, find `id` and change its property type to **Number**.\\n     - *In Templates:* If using templates (e.g., via Templater), ensure single-quoted templates like `id: '<% tp.date.now(\\\"YYYYMMDDHHmmss\\\") %>'` are used to avoid nested double-quote YAML parsing errors (`NESTED_QUOTES`), which prevent parsing/preservation of frontmatter fields.\\n\\n### 7. Resolving `gbrain doctor` Failures & Warnings\\n\\nAn overview of common doctor failures and their remediation steps:\\n\\n* **`content_sanity_audit_recent` (Fails at >= 100 events):**\\n  * *Symptom:* High number of `PAGE_OVERSIZE_WARN` events written to the weekly audit logs (usually from large meeting transcripts).\\n  * *Remediation:*\\n    1. Raise the warn threshold to avoid future flags (e.g., `gbrain config set content_sanity.bytes_warn 200000`).\\n    2. Move or rotate the current week's audit log file on disk to clear the 7-day failure history (e.g., `mv ~/.gbrain/audit/content-sanity-*.jsonl ~/.gbrain/audit/content-sanity-*.jsonl.bak`).\\n\\n* **`frontmatter_integrity`:**\\n  * *Symptom:* Syntax errors detected on-disk.\\n  * *Remediation:* Run `gbrain frontmatter validate <source-path> --fix` first. For other issues:\\n    1. **Unkeyed List Items:** Floating list items at the top of a page's frontmatter (such as aliases) will cause YAML parsing failures. Group and nest them under the `aliases:` key.\\n    2. **Empty Frontmatter:** Remove entirely empty `--- \\\\n ---` blocks from notes (common in daily notes) to clear empty frontmatter warnings.\\n    3. **Nested Quotes:** Ensure outer YAML values are single-quoted if inner values are double-quoted.\\n\\n* **`cycle_freshness`:**\\n  * *Symptom:* Stale sources that have never completed a full cycle.\\n  * *Remediation:* Complete a dream cycle for each stale source manually (e.g., `gbrain dream --source <source-id>`).\\n\\n* **`orphan_ratio` & `graph_coverage`:**\\n  * *Symptom:* High percentage of pages with no inbound links.\\n  * *Remediation:* Run a database link extraction (`gbrain extract links --by-mention --source db` or `gbrain extract all --source db`) to automatically build and materialize thousands of missing connections from mentions in page bodies.\\n\\n* **Missing Key Warnings:**\\n  * *Symptom:* Recommendation to enable specific configurations.\\n  * *Remediation:* Run the config set commands (e.g., `gbrain config set link_resolution.global_basename true`). For non-standard configurations, apply the command with `--force` (e.g., `gbrain config set conversation_parser.llm_fallback_enabled true --force`).\\n\\n\\n\", \"path\": \"devops/gbrain/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/devops/gbrain\", \"linked_files\": null, \"usage_hint\": null, \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\"}",

        {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"output\": \"gbrain schema \u2014 active schema pack management\\n\\nInspection:\\n  active                  Show resolved pack + which tier provided it\\n  list                    List installed packs (bundled + ~/.gbrain/schema-packs/)\\n  show [<pack>]           Pretty-print a manifest (default: active pack)\\n  validate [<pack>]       Validate manifest shape against the v1 schema\\n  graph                   Show type/primitive graph with link-verb edges\\n  lint [<pack>]           Lint a pack for duplicates, dangling refs, etc.\\n  stats [--source <id>]   Per-type page counts + typed-coverage from the DB\\n  explain <type>          Print resolved settings for a single type\\n  usage [--since N(d|w|m)] CLI invocation telemetry summary\\n\\nActivation:\\n  use <pack>              Activate pack (writes ~/.gbrain/config.json schema_pack)\\n  downgrade [--to <pack>] Restore the previous active pack\\n  reload [--pack <name>]  Flush the in-process pack cache; --pack scopes\\n\\nAuthoring (v0.40.6.0):\\n  init <name>             Scaffold a new pack (extends gbrain-base)\\n  fork <src> <new>        Copy a pack to a new editable name\\n  edit <name>             Print the on-disk pack file path\\n  diff <a> <b>            Compare page_type sets across two packs\\n\\n  add-type <name> --primitive <p> --prefix <dir/>\\n                          [--extractable] [--expert] [--alias <a>]* [--pack <name>]\\n  remove-type <name>      [--pack <name>]\\n  update-type <name>      [--extractable BOOL] [--expert BOOL] [--primitive P] [--pack <name>]\\n  add-alias <type> <alias>      [--pack <name>]\\n  remove-alias <type> <alias>   [--pack <name>]\\n  add-prefix <type> <prefix>    [--pack <name>]\\n  remove-prefix <type> <prefix> [--pack <name>]\\n  add-link-type <name> [--inverse <verb>] [--page-type <t>] [--target-type <t>] [--pack <name>]\\n  remove-link-type <name>       [--pack <name>]\\n  set-extractable <type> <true|false>      [--pack <name>]\\n  set-expert-routing <type> <true|false>   [--pack <name>]\\n  scaffold-extractable <type> [--pack <name>] [--dims a,b,c] [--force]\\n                          v0.42: declare a pack-supplied prompt + fixtures\\n                          + eval dimensions for an LLM-backed extractor.\\n                          Generates prompts/extract/<type>.md and\\n                          fixtures/extract/<type>.jsonl stubs the\\n                          pack-author edits, then pairs with\\n                          `gbrain extract benchmark` for the iteration loop.\\n\\nDiscovery + repair:\\n  detect                  Cluster pages by source_path \u2192 candidate page_types\\n  suggest                 Heuristic refinement on detect output\\n  review-candidates       Review disk-derived candidates; promote with --apply\\n  review-orphans          List pages with no active-pack type match\\n  sync [--apply]          Backfill page.type for rows matching pack prefixes\\n                          (dry-run by default; chunked UPDATE on apply)\\n\\nAll new verbs accept --json. Verbs scoped by source accept --source <id>.\\nPass --force to bypass per-pack lock contention on writes.\\n\\nResolution chain (7-tier, tier 1 trust-gated):\\n  1. Per-call --schema-pack flag (CLI only)\\n  2. GBRAIN_SCHEMA_PACK env var\\n  3. Per-source DB config schema_pack.source.<id>\\n  4. Brain-wide DB config schema_pack\\n  5. gbrain.yml schema: section\\n  6. ~/.gbrain/config.json schema_pack\\n  7. Default: gbrain-base\", \"exit_code\": 0, \"error\": null}",

          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "The setup is complete.\n\n1. **Vault folder created:** `/home/justin.guest/vault/sources/slack/` is ready.\n2. **gbrain updated:** Added `sources/slack/` as an active prefix for the `slack` page type in your `gbrain-personal` schema. It will auto-classify any notes placed there.\n3. **Cron job scheduled:** **Slack Brain Note Capture** is active and will run every 2 hours. It uses `fetch_slack_brains.py` to check for new `\ud83e\udde0` reactions by you.\n\nWhen it finds a new reaction, it will:\n*   Write a structured conversation summary with participants, decisions, and verbatim context to `sources/slack/`.\n*   Append a clean link and gist to today's daily note under `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad`.\n*   Mark the message processed.\n*   Ping you on Telegram with the summary.\n\nIf there are no new reactions, it exits silently.",

        {

(In fact, we might want to make this an entire phase of the morning briefing, one that's not specific to Slack, but might start out with only Slack.) Whether the note gets added via \ud83e\udde0 or via morning briefing, the shape of the Slack note is the same (and it goes in the same folder).",

          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"morning-briefing\", \"description\": \"Interactive morning briefing for Justin \u2014 runs after the 7AM cron has done background work (work log, vault hygiene Tier 1, inbox gather). Walks through a multi-phase conversation: work log highlights, calendar summary, near-term task triage, calendar inbox candidates, general inbox candidates. Load this skill whenever Justin responds to the morning greeting or asks for his morning briefing.\", \"tags\": [], \"related_skills\": [\"work-log\", \"todoist-inbox-fill\", \"obsidian-vault-hygiene\", \"todoist\"], \"content\": \"---\\nname: morning-briefing\\ndescription: \\\"Interactive morning briefing for Justin \u2014 runs after the 7AM cron has done background work (work log, vault hygiene Tier 1, inbox gather). Walks through a multi-phase conversation: work log highlights, calendar summary, near-term task triage, calendar inbox candidates, general inbox candidates. Load this skill whenever Justin responds to the morning greeting or asks for his morning briefing.\\\"\\nplatforms: [linux]\\nrelated_skills: [work-log, todoist-inbox-fill, obsidian-vault-hygiene, todoist]\\n---\\n\\n# \ud83c\udf05 Morning Briefing\\n\\nThis skill governs the **interactive phase** of the morning briefing. The 7AM cron runs background jobs and sends a greeting; once Justin responds, load this skill and walk through the phases in order.\\n\\n## Key files\\n\\n- **Cache:** `~/.hermes/morning-briefing/YYYY-MM-DD.json` \u2014 written by cron, read here\\n- **Days off:** `~/.hermes/days-off.txt` \u2014 personal non-holiday days off\\n- **Work-day helper:** `python3.12 ~/.hermes/scripts/work_day.py <cmd> [date]`\\n- **Vault:** `/home/justin.guest/vault` (or `$OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH`)\\n\\n## Entry point\\n\\nThe cron sends: *\\\"Morning, Justin! Ready to start your day?\\\"*\\n\\nJustin's reply determines the flow:\\n- **\\\"yes\\\" / any affirmative** \u2192 full briefing (all phases)\\n- **\\\"day off\\\" / \\\"taking the day off\\\" / similar** \u2192 skip Phase 1; run Phases 2\u20135 with personal-only filter\\n- **no response within the session** \u2192 nothing; cron already ran background jobs\\n\\n## Cache file schema\\n\\nThe 7AM cron writes `~/.hermes/morning-briefing/YYYY-MM-DD.json` with this structure:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"date\\\": \\\"YYYY-MM-DD\\\",\\n  \\\"is_work_day\\\": true,\\n  \\\"work_log_dates\\\": [\\\"YYYY-MM-DD\\\"],\\n  \\\"work_log_status\\\": \\\"ok | error | skipped\\\",\\n  \\\"vault_hygiene\\\": {\\n    \\\"tier1_status\\\": \\\"ok | error\\\",\\n    \\\"tier1_summary\\\": \\\"Moved 2 daily notes. Fixed 1 blank category.\\\",\\n    \\\"tier2_issues\\\": [\\n      \\\"Notebook/Some Note.md \u2014 missing category (looks like essay)\\\",\\n      \\\"Notebook/Other Note.md \u2014 malformed id field\\\"\\n    ]\\n  },\\n  \\\"inbox_candidates\\\": {\\n    \\\"status\\\": \\\"ok | error | partial\\\",\\n    \\\"sources_failed\\\": [],\\n    \\\"calendar_events\\\": [...],\\n    \\\"action_items\\\": [...]\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nIf the cache file doesn't exist (cron failed or hasn't run yet), run the background jobs inline before proceeding. Tell Justin there'll be a brief pause.\\n\\n## Phase sequence\\n\\n### Phase 0 \u2014 Dream Cycle Verification (Brief check-in)\\n\\nBefore walking through the interactive phases, check the top-level `\\\"dream_cycle\\\"` field in the daily cache file (`~/.hermes/morning-briefing/YYYY-MM-DD.json`).\\n- **If status is \\\"ok\\\":** Open with a very brief, delightful one-sentence parenthesis about your overnight synthesis (e.g. *\\\"By the way, the overnight dream cycle successfully processed and embedded N chunks across M pages in X.Ys!\\\"*). This should be a tiny \\\"status update\\\" that shows things are working perfectly.\\n- **If status is \\\"error\\\" or \\\"missing\\\":** Do NOT start with this. Keep the opening positive and push the failure note to the bottom of the entire morning briefing, listing it under the vault hygiene section (e.g. *`Dream cycle failed: [error log]`*) so Justin can review it without disrupting his morning focus.\\n\\n### Phase 1 \u2014 Work log highlights\\n\\n**Skip entirely if:** `is_work_day` is false OR Justin said \\\"day off.\\\"\\n\\n**If `is_work_day` is true:**\\n\\n1. Load `work_log_dates` from cache (computed by cron using `work_day.py logs_to_summarize`)\\n2. For each date in `work_log_dates`, read the `# \ud83d\udccb Work Log` section from the daily note in vault\\n3. Synthesize highlights across all dates into a single brief summary\\n\\n**Format:**\\n```\\n\ud83d\udccb Work log \u2014 [Day, Mon DD] (or \\\"Fri\u2013Mon\\\" for multi-day spans)\\n\\n\u2022 [2\u20134 bullet highlights \u2014 most important things that happened]\\n\u2022 Decisions: [1\u20132 if any, otherwise omit]\\n\u2022 Still open: [1\u20132 blockers/open questions, if any]\\n```\\n\\nKeep it scannable. 4\u20136 bullets max. If multiple days, don't repeat obvious context \u2014 synthesize across them. Don't recite every meeting; surface the consequential ones.\\n\\nIf no work log entries exist for the dates (notes missing or no Work Log section), say so briefly and move on. Don't block on it.\\n\\n**If the user asks you to generate the missing daily note / work log:**\\n- Gather yesterday's raw materials from Slack, Linear, GWS, and Todoist (using `/home/justin.guest/bes-backup/skills/note-taking/work-log/references/direct_execution.py` or parallel subagents).\\n- Scan for any other notes written on that date in the vault (e.g. `Notebook/Kennywood Day...` or other files with matching timestamp/daily_note fields) to capture personal context.\\n- Synthesize them into a complete daily note and work log following the `work-log` skill and the `<vault>/Templates/Daily Note.md` template.\\n- Write the daily note to the vault archive (`Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD DayName.md`).\\n- Update the morning briefing cache file (`~/.hermes/morning-briefing/YYYY-MM-DD.json`) to set `work_log_status: \\\"ok\\\"`, remove the `work_log_error`, and clear `vault_hygiene` issues if resolved.\\n- Present the synthesized work log highlights and proceed to Phase 2.\\n\\nAfter presenting, **wait for acknowledgment or a question** before moving to Phase 2. Don't immediately dump everything at once.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2 \u2014 Calendar summary\\n\\nPull today's calendar and look ahead 7 days for anything major. Since the background cache file only stores *candidates* (events not yet on the calendar), you must fetch the actual scheduled events live.\\n\\n**Sources:** Fetch live from Google Workspace across all accounts (or personal accounts only on non-work days):\\n- Work and personal: `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py --account all calendar list`\\n- Personal only (weekends/days off): `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py --account personal-main,personal-junk calendar list`\\n\\n**Format:**\\n```\\n\ud83d\udcc5 Today \u2014 [Day, Mon DD]\\n\\n\u2022 HH:MM  Event name (attendees if external)\\n\u2022 HH:MM  Event name\\n[if nothing: \\\"Nothing on the calendar today.\\\"]\\n\\nComing up this week:\\n\u2022 [Day] Event name \u2014 [why it's notable: external attendees / prep needed / important]\\n[only surface 2\u20133 notable items, not every standup]\\n```\\n\\n**On non-work days (or \\\"day off\\\"):** show personal calendar events only. Skip work account events.\\n\\nAfter presenting, wait for acknowledgment before Phase 3.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3 \u2014 Near-term task triage\\n\\nQuery Todoist for tasks with due dates or deadlines in the next 3 days (inclusive of today).\\n\\n**Rule:** Skip any tasks that are already in the \\\"Now\\\" project, or completed/cancelled tasks.\\nOnly present this if there are matching tasks. If none, skip directly to the next phase.\\n\\n**Format:**\\n```\\n\ud83d\udccc N tasks due or with deadlines in the next 3 days:\\n\\n1. [Project] Task name (due: Date/Time | deadline: Date)\\n...\\n\\nWould you like to move any of these to \\\"Now\\\"?\\n```\\n\\nOnce Justin selects tasks, move them to the \\\"Now\\\" project.\\n\\nAfter presenting and handling any movements, wait for acknowledgment before Phase 4.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4 \u2014 Calendar inbox candidates\\n\\nPresent the calendar event candidates from `inbox_candidates.calendar_events`.\\n\\nSuggest a target calendar (`work` or `personal-main`) for each candidate based on its source and content (e.g., family/school/personal tasks default to `personal-main`, while SignLab/professional ones default to `work`).\\n\\nFormat:\\n```\\n\ud83d\udcc5 N potential calendar events not yet on your calendar:\\n\\n1. [Source] Event | when: date/time | calendar: work | context: where found\\n2. [Source] Event | when: date/time | calendar: personal-main | context: where found\\n...\\n\\nWhich ones should I add directly to your Google Calendar?\\n```\\n\\nIf `calendar_events` is empty, say \\\"Nothing new to add to your calendar.\\\" and skip to Phase 5.\\n\\nOnce Justin confirms selections:\\n- Schedule/create the events directly on the respective Google Calendar accounts (`work` or `personal-main`) using the calendar write capabilities (`gws_multi.py --account <name> calendar create --summary \\\"...\\\" --start \\\"...\\\" --end \\\"...\\\" --attendees \\\"...\\\" --description \\\"...\\\"`).\\n- Only create a Todoist \\\"Add to calendar:\\\" task as an exceptional fallback if Justin specifically requests it or if the event timing/date is too ambiguous to schedule directly.\\n- Report the scheduling confirmation with event details and links, then move to Phase 5.\\n\\n**On non-work days / day off:** still run this phase but filter to personal events only (skip work Slack, work email sources).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5 \u2014 Inbox candidates\\n\\nPresent the action-item candidates from `inbox_candidates.action_items`.\\n\\nUse the format from `todoist-inbox-fill` Step 4 Section B \u2014 semantically batched groups:\\n```\\n\ud83d\udce5 N potential inbox items:\\n\\n**[Group label]**\\n1. [Source] Task description\\n2. [Source] Task description\\n\\n**[Group label]**\\n3. ...\\n\\nWhich ones should I add?\\n```\\n\\nIf `action_items` is empty, say \\\"Inbox looks clear.\\\" \\n\\nOnce Justin confirms \u2192 batch-add to Todoist Inbox with comments.\\n\\n**On non-work days / day off:** filter out work-sourced tasks (Linear, work email, work Slack). Keep home/family/personal ones.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### After all phases\\n\\nIf vault hygiene Tier 2 issues exist in the cache, append a brief report at the end:\\n\\n```\\n\ud83d\uddc2 Vault hygiene \u2014 N items to look at when you have a moment:\\n\u2022 Notebook/Some Note.md \u2014 missing category (looks like an essay)\\n\u2022 ...\\n```\\n\\nDon't block on this or ask for immediate action. It's informational.\\n\\nThen close out: *\\\"That's everything. Have a good [day/weekend/Monday].\\\"* (Match the day.)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Work-day detection quick reference\\n\\n```bash\\n# Is today a work day?\\npython3.12 ~/.hermes/scripts/work_day.py is_work_day\\n\\n# Which dates' work logs to summarize today?\\npython3.12 ~/.hermes/scripts/work_day.py logs_to_summarize\\n\\n# Previous work day\\npython3.12 ~/.hermes/scripts/work_day.py prev_work_day\\n```\\n\\nUS federal holidays auto-detected. Personal days off in `~/.hermes/days-off.txt` (one YYYY-MM-DD per line, # comments ok).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Tone and pacing\\n\\n- One phase per message. Don't dump everything at once.\\n- Wait for acknowledgment between phases. Justin may want to ask a question or act on something before moving on.\\n- Keep each phase message short. The briefing should feel like a quick check-in, not a wall of text.\\n- Match energy to day: Monday morning gets a slightly warmer opener; Friday gets a brief \\\"anything to wrap up this week?\\\" at the end if there are open blockers.\\n- On weekends / days off: lighter, shorter, personal focus only.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Pitfalls\\n\\n- **Child name or grade misattribution.** Do not guess or assume which child a school event (like a graduation, potluck, or class celebration) belongs to. Always verify school grades and ages against the user profile (e.g., Jamie is in 5th grade/G5, Sam is in 6th grade/G6) before writing descriptions or adding summaries.\\n- **Decision misattribution.** When summarizing work logs or calendar updates, do not attribute decisions made by others (colleagues, family members, teachers) to Justin. Always explicitly credit the actual decision-maker.\\n- **Don't re-run background jobs if the cache is fresh.** Check the cache first. Only re-run if the file is missing or >4h old.\\n- **Work log section header is `# \ud83d\udccb Work Log`** in the daily note. If it's absent, the note may not have been logged yet \u2014 say so, don't silently skip.\\n- **Multiple work-log dates:** synthesize, don't concatenate. Justin doesn't want to read Friday's full log on Monday \u2014 he wants the 3-line version.\\n- **Calendar dedup is against the full 30-day snapshot** already in the cache. Don't re-fetch unless you need to.\\n- **\\\"Day off\\\" filter applies per-phase.** Check it before each phase, not just once at the top.\\n- **Vault hygiene is never blocking.** Always put it last, always frame it as \\\"when you have a moment.\\\"\\n\", \"path\": \"productivity/morning-briefing/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/morning-briefing\", \"linked_files\": null, \"usage_hint\": null, \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\"}",
          "timestamp": 1780691767.4760847,
          "message_id": null
        },
        {
          "role": "assistant",

        {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"todoist-inbox-fill\", \"description\": \"Use when Justin asks to 'fill my inbox', 'sync my tasks', 'what am I missing in Todoist', or wants open actions from external sources (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Obsidian daily notes, iMessages, Linear) surfaced into his Todoist Inbox \u2014 without duplicating what's already there. Also detects potential calendar events and surfaces them as Google Calendar event candidates to schedule directly on his behalf. Accepts an optional lookback window (default 48h).\", \"tags\": [], \"related_skills\": [], \"content\": \"---\\nname: todoist-inbox-fill\\ndescription: \\\"Use when Justin asks to 'fill my inbox', 'sync my tasks', 'what am I missing in Todoist', or wants open actions from external sources (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Obsidian daily notes, iMessages, Linear) surfaced into his Todoist Inbox \u2014 without duplicating what's already there. Also detects potential calendar events and surfaces them as Google Calendar event candidates to schedule directly on his behalf. Accepts an optional lookback window (default 48h).\\\"\\nplatforms: [linux, macos]\\n---\\n\\n# \ud83d\udce5 Todoist Inbox Fill\\n\\nScan external sources for open actions Justin owns and needs to do. Deduplicate against what already exists in Todoist. Surface candidates to Justin for confirm/edit. Batch-add confirmed tasks to Inbox.\\n\\n**This is not a sync.** It's a one-way capture pass. It does not complete, reschedule, or remove tasks. It only adds what's genuinely missing.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 1 \u2014 Pre-flight\\n\\nDetermine the lookback window. If Justin specified one (e.g. \\\"last 24 hours\\\", \\\"go back 3 days\\\"), use it. Otherwise default to **48 hours**.\\n\\nCompute dates and snapshot the current Todoist state. Do this in-context (not in a subagent).\\n\\n```bash\\nTODAY=$(date +%F)           # e.g. 2026-05-21\\nTOMORROW=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY + 1 day\\\" +%F)\\nTODAY_SLASH=$(date +%Y/%m/%d)\\nTOMORROW_SLASH=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY + 1 day\\\" +%Y/%m/%d)\\nWEEK_FROM_NOW=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY + 7 days\\\" +%F)\\n\\n# Lookback window (default 48h = 2 days)\\nLOOKBACK_HOURS=48           # override if Justin specified something else\\nLOOKBACK_START=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY - ${LOOKBACK_HOURS} hours\\\" +%F)\\nLOOKBACK_START_SLASH=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY - ${LOOKBACK_HOURS} hours\\\" +%Y/%m/%d)\\n```\\n\\nPass `LOOKBACK_HOURS`, `LOOKBACK_START`, `LOOKBACK_START_SLASH`, and `LOOKBACK_ISO` (ISO 8601 datetime for Linear, e.g. `2026-05-21T00:00:00.000Z`) to subagents so they scope their searches to the right window.\\n\\nCompute `LOOKBACK_ISO`:\\n```bash\\nLOOKBACK_ISO=$(date -u -d \\\"${LOOKBACK_HOURS} hours ago\\\" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.000Z)\\n```\\n\\nThen snapshot **all open tasks** in Todoist so you can deduplicate later. Call both:\\n1. `find-tasks-by-date(startDate=\\\"today\\\", daysCount=7, overdueOption=\\\"include-overdue\\\", limit=100)`\\n2. `find-tasks(filter=\\\"no date\\\", limit=100)`\\n\\nMerge results and keep them in-context. You'll diff against this snapshot in Step 3. This two-call method is robust and completely avoids HTTP 400 errors caused by complex Todoist search query syntax.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 2 \u2014 Gather candidates (parallel subagents)\\n\\nSpawn one subagent per source in a **single batch**. Each subagent returns a compact bullet list of *open actions* \u2014 things Justin needs to do. Not FYIs. Not things others owe him (unless he needs to follow up). Not decisions already made.\\n\\nPass `TODAY`, `TOMORROW`, `TODAY_SLASH`, `TOMORROW_SLASH`, `WEEK_FROM_NOW`, `LOOKBACK_HOURS`, `LOOKBACK_START`, and `LOOKBACK_START_SLASH` into each subagent as verbatim substituted strings.\\n\\n**Budget for every subagent: \u22648 tool calls. Return partial results and stop if budget is exhausted.**\\n\\nSources to cover in three batches (concurrency cap is 3):\\n- **Batch 1:** Slack, Gmail, Obsidian daily notes\\n- **Batch 2:** Calendar, Linear, iMessages\\n- **Batch 3:** Granola meeting notes\\n\\nSources: **Slack, Gmail, Obsidian daily notes, Calendar, Linear, iMessages, Granola.**\\n\\n**Do not pipe command output into a language interpreter** (no `... | python3 -c \\\"...\\\"`, no `... | bash`, no `... | node -e \\\"...\\\"`). The security scanner flags `cmd | python3` etc. as `pipe_to_interpreter` (HIGH) regardless of intent and will halt your run for approval. If you need to post-process JSON, use `jq` (installed). If you need real Python, write a short script to a tempfile and run it as `python3 /tmp/foo.py` \u2014 the file boundary is what satisfies the scanner.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent A \u2014 Slack (SignLab)\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `slack`\\n- **Goal:** Find open actions for Justin in Slack today. Budget: 8 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Extract open actions from Slack for Justin. **Only surface messages where Justin has set a Slack reminder** \u2014 these are the clearest signal of intent to act.\\n  >\\n  > Run one search:\\n  > 1. `python3 ${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/social-media/slack/scripts/slack.py search 'has:reminder after:<LOOKBACK_START>' --limit 50`\\n  >\\n  > `has:reminder` returns messages Justin flagged with Slack's native reminder feature. This is intentionally narrow \u2014 Justin sets reminders on things he means to act on, so false positives are rare.\\n  >\\n  > Note: the word \\\"reminder\\\" appearing in message *text* (e.g. \\\"Reminder: meeting at 3pm\\\") can occasionally leak in. Drop these if they're clearly not reminder-flagged \u2014 use context and channel to judge.\\n  >\\n  > Drop: bot/automation messages, notifications, things that are clearly already done.\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Do NOT pipe `slack.py` output into a language interpreter (`| python3 -c`, `| bash`, `| node -e`). The security scanner blocks these as `pipe_to_interpreter` (HIGH) and your run will halt for approval. If you need to inspect or reshape the JSON, use `jq`. Example:\\n  > ```bash\\n  > python3 ${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/social-media/slack/scripts/slack.py search 'has:reminder after:<LOOKBACK_START>' --limit 50 \\\\\\\\\\n  >   | jq -r '.[] | \\\"\\\\\\\\(.channel_name) | \\\\\\\\(.username) | \\\\\\\\(.permalink) | \\\\\\\\(.text[:200])\\\"'\\n  > ```\\n  > For non-trivial Python, write to a tempfile and run as `python3 /tmp/foo.py` \u2014 the file boundary is what satisfies the scanner.\\n  >\\n  > Format each as a candidate task:\\n  > `- [Slack] <concise action> | context: <#channel or @person, brief what/why> | url: <permalink>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 8 tool calls. Return what you have and stop if budget exhausted.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent B \u2014 Gmail (work + personal-main)\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `google-workspace`\\n- **Goal:** Find emails where Justin owes a reply or has an explicit action item. Budget: 8 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Use the wrapper at `${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py`. Read-only \u2014 do NOT attempt sends.\\n  >\\n  > Run exactly two searches (no personal-junk unless Justin explicitly asked):\\n  > 1. `gws_multi.py --account work gmail search 'after:<LOOKBACK_START_SLASH> before:<TOMORROW_SLASH> in:inbox -label:sent' --max 50`\\n  > 2. `gws_multi.py --account personal-main gmail search 'after:<LOOKBACK_START_SLASH> before:<TOMORROW_SLASH> in:inbox -label:sent' --max 50`\\n  >\\n  > The `in:inbox` filter is critical \u2014 it excludes archived threads. **Do not surface tasks from archived emails.** If a thread has been archived, Justin has already processed it and it is not an open action.\\n  >\\n  > From results (subject, from, snippet only \u2014 do NOT fetch full bodies), extract:\\n  > - Emails from humans that contain a direct question or request for Justin.\\n  > - Emails Justin sent that contain a commitment or follow-up he still needs to do.\\n  > - Threads where he's last-replied-to but hasn't responded.\\n  >\\n  > For each result, include the message ID or thread ID if available in the search output (needed to construct a Gmail link). Gmail deep links follow the pattern `https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#Inbox/<threadId>` \u2014 include this if you can derive it.\\n  >\\n  > Skip: automated notifications, CI/build alerts, shipping/delivery, marketing, newsletters, receipts, calendar invites, App Store Connect issue alerts, Todoist onboarding, Readwise, Substack.\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Do NOT pipe `gws_multi.py` output into a language interpreter (`| python3 -c`, `| bash`, `| node -e`). The scanner blocks these. Use `jq` for JSON, or write a Python helper to `/tmp/foo.py` and run as `python3 /tmp/foo.py`.\\n  >\\n  > Format each candidate task:\\n  > `- [Email/<account>] <concise action> | from: <sender> | subject: <subject>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates (work: X, personal-main: Y)`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 8 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent G \u2014 Granola meeting notes (Next Steps for Justin)\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\", \\\"file\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `obsidian`\\n- **Goal:** Find Next Steps assigned to Justin in recent Granola meeting notes. Budget: 8 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Vault path: resolve from env `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` (fallback: `~/Documents/Obsidian Vault`). Granola notes live under `<vault>/Granola/`, organized by month folders (`YYYY-MM/`). Each note filename starts with the meeting date (`YYYY-MM-DD`).\\n  >\\n  > Identify which month folders to search based on the lookback window (`<LOOKBACK_START>` to `<TODAY>`). Typically just the current month, but if the lookback crosses a month boundary, include both.\\n  >\\n  > For each `.md` file in the relevant folder(s) whose date prefix falls within the lookback window:\\n  > 1. Read the file.\\n  > 2. Find the `### Next Steps` section.\\n  > 3. Extract only lines that begin with `- Justin:` (case-insensitive). These are the action items assigned to Justin.\\n  > 4. Skip all other lines (other people's action items, bullets under other sections).\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Use `search_files` and `read_file` from the file toolset \u2014 do NOT use `grep` piped to `python3 -c` or similar (scanner blocks `pipe_to_interpreter`). Use `jq` if reshaping JSON.\\n  >\\n  > Format each candidate task:\\n  > `- [Granola/<YYYY-MM-DD>] <action text (strip the \\\"Justin: \\\" prefix)> | meeting: <note title> | file: <relative path>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates across M meeting notes`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 8 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent C \u2014 Obsidian daily notes (lookback window)\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\", \\\"file\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `obsidian`\\n- **Goal:** Find uncaptured open actions in recent daily notes. Budget: 8 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Read `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` from env (fallback: `~/Documents/Obsidian Vault`). Daily note filename format: `YYYY-MM-DD DayName.md`. Current notes live in the vault root; older ones in `Daily Notes/`.\\n  >\\n  > For each of the last N days matching the lookback window (starting today, <TODAY>, going back to <LOOKBACK_START>), find and read the daily note if it exists.\\n  >\\n  > From each note, extract **only**:\\n  > - Unchecked task items: lines matching `- [ ]` that are not marked done.\\n  > - Lines containing explicit first-person commitment phrases: \\\"I need to\\\", \\\"I need to remember\\\", \\\"I have to\\\", \\\"I should\\\", \\\"I must\\\", \\\"don't forget\\\", \\\"follow up on\\\", \\\"remind me\\\".\\n  >\\n  > Do NOT infer tasks from section headings, bullet points under \\\"Open Questions\\\" or \\\"Blockers\\\", or general observations. Only literal `- [ ]` items and lines with the above explicit keywords qualify.\\n  >\\n  > Skip: `- [x]` lines, headings, pure notes, observations, decisions, highlights.\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Use the `file` toolset and `search_files` to read notes \u2014 do NOT pipe shell output into `python3 -c` / `bash` / `node -e` (scanner blocks `pipe_to_interpreter`).\\n  >\\n  > Format each candidate task:\\n  > `- [Obsidian/<date>] <action text> | note: <YYYY-MM-DD DayName.md>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates across M notes`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 8 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent \u2014 Linear\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `linear`\\n- **Goal:** Find Linear issues assigned to Justin (To Do / In Progress) and new Triage issues from within the lookback window. Budget: 8 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Use `curl` against `https://api.linear.app/graphql` with `Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY`.\\n  >\\n  > Run **two queries**:\\n  >\\n  > **Query 1 \u2014 Assigned issues in To Do, In Progress, or Monitoring:**\\n  > ```graphql\\n  > { viewer { assignedIssues(filter: {\\n  >     or: [\\n  >       { state: { type: { in: [\\\"unstarted\\\", \\\"started\\\"] } } },\\n  >       { state: { name: { eq: \\\"Monitoring\\\" } } }\\n  >     ]\\n  >   }, first: 50) {\\n  >     nodes { identifier title state { name type } url }\\n  >   } } }\\n  > ```\\n  > `unstarted` = To Do, `started` = In Progress, `Monitoring` = custom state (matched by name).\\n  >\\n  > **Query 2 \u2014 New Triage issues created within the lookback window (any assignee):**\\n  > ```graphql\\n  > { issues(filter: {\\n  >     and: [\\n  >       { state: { type: { eq: \\\"triage\\\" } } },\\n  >       { createdAt: { gt: \\\"<LOOKBACK_ISO>\\\" } }\\n  >     ]\\n  >   }, first: 50) {\\n  >     nodes { identifier title state { name type } url createdAt }\\n  >   } }\\n  > ```\\n  > (Replace `<LOOKBACK_ISO>` with the computed ISO timestamp, e.g. `2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z`.)\\n  >\\n  > **Task naming rules (these exact formats will become Todoist task content):**\\n  > - Assigned To Do/In Progress: `Work on <identifier> <title>`\\n  > - Assigned Monitoring: `Check in on <identifier> <title>`\\n  > - Triage: `Triage <identifier> <title>`\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Parse the curl JSON response with `jq`, never with `| python3 -c` or `| node -e` (scanner blocks `pipe_to_interpreter`). Example:\\n  > ```bash\\n  > curl -s -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\\\\\n  >   -d '{\\\"query\\\":\\\"...\\\"}' https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\\\\\n  >   | jq -r '.data.viewer.assignedIssues.nodes[] | \\\"\\\\\\\\(.identifier) | \\\\\\\\(.title) | \\\\\\\\(.state.name) | \\\\\\\\(.url)\\\"'\\n  > ```\\n  >\\n  > Format each result:\\n  > - For assigned: `- [Linear/assigned] Work on <identifier> <title> | url: <url>`\\n  > - For triage: `- [Linear/triage] Triage <identifier> <title> | url: <url>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates (assigned: X, triage: Y)`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 8 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent E \u2014 iMessages (family)\\n- **Goal:** Find open actions from family texts in the lookback window. Budget: 5 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Read recent messages across the allowlisted iMessage chats via SSH proxy:\\n  > ```bash\\n  > ssh mac-host bes-imsg recent-all --since <LOOKBACK_HOURS>h\\n  > ```\\n  >\\n  > From results, extract messages where someone is:\\n  > - Making an explicit request of Justin (\\\"Can you pick up\u2026\\\", \\\"Don't forget\u2026\\\", \\\"Can we\u2026\\\")\\n  > - Asking a direct question that Justin hasn't answered (you only see incoming messages, not Justin's replies \u2014 flag these as potential outstanding replies)\\n  > - Communicating a time-sensitive need (appointments, pickups, deadlines)\\n  >\\n  > Use friendly names for senders (Nana, Sam, Jamie, Rosie, Kathy, etc.) \u2014 not phone numbers.\\n  >\\n  > Skip: pure reactions (\\\"YAYYY\\\", \\\"I did\\\", \\\"Kk\\\"), FYIs with no ask, group chatter with no clear request directed at Justin.\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** If `bes-imsg recent-all` returns JSON, parse it with `jq`, not `| python3 -c` (scanner blocks `pipe_to_interpreter`).\\n  >\\n  > Format each candidate task:\\n  > `- [iMessage/<chat label>] <concise action> | from: <sender> | context: <brief quote or summary>`\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N candidates`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 5 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Subagent D \u2014 Calendar (upcoming meetings needing prep)\\n\\n- **Toolsets:** `[\\\"terminal\\\"]`\\n- **Skill:** `google-workspace`\\n- **Goal:** Identify upcoming meetings in the next 7 days where Justin might need a prep task. Budget: 5 tool calls.\\n- **Context:**\\n  > Run one command:\\n  > ```\\n  > gws_multi.py --account all calendar list --start <TODAY>T00:00:00 --end <WEEK_FROM_NOW>T23:59:59 --max 50\\n  > ```\\n  >\\n  > From the results, surface only meetings that:\\n  > - Have external attendees (not just Justin + internal SignLab team) OR are high-stakes internal meetings (reviews, all-hands, 1:1s with leadership).\\n  > - Are in the next 3 days (anything beyond that is low urgency right now).\\n  > - Don't obviously already have a prep task implied by the title.\\n  >\\n  > **Command safety:** Use `jq` to filter/reshape `gws_multi.py` JSON output. Do not pipe to `python3 -c` / `bash` / `node -e`.\\n  >\\n  > For each, suggest a prep action:\\n  > `- [Calendar] Prep for: <meeting title> | when: <date HH:MM> | attendees: <names>`\\n  >\\n  > Keep it minimal \u2014 only surface meetings where prep is clearly warranted, not every 30-min standup.\\n  >\\n  > End with `Total: N prep candidates`.\\n  >\\n  > Budget: 5 tool calls. Return what you have and stop.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 2b \u2014 Calendar event detection (separate pass)\\n\\nThis pass scans the same sources for *potential calendar events* \u2014 future commitments that aren't already on Justin's Google Calendar. Results are presented separately from general Todoist inbox candidates (see Step 4).\\n\\n**What counts as a potential calendar event (cast wide):**\\n- Any specific future date + time mentioned in context (meetings, calls, appointments, deadlines with a time component)\\n- Phrases like \\\"let's sync Thursday\\\", \\\"can we meet next week\\\", \\\"I'll call you at 3\\\", \\\"dinner Friday\\\", \\\"flight on the 15th\\\"\\n- iMessage/Slack scheduling exchanges (even informal ones)\\n- Obsidian notes with a date reference that looks like a future commitment\\n- Email invites that didn't come with a calendar invite attachment\\n- When in doubt, surface it \u2014 Justin will decide what belongs on his calendar\\n\\n**What to skip:**\\n- Events already on the calendar (checked below)\\n- Pure deadlines with no time component (those become regular Todoist tasks)\\n- Recurring standing meetings already on the calendar\\n- Past dates\\n\\n### Pre-flight: snapshot calendar for dedup\\n\\nBefore spawning calendar-detection subagents, pull Justin's calendar for the next 30 days across all 3 accounts:\\n\\n```bash\\ngws_multi.py --account all calendar list \\\\\\n  --start <TODAY>T00:00:00 \\\\\\n  --end <30_DAYS_OUT>T23:59:59 \\\\\\n  --max 100\\n```\\n\\nKeep this in-context as `CALENDAR_SNAPSHOT`. You'll use it to dedup candidates.\\n\\nCompute `30_DAYS_OUT`:\\n```bash\\nTHIRTY_DAYS_OUT=$(date -d \\\"$TODAY + 30 days\\\" +%F)\\n```\\n\\n### Calendar-detection subagents\\n\\nRun alongside Batch 1/2/3 of the regular inbox gather \u2014 they share the same sources but look for different signals. The same subagents can return both action-item candidates AND calendar-event candidates in a single run; just ask them to produce two labeled sections in their output:\\n\\n**Section 1: `## Action items`** \u2014 regular Todoist tasks (as before)\\n**Section 2: `## Potential calendar events`** \u2014 future commitments spotted while scanning\\n\\nUpdate each existing subagent's context to include:\\n\\n> Also scan for **potential calendar events**: any specific future date + time mentioned, scheduling language (\\\"let's sync\\\", \\\"meet me at\\\", \\\"dinner\\\", \\\"call\\\", \\\"flight\\\", \\\"appointment\\\"), or implied commitments with a time component. Cast wide \u2014 surface anything that looks like it might belong on a calendar, even informal scheduling. List these under a separate `## Potential calendar events` section.\\n>\\n> Format each calendar candidate:\\n> `- [Source] <concise event description> | when: <date/time as mentioned> | context: <brief where/with whom>`\\n\\n### Calendar dedup\\n\\nAfter subagents return, filter the calendar candidates against `CALENDAR_SNAPSHOT`:\\n- If an event with the same date, time, and rough description already exists \u2192 mark EXISTS, drop it\\n- If something similar exists but details differ \u2192 mark SIMILAR, keep with a note\\n- Everything else \u2192 NEW\\n\\n### Calendar candidates output format\\n\\nPresent calendar candidates in a dedicated section (Step 4 output), separate from regular inbox candidates. Suggest a target calendar account (`work` or `personal-main`) for each candidate based on context.\\n\\n```\\n\ud83d\udcc5 Found N potential calendar events not yet on your calendar:\\n\\n1. [Slack] Sync with Maya re: sprint planning | when: Thursday 2pm | calendar: work | context: #product-eng\\n2. [Email/work] Call with Alex | when: next Monday 10am | calendar: work | context: email from Alex Chen\\n3. [iMessage/Nana] Dentist appointment (Jamie) | when: June 3rd | calendar: personal-main | context: Nana's text\\n```\\n\\nWhen Justin confirms, schedule/create the events directly on the respective Google Calendar accounts (`work` or `personal-main`) using the calendar write capabilities:\\n`gws_multi.py --account <name> calendar create --summary \\\"<Summary>\\\" --start \\\"<Start>\\\" --end \\\"<End>\\\" --location \\\"<Location>\\\" --description \\\"<Description>\\\"`\\n\\nOnly create a Todoist \\\"Add to calendar:\\\" task as an exceptional fallback if Justin specifically requests it or if the event timing/date is too ambiguous to schedule directly.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 3 \u2014 Deduplicate\\n\\nYou now have subagent summaries (3\u20135 source lists) plus the Todoist snapshot from Step 1.\\n\\nFor each candidate task:\\n1. Check the Todoist snapshot for a semantically similar task. Similar = same person/issue/thread/subject matter, close enough that adding a second task would be redundant.\\n2. For Linear issues, check if an existing Todoist task's title, description, or content links/references the same Linear issue identifier (e.g., `PROD-533` or `CLAW-31`). If a match is found, mark it **EXISTS** and filter it out. Do not suggest a new task for any Linear issue already tracked in Todoist in any form.\\n3. Mark it as **NEW** (not in Todoist) or **EXISTS** (already covered). If it's close but not exact, mark it **SIMILAR** and note what the existing task is.\\n\\nThis dedup is LLM reasoning \u2014 you're comparing intent and subject matter, not text equality. Be conservative: if in doubt, mark it NEW and let Justin decide.\\n\\nBuild the final candidate list: only NEW and SIMILAR items. Drop EXISTS.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 4 \u2014 Present to Justin\\n\\nShow two sections. Within the inbox section, group candidates by semantic theme (same project, same person, same context). Don't number across groups \u2014 number within each group.\\n\\n**When called from morning briefing:** present calendar candidates first (Phase 3 of briefing), then inbox candidates (Phase 4). The briefing skill controls the turn order; this skill just provides the formatted output for each section.\\n\\n**When called standalone:** present both sections in one message.\\n\\n### Section A \u2014 Calendar events\\n\\n```\\n\ud83d\udcc5 N potential calendar events not yet on your calendar:\\n\\n1. [Slack] Sync with Maya re: sprint planning | when: Thu 2pm | calendar: work | context: #product-eng\\n2. [iMessage/Nana] Jamie's dentist | when: June 3rd | calendar: personal-main | context: Nana's text Tue\\n3. [Email/work] Call with Alex | when: next Mon 10am | calendar: work | context: email from Alex Chen\\n\\nWhich ones should I add directly to your Google Calendar? (say \\\"all\\\", \\\"1 3\\\", or \\\"skip 2\\\")\\n```\\n\\n### Section B \u2014 Inbox tasks, batched by theme\\n\\nGroup semantically related items under a bolded label. Number items continuously across groups.\\n\\n```\\n\ud83d\udce5 N potential inbox items not yet in Todoist:\\n\\n**Replies owed**\\n1. [Slack] Reply to @maya re: sprint retro format \u2014 #product-eng\\n2. [Email/work] Reply to Alex about Q3 roadmap review\\n\\n**Linear**\\n3. [Linear/assigned] Work on SL-204 Update onboarding copy\\n4. [Linear/triage] Triage SL-312 Crash on lesson complete\\n\\n**Home / family**\\n5. [Obsidian/2026-05-20] Follow up on Nana's dentist appointment\\n\\n~SIMILAR~ (already in Todoist but different angle):\\n6. [Email/work] Alex re: API rate limit spike \u2014 similar to existing \\\"Investigate API latency\\\" task\\n\\nWhich ones should I add? (say \\\"all\\\", \\\"1 3 5\\\", or \\\"skip 4\\\" \u2014 or edit any item before I add it)\\n```\\n\\nThen wait. Do not add anything yet.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 5 \u2014 Add confirmed tasks\\n\\nOnce Justin responds:\\n\\n- Parse his selection (\\\\\\\"all\\\\\\\", specific numbers, exclusions like \\\\\\\"skip 4\\\\\\\").\\n- For each confirmed task, shape it as a Todoist task:\\n  - **content**: concise action (strip the source prefix, keep it clean)\\n  - **description**: everything Justin needs to start the task cold \u2014 where details live, direct URL or permalink, relevant phone numbers or contact info, sender/assignee, deadline if known, Obsidian project link if applicable. One line per piece of context. If the source has a URL, always include it. The bar: can Justin open this task and immediately know where to go?\\n  - **projectId**: omit (\u2192 Inbox)\\n  - **priority**: `\\\"p4\\\"` unless the source clearly signals urgency\\n  - **labels**: infer from content (`@location` labels if applicable \u2014 do NOT use `work` or `home` as labels; those are Todoist projects, not labels)\\n  - **dueString**: only set if the source has a clear deadline or Justin requests one; otherwise leave unset\\n\\n- Batch-add with a single `add-tasks` call (up to 25 items).\\n- For each created task, immediately add a brief comment via `add-comments` explaining the source and why it was captured. Keep it to one short sentence. Examples:\\n  - \\\"From Slack \u2014 reminder set on @maya's message re: sprint retro in #product-eng.\\\"\\n  - \\\"Email from Alex Chen (work) \u2014 direct ask about Q3 roadmap, still in inbox.\\\"\\n  - \\\"From daily note 2026-05-20 \u2014 unchecked action item under Open Questions.\\\"\\n  - \\\"Calendar: Board review Thu 2pm \u2014 external attendees, prep warranted.\\\"\\n  Use a single `add-comments` call with all comments in one batch (one per task).\\n- Reply with a confirmation: \\\\\\\"Added N tasks to Inbox.\\\\\\\" and list them with their Todoist task IDs.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Important behaviors\\n\\n- **Never add without confirmation.** The present-then-confirm loop (Steps 4\u20135) is mandatory. Don't silently add.\\n- **No FYIs or informational notes.** If it's not something Justin needs to do, it doesn't belong in Todoist.\\n- **No hermes-labeled tasks.** Never add or modify the `hermes` label. Skip tasks labeled `hermes` in the Todoist snapshot \u2014 they're Hermes's.\\n- **Fail gracefully.** If a subagent fails, note it in the header (\\\"Slack: ERR \u2014 auth expired\\\") and continue with the other sources. A 3-source run is still useful.\\n- **Calendar prep tasks: be selective.** Justin generally does *not* want generic meeting prep tasks suggested. Skip standups, regular team syncs, internal 1:1s, leadership syncs, and short chat blocks. Only suggest prep tasks for high-stakes meetings with external clients/partners where explicit preparation is clearly warranted.\\n- **Date precision.** Pre-compute dates once (Step 1) and pass them to every subagent. Don't trust subagents to re-derive TODAY or LOOKBACK_START.\\n\\n## Pitfalls\\n\\n- **Granola notes are in `<vault>/Granola/YYYY-MM/` month subfolders.** Filenames start with `YYYY-MM-DD`. Only lines beginning `- Justin:` (case-insensitive) under the `### Next Steps` section are action items for Justin. Everything else is noise.\\n- **`delegate_task` concurrency cap is 3.** With 7 sources (Slack, Gmail, Obsidian, Calendar, Linear, iMessages, Granola), split into three batches: Batch 1 (Slack, Gmail, Obsidian), Batch 2 (Calendar, Linear, iMessages), Batch 3 (Granola). Don't try to pass more than 3 at once \u2014 it errors immediately.\\n- **Linear task naming is fixed.** Do not invent task titles for Linear items. Use exactly `Work on <ID> <name>` for assigned issues and `Triage <ID> <name>` for triage issues. Strip these from the source prefix when adding to Todoist \u2014 the content field should be the bare task name, not `[Linear/assigned] Work on\u2026`.\\n- **`find-tasks` requires at least one filter.** If you call it with no args it errors. See Step 1 for the right snapshot call.\\n- **Dedup is semantic, not textual.** \\\\\\\"Reply to Maya about retro\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"Respond to Maya re: retrospective\\\\\\\" are the same action. Don't add both.\\n- **Priorities are strings (`\\\"p1\\\"`\u2013`\\\"p4\\\"`), not integers.** Default to `\\\"p4\\\"`.\\n- **Don't use `update-tasks` to change due dates on recurring tasks** \u2014 use `reschedule-tasks`. This skill mostly adds new tasks, so this pitfall is rare here.\\n- **Gmail date format is `YYYY/MM/DD` (slashes), not dashes.** Calendar and Linear use ISO dashes. Keep them separate.\\n- **`gws_multi.py` path varies.** Always resolve as `${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py`.\\n- **Slack `--limit` flag.** The Slack CLI may not support `--limit` on all commands. If it errors, drop the flag and let it return the default count.\\n- **App Store Connect emails.** These are engineering issues, not Justin's. Filter them out at the Gmail subagent stage.\\n- **Never call the Todoist REST API directly (`api.todoist.com`) in custom scripts or subagents.** The sync v9 and REST v2 endpoints are deprecated (HTTP 410 / 404). Always use the native `mcp_todoist_*` tools. If a script inside `execute_code` or a background task absolutely must make raw HTTP requests, use the correct v1 path `https://api.todoist.com/api/v1/` (e.g. `/api/v1/tasks` or `/api/v1/sync` as a POST request) and include the `TODOIST_API_KEY` header.\\n\", \"path\": \"productivity/todoist-inbox-fill/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/todoist-inbox-fill\", \"linked_files\": null, \"usage_hint\": null, \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\"}",

          "content": "{\"status\": \"success\", \"output\": \"Thread 1 Messages:\\n  U0B21KU2J7Q: *Daily DAU \u2014 Thursday, 2026-06-04 \u2014 cc: <@U095LHMC4UW>*\\n\\n*PostHog SDK only.*\\n\\n*Overall: 25,858 DAU* (:arrow_lower_right: -3.6% vs previous 7d average)\\n\\n*By app:*\\n```\\nApp             | DAUs     | Trend | % Change (7d Avg) \\n----------------------------------------------------\\nASL Bloom       | 20,707   |  \u25bc    | -2.5%             \\nBright BSL      | 1,009    |  \u25bc    | -6.3%             \\nLibrasLab       | 976      |  \u25ac    | -2.1%             \\nyoDGS           | 936      |  \u25bc    | -11.7%            \\nAuslan Wiz      | 927      |  \u25bc    | -6.3%             \\nPause LSF       | 587      |  \u25bc    | -5.7%             \\nToleio          | 359      |  \u25bc    | -7.2%             \\nMeLISegno       | 357      |  \u25bc    | -16.6%            \\nPSL Isharay     | 0        |  NEW  | new               \\n```\\n\\n_ASL Bloom includes DAU from older Android builds with `product_name` unset._\\n_Note: Trend symbols (\u25b2/\u25bc) use volatility-scaled noise filtering. Small fluctuations are treated as neutral (\u25ac)._\\n\\n---\\n\\n*Anomaly Check*\\n2 anomalies detected\\n\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Bright BSL - New User Onboarding` (ID: 49) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (787 vs 14-day avg: 1081.4, Z-score: -3.14)\\n\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Auslan Wiz - New User Onboarding` (ID: 51) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (520 vs 14-day avg: 675.6, Z-score: -2.86)\\n  U095LHMC4UW: <@U0B21KU2J7Q> Does this data still include DAUs from older Android builds? Or have we moved passed that window?\\n  U0B21KU2J7Q: :hourglass_flowing_sand: Still working... (3 min elapsed \u2014 iteration 22/90, waiting for provider response (streaming))\\n  U0B21KU2J7Q: No, *we have not moved past that window.* The data still heavily relies on older, legacy Android builds where `properties.product_name` is null. This legacy cohort represents a massive, highly stable portion of our daily active users.\\n\\nHere is the direct evidence from the live PostHog SDK database (Project 118126) for yesterday, *Thursday, 2026-06-04*:\\n\\n*Yesterday's Snapshot (2026-06-04)*\\n* *Overall Impact:* Out of *25,858* total DAUs, *5,717 (22.1%)* had `product_name` unset. \\n* *ASL Bloom Sizing:* For ASL Bloom (reported at *20,707* DAU yesterday), *5,717 (27.6%)* of those users actually had a null `product_name` and were coalesced into the ASL Bloom metrics by the reporting script.\\n* *The Platform Split:*\\n  * *Android:* *5,552* out of *6,675* Android DAUs had `product_name` unset\u2014meaning *83.2% of our active Android users* are still running these older builds.\\n  * *iOS / iPadOS:* Only *163* active users had `product_name` unset, which is less than 1% of iOS traffic.\\n\\n---\\n\\n*The 30-Day Trend: A Stable Plateau*\\nLooking at the trend over the last month, we appear to have hit a hard plateau rather than a gradual phase-out:\\n\\n* *In early May,* the legacy cohort represented roughly 35% of overall DAUs.\\n* *By late May,* that share settled to around *22.1%*.\\n* *However, the absolute volume has remained completely flat.* For the past three weeks, the number of active users on these older builds (almost entirely *Android version 1.36.38*, which predates the super-property implementation as noted in `tracking_schema.yaml L2772`) has hovered consistently between *5,300 and 5,600 DAUs* every single day. \\n\\nThis indicates that this cohort represents highly retained, highly active users who are opening the app daily but are simply not upgrading their apps. If we were to remove the coalesce fallback today, our reported ASL Bloom DAU would appear to immediately drop by more than a quarter.\\n\\n---\\n\\n*A Minor Allocation Caveat*\\nA tiny sliver of these nulls (typically 10 to 25 DAUs; exactly *11* yesterday) are actually legacy *PSL Isharay* users (`product_id: 'signlab-pakistan'`). Because they also send a null `product_name`, the daily report's coalesce script currently groups them under ASL Bloom, though the volume is small enough to not skew the overall trends.\\n\\nThread 2 Messages:\\n  U095LHMC4UW: Interesting tips, many of them based on the Compound Engineering plugin, which seems a little more hands-on than Superpowers (with all the pros and cons that entails).\\n<https://x.com/mvanhorn/status/2061877533885473181>\\n  U7A6V8E9Z: Really good article <@U095LHMC4UW>. Not often I read articles like this end to end. I tried CE back in January, but will give it another go. Lots of new small tweaks and ideas to try to get the agents to do more of the work. \\n  U095LHMC4UW: <@U7DJZAQ3G> it occurred to me that CE might be a better fit for you than something like Superpowers (on the theory that you need and want less handholding than Superpowers gives you) - maybe worth trying?\\n  U095LHMC4UW: <@U02SSCPM1MH> The point about HyperFrames for video may be helpful\\n  U095LHMC4UW: For my part, Ill be checking out some of the agent memory stuff today, time permitting\\n\", \"tool_calls_made\": 0, \"duration_seconds\": 0.44}",

          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"diff\": \"--- a//home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py\\n+++ b//home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py\\n@@ -108,6 +108,127 @@\\n         return ch.get(\\\"name\\\") or channel_id\\n     except Exception:\\n         return channel_id\\n+\\n+def get_parent_ts(client, channel_id, ts):\\n+    try:\\n+        res = client.conversations_history(channel=channel_id, latest=ts, limit=1, inclusive=True)\\n+        msgs = res.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n+        if msgs:\\n+            m = msgs[0]\\n+            if m.get(\\\"thread_ts\\\"):\\n+                return m.get(\\\"thread_ts\\\")\\n+            return ts\\n+    except Exception:\\n+        pass\\n+    return ts\\n+\\n+def fetch_note_candidates(client):\\n+    import datetime as dt\\n+    user_id = client.auth_test()[\\\"user_id\\\"]\\n+    processed = load_processed()\\n+    processed_set = {f\\\"{p['channel']}:{p['ts']}\\\" for p in processed}\\n+    \\n+    # Check last 36 hours for candidate conversations\\n+    start_date = (dt.date.today() - dt.timedelta(days=2)).strftime(\\\"%Y-%m-%d\\\")\\n+    \\n+    # 1. Search messages from Justin\\n+    try:\\n+        res_from = client.search_messages(query=f\\\"from:me after:{start_date}\\\", count=30)\\n+        matches_from = res_from.get(\\\"messages\\\", {}).get(\\\"matches\\\", [])\\n+    except Exception:\\n+        matches_from = []\\n+        \\n+    # 2. Search mentions of Justin\\n+    try:\\n+        res_ment = client.search_messages(query=f\\\"<@{user_id}> after:{start_date}\\\", count=30)\\n+        matches_ment = res_ment.get(\\\"messages\\\", {}).get(\\\"matches\\\", [])\\n+    except Exception:\\n+        matches_ment = []\\n+        \\n+    all_matches = matches_from + matches_ment\\n+    \\n+    # Find unique (channel_id, parent_ts) threads\\n+    unique_conversations = set()\\n+    for m in all_matches:\\n+        channel_id = (m.get(\\\"channel\\\") or {}).get(\\\"id\\\")\\n+        ts = m.get(\\\"ts\\\")\\n+        if channel_id and ts:\\n+            unique_conversations.add((channel_id, ts))\\n+            \\n+    unique_threads = set()\\n+    for channel_id, ts in unique_conversations:\\n+        parent_ts = get_parent_ts(client, channel_id, ts)\\n+        unique_threads.add((channel_id, parent_ts))\\n+        \\n+    candidates = []\\n+    for channel_id, parent_ts in unique_threads:\\n+        key = f\\\"{channel_id}:{parent_ts}\\\"\\n+        if key in processed_set:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        try:\\n+            r = client.conversations_replies(channel=channel_id, ts=parent_ts, limit=100)\\n+            messages = r.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n+        except SlackApiError:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        if len(messages) < 3:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        # Verify Justin participated\\n+        participated = any(m.get(\\\"user\\\") == user_id for m in messages)\\n+        if not participated:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        # Verify distinct users\\n+        users = {m.get(\\\"user\\\") for m in messages if m.get(\\\"user\\\")}\\n+        if len(users) < 2:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        # Check if Justin added a 'brain' reaction to any message in the thread\\n+        has_brain = False\\n+        for m in messages:\\n+            for rxn in m.get(\\\"reactions\\\", []):\\n+                if rxn.get(\\\"name\\\") == \\\"brain\\\" and user_id in rxn.get(\\\"users\\\", []):\\n+                    has_brain = True\\n+                    break\\n+            if has_brain:\\n+                break\\n+                \\n+        if has_brain:\\n+            continue\\n+            \\n+        # Resolve all users in messages\\n+        resolve_users_in_messages(client, messages)\\n+        \\n+        # Clean messages\\n+        cleaned_messages = [clean_message(m, client) for m in messages]\\n+        \\n+        # Get permalink\\n+        permalink = None\\n+        try:\\n+            pl = client.chat_getPermalink(channel=channel_id, message_ts=parent_ts)\\n+            permalink = pl.get(\\\"permalink\\\")\\n+        except SlackApiError:\\n+            permalink = f\\\"https://signlab.slack.com/archives/{channel_id}/p{parent_ts.replace('.', '')}\\\"\\n+            \\n+        channel_name = get_channel_name(client, channel_id)\\n+        \\n+        participants_names = list(set(m[\\\"user_name\\\"] for m in cleaned_messages if m.get(\\\"user_name\\\")))\\n+        \\n+        candidates.append({\\n+            \\\"channel_id\\\": channel_id,\\n+            \\\"channel_name\\\": channel_name,\\n+            \\\"ts\\\": parent_ts,\\n+            \\\"permalink\\\": permalink,\\n+            \\\"is_thread\\\": True,\\n+            \\\"message_count\\\": len(cleaned_messages),\\n+            \\\"participants\\\": participants_names,\\n+            \\\"messages\\\": cleaned_messages,\\n+            \\\"preview\\\": cleaned_messages[0][\\\"text\\\"][:150] + \\\"...\\\" if len(cleaned_messages[0][\\\"text\\\"]) > 150 else cleaned_messages[0][\\\"text\\\"]\\n+        })\\n+        \\n+    return candidates\\n \\n def fetch_new_brains(client):\\n     user_id = client.auth_test()[\\\"user_id\\\"]\\n\", \"files_modified\": [\"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py\"], \"lint\": {\"status\": \"ok\", \"output\": \"\"}}",

5 preceding messages (including current)\\n   286|                res_prec = client.conversations_history(channel=channel_id, latest=ts, limit=6, inclusive=True)\\n   287|                msgs_prec = res_prec.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n   288|                \\n   289|                # 5 subsequent messages (including current)\\n   290|                res_sub = client.conversations_history(channel=channel_id, oldest=ts, limit=6, inclusive=True)\\n   291|                msgs_sub = res_sub.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n   292|                \\n   293|                # Combine and deduplicate\\n   294|                combined = {}\\n   295|                for m in msgs_prec + msgs_sub:\\n   296|                    combined[m[\\\"ts\\\"]] = m\\n   297|                messages = [combined[key_ts] for key_ts in sorted(combined.keys())]\\n   298|            except SlackApiError as e:\\n   299|                print(f\\\"Error fetching context for {ts} in {channel_id}: {e}\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\n   300|                messages = [msg]\\n   301|                \\n   302|        # check if already exists to prevent duplicate\\n   342|        key = f\\\"{channel_id}:{ts}\\\"\\n   343|        if not any(p[\\\"channel\\\"] == channel_id and p[\\\"ts\\\"] == ts for p in processed):\\n   344|            processed.append({\\\"channel\\\": channel_id, \\\"ts\\\": ts})\\n   345|            save_processed(processed)\\n   346|            print(json.dumps({\\\"ok\\\": True, \\\"marked\\\": key}))\\n   347|        else:\\n   348|            print(json.dumps({\\\"ok\\\": True, \\\"already_processed\\\": key}))\\n   349|        sys.exit(0)\\n   350|        \\n   351|    #           "content": "{\"output\": \"[\\n  {\\n    \\\"channel_id\\\": \\\"C0B32RWR5PC\\\",\\n    \\\"channel_name\\\": \\\"mobile-game-doctors-signlab\\\",\\n    \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780591352.016859\\\",\\n    \\\"permalink\\\": \\\"https://sign-lab.slack.com/archives/C0B32RWR5PC/p1780591352016859?thread_ts=1780591352.016859&cid=C0B32RWR5PC\\\",\\n    \\\"is_thread\\\": true,\\n    \\\"message_count\\\": 4,\\n    \\\"participants\\\": [\\n      \\\"Emma B\\\",\\n      \\\"Bruna\\\",\\n      \\\"Justin\\\"\\n    ],\\n    \\\"messages\\\": [\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780591352.016859\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U099S9K5VB9\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Emma B\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Hi everyone,\\\\nI\\\\u2019ve prepared a few slides to frame the next direction for Bloom and Joy, based on the feedback from the last iteration.\\\\nThe goal is to move them further away from a generic avatar feeling and closer to warm, expressive brand characters, with softer appeal, less plastic materials, more natural expressions, and a stronger emotional connection, while still keeping everything clear and simple enough for sign language.\\\\nI\\\\u2019m also including some thoughts on how the characters, real-person videos and UI could feel more integrated, so the experience feels warmer, more cohesive and less empty around the video areas.\\\\nI\\\\u2019ll walk you through the thinking in the presentation and would love to hear your feedback.\\\\n<https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1omlfEkUCNFv9eeQYAbitnWPvJl3_SDXBPIH9c3Nf4xM/edit?slide=id.g3e8531f98b8_0_0#slide=id.g3e8531f98b8_0_0>\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780616045.690519\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U073AF3UQKW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Bruna\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Done! :white_check_mark:\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780665198.059539\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U095LHMC4UW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Justin\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"I'd be curious to hear @Endre\\\\u2019s thoughts on this one.\\\\n\\\\nSubjectively, I liked the more cartoonish feel of the second iteration - it felt more distinctive and cozy, whereas the third iteration felt more similar to other things I've seen before. This is purely subjective and it's not something I'll insist on. :smile:\\\\n\\\\nBut I could see either one working, and I thought that the screen with Bloom and the other character together looked pretty nice. I do think the more detailed faces from iteration 3 may give us some more power when it comes to facial expressions, which is pretty important for us.\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780668706.002439\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U073AF3UQKW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Bruna\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"I liked the style of all of iterations to be honest. I don't have a particular opinion about that. I feel that the touch in the hair was also good for me.\\\\n\\\\nThe only thing I'm insecure about is how we want to convey this idea of the avatars. We need to be extremely careful not to convey our characters as a replacement for Deaf people/employees, but a gamification feature only.\\\"\\n      }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"preview\\\": \\\"Hi everyone,\\\\nI\\\\u2019ve prepared a few slides to frame the next direction for Bloom and Joy, based on the feedback from the last iteration.\\\\nThe goal is to m...\\\"\\n  },\\n  {\\n    \\\"channel_id\\\": \\\"C7A92PMCJ\\\",\\n    \\\"channel_name\\\": \\\"random\\\",\\n    \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780657827.620529\\\",\\n    \\\"permalink\\\": \\\"https://sign-lab.slack.com/archives/C7A92PMCJ/p1780657827620529?thread_ts=1780657827.620529&cid=C7A92PMCJ\\\",\\n    \\\"is_thread\\\": true,\\n    \\\"message_count\\\": 5,\\n    \\\"participants\\\": [\\n      \\\"Endre\\\",\\n      \\\"Justin\\\"\\n    ],\\n    \\\"messages\\\": [\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780657827.620529\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U095LHMC4UW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Justin\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Interesting tips, many of them based on the Compound Engineering plugin, which seems a little more hands-on than Superpowers (with all the pros and cons that entails).\\\\n<https://x.com/mvanhorn/status/2061877533885473181>\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780660189.933629\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U7A6V8E9Z\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Endre\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Really good article @Justin.
