true,\\n    \\\"message_count\\\": 4,\\n    \\\"participants\\\": [\\n      \\\"Justin\\\",\\n      \\\"Clio (bot)\\\"\\n    ],\\n    \\\"messages\\\": [\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780671238.430839\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U0B21KU2J7Q\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Clio (bot)\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"*Daily DAU \\\\u2014 Thursday, 2026-06-04 \\\\u2014 cc: @Justin*\\\\n\\\\n*PostHog SDK only.*\\\\n\\\\n*Overall: 25,858 DAU* (:arrow_lower_right: -3.6% vs previous 7d average)\\\\n\\\\n*By app:*\\\\n```\\\\nApp             | DAUs     | Trend | % Change (7d Avg) \\\\n----------------------------------------------------\\\\nASL Bloom       | 20,707   |  \\\\u25bc    | -2.5%             \\\\nBright BSL      | 1,009    |  \\\\u25bc    | -6.3%             \\\\nLibrasLab       | 976      |  \\\\u25ac    | -2.1%             \\\\nyoDGS           | 936      |  \\\\u25bc    | -11.7%            \\\\nAuslan Wiz      | 927      |  \\\\u25bc    | -6.3%             \\\\nPause LSF       | 587      |  \\\\u25bc    | -5.7%             \\\\nToleio          | 359      |  \\\\u25bc    | -7.2%             \\\\nMeLISegno       | 357      |  \\\\u25bc    | -16.6%            \\\\nPSL Isharay     | 0        |  NEW  | new               \\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n_ASL Bloom includes DAU from older Android builds with `product_name` unset._\\\\n_Note: Trend symbols (\\\\u25b2/\\\\u25bc) use volatility-scaled noise filtering. Small fluctuations are treated as neutral (\\\\u25ac)._\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*Anomaly Check*\\\\n2 anomalies detected\\\\n\\\\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Bright BSL - New User Onboarding` (ID: 49) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (787 vs 14-day avg: 1081.4, Z-score: -3.14)\\\\n\\\\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Auslan Wiz - New User Onboarding` (ID: 51) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (520 vs 14-day avg: 675.6, Z-score: -2.86)\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780672270.617289\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U095LHMC4UW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Justin\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"@Clio (bot) Does this data still include DAUs from older Android builds? Or have we moved passed that window?\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780672452.865999\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U0B21KU2J7Q\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Clio (bot)\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\":hourglass_flowing_sand: Still working... (3 min elapsed \\\\u2014 iteration 22/90, waiting for provider response (streaming))\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780672571.542739\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U0B21KU2J7Q\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Clio (bot)\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"No, *we have not moved past that window.* The data still heavily relies on older, legacy Android builds where `properties.product_name` is null. This legacy cohort represents a massive, highly stable portion of our daily active users.\\\\n\\\\nHere is the direct evidence from the live PostHog SDK database (Project 118126) for yesterday, *Thursday, 2026-06-04*:\\\\n\\\\n*Yesterday's Snapshot (2026-06-04)*\\\\n* *Overall Impact:* Out of *25,858* total DAUs, *5,717 (22.1%)* had `product_name` unset. \\\\n* *ASL Bloom Sizing:* For ASL Bloom (reported at *20,707* DAU yesterday), *5,717 (27.6%)* of those users actually had a null `product_name` and were coalesced into the ASL Bloom metrics by the reporting script.\\\\n* *The Platform Split:*\\\\n  * *Android:* *5,552* out of *6,675* Android DAUs had `product_name` unset\\\\u2014meaning *83.2% of our active Android users* are still running these older builds.\\\\n  * *iOS / iPadOS:* Only *163* active users had `product_name` unset, which is less than 1% of iOS traffic.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*The 30-Day Trend: A Stable Plateau*\\\\nLooking at the trend over the last month, we appear to have hit a hard plateau rather than a gradual phase-out:\\\\n\\\\n* *In early May,* the legacy cohort represented roughly 35% of overall DAUs.\\\\n* *By late May,* that share settled to around *22.1%*.\\\\n* *However, the absolute volume has remained completely flat.* For the past three weeks, the number of active users on these older builds (almost entirely *Android version 1.36.38*, which predates the super-property implementation as noted in `tracking_schema.yaml L2772`) has hovered consistently between *5,300 and 5,600 DAUs* every single day. \\\\n\\\\nThis indicates that this cohort represents highly retained, highly active users who are opening the app daily but are simply not upgrading their apps. If we were to remove the coalesce fallback today, our reported ASL Bloom DAU would appear to immediately drop by more than a quarter.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*A Minor Allocation Caveat*\\\\nA tiny sliver of these nulls (typically 10 to 25 DAUs; exactly *11* yesterday) are actually legacy *PSL Isharay* users (`product_id: 'signlab-pakistan'`). Because they also send a null `product_name`, the daily report's coalesce script currently groups them under ASL Bloom, though the volume is small enough to not skew the overall trends.\\\"\\n      }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"preview\\\": \\\"*Daily DAU \\\\u2014 Thursday, 2026-06-04 \\\\u2014 cc: @Justin*\\\\n\\\\n*PostHog SDK only.*\\\\n\\\\n*Overall: 25,858 DAU* (:arrow_lower_right: -3.6% vs previous 7d average)\\\\n\\\\n*By a...\\\"\\n  },\\n  {\\n    \\\"channel_id\\\": \\\"C09KDJQMUSH\\\",\\n    \\\"channel_name\\\": \\\"product-leads\\\",\\n    \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780646407.918809\\\",\\n    \\\"permalink\\\": \\\"https://sign-lab.slack.com/archives/C09KDJQMUSH/p1780646407918809?thread_ts=1780646407.918809&cid=C09KDJQMUSH\\\",\\n    \\\"is_thread\\\": true,\\n    \\\"message_count\\\": 4,\\n    \\\"participants\\\": [\\n      \\\"Tor\\\",\\n      \\\"Endre\\\",\\n      \\\"Justin\\\",\\n      \\\"B0ADS39ST19\\\"\\n    ],\\n    \\\"messages\\\": [\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780646407.918809\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"B0ADS39ST19\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"B0ADS39ST19\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Good morning! Please post a quick update in the thread. :speech_balloon:\\\\n\\\\u2022 What's your top priority for today?\\\\n\\\\u2022 Anything anyone can do to help you get that done?\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780646845.267429\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U7DJZAQ3G\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Tor\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Going to sleep now. Priorities I have in mind for the weekend:\\\\n\\\\u2022 App updates / bug fixing / robustness (sent some android updates today, and will work on sentry-issues from newest updates)\\\\n\\\\u2022 Live activities\\\\n\\\\u2022 Pricing AB-test (perhaps some posthog AB \\\\u201cinfrastructure\\\\u201c while I\\\\u2019m at it)\\\\n\\\\u2022 Integrations (revenuecat integration for web payments has been requested)\\\\nWill see how far I get / what I have time for.\\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780658236.475549\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U7A6V8E9Z\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Endre\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"Will push forward Paddle/Stripe MoR deal \\\"\\n      },\\n      {\\n        \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780663672.020359\\\",\\n        \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U095LHMC4UW\\\",\\n        \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Justin\\\",\\n        \\\"text\\\": \\\"\\\\u2022 Recovering physically from yesterday :joy: the last day of school includes 6 hours at the local amusement park ... my body is broken lol\\\\n\\\\u2022 Making some small improvements to Clio, experimenting with some other bot stuff\\\"\\n      }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"preview\\\": \\\"Good morning!           "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"content\": \"   100|### Phase 2 \u2014 Calendar summary\\n   101|\\n   102|Pull today's calendar and look ahead 7 days for anything major. Since the background cache file only stores *candidates* (events not yet on the calendar), you must fetch the actual scheduled events live.\\n   103|\\n   104|**Sources:** Fetch live from Google Workspace across all accounts (or personal accounts only on non-work days):\\n   105|- Work and personal: `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py --account all calendar list`\\n   106|- Personal only (weekends/days off): `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py --account personal-main,personal-junk calendar list`\\n   107|\\n   108|**Format:**\\n   109|```\\n   110|\ud83d\udcc5 Today \u2014 [Day, Mon DD]\\n   111|\\n   112|\u2022 HH:MM  Event name (attendees if external)\\n   113|\u2022 HH:MM  Event name\\n   114|[if nothing: \\\"Nothing on the calendar today.\\\"]\\n   115|\\n   116|Coming up this week:\\n   117|\u2022 [Day] Event name \u2014 [why it's notable: external attendees / prep needed / important]\\n   118|[only surface 2\u20133 notable items, not every standup]\\n   119|```\\n   120|\\n   121|**On non-work days (or \\\"day off\\\"):** show personal calendar events only. Skip work account events.\\n   122|\\n   123|After presenting, wait for acknowledgment before Phase 3.\\n   124|\\n   125|---\\n   126|\\n   127|### Phase 3 \u2014 Near-term task triage\\n   128|\\n   129|Query Todoist for tasks with due dates or deadlines in the next 3 days (inclusive of today).\\n   130|\\n   131|**Rule:** Skip any tasks that are already in the \\\"Now\\\" project, or completed/cancelled tasks.\\n   132|Only present this if there are matching tasks. If none, skip directly to the next phase.\\n   133|\\n   134|**Format:**\\n   135|```\\n   136|\ud83d\udccc N tasks due or with deadlines in the next 3 days:\\n   137|\\n   138|1. [Project] Task name (due: Date/Time | deadline: Date)\\n   139|...\\n   140|\\n   141|Would you like to move any of these to \\\"Now\\\"?\\n   142|```\\n   143|\\n   144|Once Justin selects tasks, move them to the \\\"Now\\\" project.\\n   145|\\n   146|After presenting and handling any movements, wait for acknowledgment before Phase 4.\\n   147|\\n   148|---\\n   149|\\n   150|### Phase 4 \u2014 Calendar inbox candidates\\n   151|\\n   152|Present the calendar event candidates from `inbox_candidates.calendar_events`.\\n   153|\\n   154|Suggest a target calendar (`work` or `personal-main`) for each candidate based on its source and content (e.g., family/school/personal tasks default to `personal-main`, while SignLab/professional ones default to `work`).\\n   155|\\n   156|Format:\\n   157|```\\n   158|\ud83d\udcc5 N potential calendar events not yet on your calendar:\\n   159|\\n   160|1. [Source] Event | when: date/time | calendar: work | context: where found\\n   161|2. [Source] Event | when: date/time | calendar: personal-main | context: where found\\n   162|...\\n   163|\\n   164|Which ones should I add directly to your Google Calendar?\\n   165|```\\n   166|\\n   167|If `calendar_events` is empty, say \\\"Nothing new to add to your calendar.\\\" and skip to Phase 5.\\n   168|\\n   169|Once Justin confirms selections:\\n   170|- Schedule/create the events directly on the respective Google Calendar accounts (`work` or `personal-main`) using the calendar write capabilities (`gws_multi.py --account <name> calendar create --summary \\\"...\\\" --start \\\"...\\\" --end \\\"...\\\" --attendees \\\"...\\\" --description \\\"...\\\"`).\\n   171|- Only create a Todoist \\\"Add to calendar:\\\" task as an exceptional fallback if Justin specifically requests it or if the event timing/date is too ambiguous to schedule directly.\\n   172|- Report the scheduling confirmation with event details and links, then move to Phase 5.\\n   173|\\n   174|**On non-work days / day off:** still run this phase but filter to personal events only (skip work Slack, work email sources).\\n   175|\\n   176|---\\n   177|\\n   178|### Phase 5 \u2014 Inbox candidates\\n   179|\\n   180|Present the action-item candidates from `inbox_candidates.action_items`.\\n   181|\\n   182|Use the format from `todoist-inbox-fill` Step 4 Section B \u2014 semantically batched groups:\\n   183|```\\n   184|\ud83d\udce5 N potential inbox items:\\n   185|\\n   186|**[Group label]**\\n   187|1. [Source] Task description\\n   188|2. [Source] Task description\\n   189|\\n   190|**[Group label]**\\n   191|3. ...\\n   192|\\n   193|Which ones should I add?\\n   194|```\\n   195|\\n   196|If `action_items` is empty, say \\\"Inbox looks clear.\\\" \\n   197|\\n   198|Once Justin confirms \u2192 batch-add to Todoist Inbox with comments.\\n   199|\\n   200|**On non-work days / day off:** filter out work-sourced tasks (Linear, work email, work Slack). Keep home/family/personal ones.\\n   201|\\n   202|---\\n   203|\\n   204|### After all phases\\n   205|\\n   206|If vault hygiene Tier 2 issues exist in the cache, append a brief report at the end:\\n   207|\\n   208|```\\n   209|\ud83d\uddc2 Vault hygiene \u2014 N items to look at when you have a moment:\\n   210|\u2022 Notebook/Some Note.md \u2014 missing category (looks like an essay)\\n   211|\u2022 ...\\n   212|```\\n   213|\\n   214|Don't block on this or ask for immediate action. It's informational.\\n   215|\\n   216|Then close out: *\\\"That's everything. Have a good [day/weekend/Monday].\\\"* (Match the day.)\\n   217|\\n   218|---\\n   219|\\n   220|\", \"total_lines\": 256, \"file_size\": 11786, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=220 to continue reading (showing 100-219 of 256 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

[Source] Task description\\n   188|2. [Source] Task description\\n   189|\\n   190|**[Group label]**\\n   191|3. ...\\n   192|\\n   193|Which ones should I add?\\n   194|```\\n   195|\", \"total_lines\": 256, \"file_size\": 11786, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=195 to continue reading (showing 160-194 of 256 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

Channel name (e.g., `\\\\#product-leads`)\\n+  - Participants list\\n+  - A short topic / text preview\\n+- Format:\\n+  ```\\n+  \ud83e\udde0 N potential source notes:\\n+\\n+  1. For each selected conversation:\\n+   - Synthesize a high-quality summary showing \\\"who said what\\\" clearly.\\n+   - Use the standard Obsidian note layout for Slack notes (write to `/home/justin.guest/vault/sources/slack/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md`).\\n+   - Append a link and one-sentence gist to today's daily note notepad under `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad`.\\n+   - Run the command to mark it processed:\\n+     `python3 /home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py --mark-processed <channel_id> <ts>`\\n+   - Report the note(s) successfully saved.\\n+\\n+If no candidates are found, skip this phase entirely and proceed to Phase 5.\\n \\n ---\\n \\n\", \"files_modified\": [\"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/morning-briefing/SKILL.md\"], \"lint\": {\"status\": \"skipped\", \"message\": \"No linter for .md files\"}, \"_warning\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/morning-briefing/SKILL.md was last read with offset/limit pagination (partial view).           "role": "assistant",
          "content": "This is completely set up.\n\n1. **Vault & gbrain mapping done:** Notes added via the briefing go to the same `sources/slack/` directory and are fully recognized as `slack` page types in `gbrain`.\n2. **Interactive Phase 4.5 Added:** I've modified your `morning-briefing` skill. There is now a dedicated **Phase 4.5 \u2014 Source note candidates** situated right between Calendar Inbox Candidates and general Inbox Candidates.\n3. **Smart Candidate Detection:** I added `--list-candidates` to the Python helper. When the briefing runs Phase 4.5, it scans your last 36 hours of Slack activity for active threads with:\n   *   `>= 3` messages total (ensuring it's an actual back-and-forth).\n   *   `>= 2` unique human participants.\n   *   Your direct participation or mention.\n   *   **No existing `\ud83e\udde0` reaction** (if you already brain-tagged it, the automated cron handles it and the briefing skips it to avoid double-prompting).\n\n### What it looks like during the briefing:\n```markdown\n\ud83e\udde0 2 potential source notes:\n\n1. [\\#mobile-game-doctors-signlab] Discussion on characters Joy and Bloom \u2014 participants: Emma B, Bruna, Justin\n2. [\\#random] Twitter article on Compound Engineering vs Superpowers \u2014 participants: Endre, Justin\n\nWould you like to turn any of these conversations into notes in your vault?\n```\n\nIf you select any (e.g., `\"yes, 1\"` or `\"save 2\"`), I will synthesize the conversation, write it to `sources/slack/` with the exact same structure as the `\ud83e\udde0` automation, append the link and gist to your Daily Note's `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad`, and mark it as processed.",

        {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"google-workspace\", \"description\": \"Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets via gws CLI or Python.\", \"tags\": [\"Google\", \"Gmail\", \"Calendar\", \"Drive\", \"Sheets\", \"Docs\", \"Contacts\", \"Email\", \"OAuth\"], \"related_skills\": [\"himalaya\"], \"content\": \"---\\nname: google-workspace\\ndescription: \\\"Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets via gws CLI or Python.\\\"\\nversion: 1.1.0\\nauthor: Nous Research\\nlicense: MIT\\nplatforms: [linux, macos, windows]\\nrequired_credential_files:\\n  - path: google_token.json\\n    description: Google OAuth2 token (created by setup script)\\n  - path: google_client_secret.json\\n    description: Google OAuth2 client credentials (downloaded from Google Cloud Console)\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [Google, Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Sheets, Docs, Contacts, Email, OAuth]\\n    homepage: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent\\n    related_skills: [himalaya]\\n---\\n\\n# Google Workspace\\n\\nGmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs \u2014 through Hermes-managed OAuth and a thin CLI wrapper. When `gws` is installed, the skill uses it as the execution backend for broader Google Workspace coverage; otherwise it falls back to the bundled Python client implementation.\\n\\n\\n> \u26a0\ufe0f **GMAIL-READ-ONLY MODE (Bes-specific customization)**\\n>\\n> This installation of the google-workspace skill has **full write access** for\\n> **Google Calendar, Google Drive, Google Sheets, and Google Docs**, so you can\\n> schedule events, upload/delete files, and manage documents/spreadsheets on\\n> Justin's behalf.\\n>\\n> **Gmail** remains strictly **read-only** (`gmail.readonly`) for security.\\n>\\n> **You can use these write commands:**\\n> - `calendar create`, `calendar delete`\\n> - `drive upload`, `drive create-folder`, `drive share`, `drive delete`\\n> - `sheets create`, `sheets update`, `sheets append`\\n> - `docs create`, `docs append`\\n>\\n> **You can use these read-only subcommands:**\\n> - `gmail search`, `gmail get`, `gmail labels`\\n> - `calendar list`\\n> - `drive search`, `drive get`, `drive download`\\n> - `contacts list`\\n> - `sheets get`\\n> - `docs get`\\n>\\n> **You CANNOT use these write commands \u2014 calls will return 403:**\\n> - `gmail send`, `gmail reply`, `gmail modify`\\n>\\n> If Justin asks you to send or modify email, tell him Gmail is read-only.\\n> All other Google Workspace services (Calendar, Drive, Sheets, Docs) have full write capabilities.\\n\\n## References\\n\\n- `references/gmail-search-syntax.md` \u2014 Gmail search operators (is:unread, from:, newer_than:, etc.)\\n\\n## Scripts\\n\\n- `scripts/setup.py` \u2014 OAuth2 setup (run once to authorize)\\n- `scripts/google_api.py` \u2014 compatibility wrapper CLI. It prefers `gws` for operations when available, while preserving Hermes' existing JSON output contract.\\n\\n## First-Time Setup\\n\\nThe setup is fully non-interactive \u2014 you drive it step by step so it works\\non CLI, Telegram, Discord, or any platform.\\n\\nDefine a shorthand first:\\n\\n```bash\\nGSETUP=\\\"python ${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/setup.py\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Step 0: Check if already set up\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --check\\n```\\n\\nIf it prints `AUTHENTICATED`, skip to Usage \u2014 setup is already done.\\n\\n### Step 1: Triage \u2014 ask the user what they need\\n\\nBefore starting OAuth setup, ask the user TWO questions:\\n\\n**Question 1: \\\"What Google services do you need? Just email, or also\\nCalendar/Drive/Sheets/Docs?\\\"**\\n\\n- **Email only** \u2192 They don't need this skill at all. Use the `himalaya` skill\\n  instead \u2014 it works with a Gmail App Password (Settings \u2192 Security \u2192 App\\n  Passwords) and takes 2 minutes to set up. No Google Cloud project needed.\\n  Load the himalaya skill and follow its setup instructions.\\n\\n- **Email + Calendar** \u2192 Continue with this skill, but use\\n  `--services email,calendar` during auth so the consent screen only asks for\\n  the scopes they actually need.\\n\\n- **Calendar/Drive/Sheets/Docs only** \u2192 Continue with this skill and use a\\n  narrower `--services` set like `calendar,drive,sheets,docs`.\\n\\n- **Full Workspace access** \u2192 Continue with this skill and use the default\\n  `all` service set.\\n\\n**Question 2: \\\"Does your Google account use Advanced Protection (hardware\\nsecurity keys required to sign in)? If you're not sure, you probably don't\\n\u2014 it's something you would have explicitly enrolled in.\\\"**\\n\\n- **No / Not sure** \u2192 Normal setup. Continue below.\\n- **Yes** \u2192 Their Workspace admin must add the OAuth client ID to the org's\\n  allowed apps list before Step 4 will work. Let them know upfront.\\n\\n### Step 2: Create OAuth credentials (one-time, ~5 minutes)\\n\\nTell the user:\\n\\n> You need a Google Cloud OAuth client. This is a one-time setup:\\n>\\n> 1. Create or select a project:\\n>    https://console.cloud.google.com/projectselector2/home/dashboard\\n> 2. Enable the required APIs from the API Library:\\n>    https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library\\n>    Enable: Gmail API, Google Calendar API, Google Drive API,\\n>    Google Sheets API, Google Docs API, People API\\n> 3. Create the OAuth client here:\\n>    https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials\\n>    Credentials \u2192 Create Credentials \u2192 OAuth 2.0 Client ID\\n> 4. Application type: \\\"Desktop app\\\" \u2192 Create\\n> 5. If the app is still in Testing, add the user's Google account as a test user here:\\n>    https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/audience\\n>    Audience \u2192 Test users \u2192 Add users\\n> 6. Download the JSON file and tell me the file path\\n>\\n> Important Hermes CLI note: if the file path starts with `/`, do NOT send only the bare path as its own message in the CLI, because it can be mistaken for a slash command. Send it in a sentence instead, like:\\n> `The JSON file path is: /home/user/Downloads/client_secret_....json`\\n\\nOnce they provide the path:\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --client-secret /path/to/client_secret.json\\n```\\n\\nIf they paste the raw client ID / client secret values instead of a file path,\\nwrite a valid Desktop OAuth JSON file for them yourself, save it somewhere\\nexplicit (for example `~/Downloads/hermes-google-client-secret.json`), then run\\n`--client-secret` against that file.\\n\\n### Step 3: Get authorization URL\\n\\nUse the service set chosen in Step 1. Examples:\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --auth-url --services email,calendar --format json\\n$GSETUP --auth-url --services calendar,drive,sheets,docs --format json\\n$GSETUP --auth-url --services all --format json\\n```\\n\\nThis returns JSON with an `auth_url` field and also saves the exact URL to\\n`~/.hermes/google_oauth_last_url.txt`.\\n\\nAgent rules for this step:\\n- Extract the `auth_url` field and send that exact URL to the user as a single line.\\n- Tell the user that the browser will likely fail on `http://localhost:1` after approval, and that this is expected.\\n- Tell them to copy the ENTIRE redirected URL from the browser address bar.\\n- If the user gets `Error 403: access_denied`, send them directly to `https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/audience` to add themselves as a test user.\\n\\n### Step 4: Exchange the code\\n\\nThe user will paste back either a URL like `http://localhost:1/?code=4/0A...&scope=...`\\nor just the code string. Either works. The `--auth-url` step stores a temporary\\npending OAuth session locally so `--auth-code` can complete the PKCE exchange\\nlater, even on headless systems:\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --auth-code \\\"THE_URL_OR_CODE_THE_USER_PASTED\\\" --format json\\n```\\n\\nIf `--auth-code` fails because the code expired, was already used, or came from\\nan older browser tab, it now returns a fresh `fresh_auth_url`. In that case,\\nimmediately send the new URL to the user and have them retry with the newest\\nbrowser redirect only.\\n\\n### Step 5: Verify\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --check\\n```\\n\\nShould print `AUTHENTICATED`. Setup is complete \u2014 token refreshes automatically from now on.\\n\\n### Notes\\n\\n- Token is stored at `~/.hermes/google_token.json` and auto-refreshes.\\n- Pending OAuth session state/verifier are stored temporarily at `~/.hermes/google_oauth_pending.json` until exchange completes.\\n- If `gws` is installed, `google_api.py` points it at the same `~/.hermes/google_token.json` credentials file. Users do not need to run a separate `gws auth login` flow.\\n- To revoke: `$GSETUP --revoke`\\n\\n## Usage\\n\\nAll commands go through the API script. Set `GAPI` as a shorthand:\\n\\n```bash\\nGAPI=\\\"python ${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/google_api.py\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Gmail\\n\\n```bash\\n# Search (returns JSON array with id, from, subject, date, snippet)\\n$GAPI gmail search \\\"is:unread\\\" --max 10\\n$GAPI gmail search \\\"from:boss@company.com newer_than:1d\\\"\\n$GAPI gmail search \\\"has:attachment filename:pdf newer_than:7d\\\"\\n\\n# Read full message (returns JSON with body text)\\n$GAPI gmail get MESSAGE_ID\\n\\n# Extract one field as raw text \u2014 preferred for shell, avoids `| python3 -c` and `| jq`\\n# (the python3 pipe pattern trips Hermes' command-approval security scanner)\\n$GAPI gmail get MESSAGE_ID --field body\\n$GAPI gmail get MESSAGE_ID --field body --max-len 2000\\n$GAPI gmail get MESSAGE_ID --field subject\\n# Fallback if you need a field --field doesn't cover, use jq (NOT python3 -c):\\n$GAPI gmail get MESSAGE_ID | jq -r '.body | .[0:2000]'\\n\\n# Send\\n$GAPI gmail send --to user@example.com --subject \\\"Hello\\\" --body \\\"Message text\\\"\\n$GAPI gmail send --to user@example.com --subject \\\"Report\\\" --body \\\"<h1>Q4</h1><p>Details...</p>\\\" --html\\n$GAPI gmail send --to user@example.com --subject \\\"Hello\\\" --from '\\\"Research Agent\\\" <user@example.com>' --body \\\"Message text\\\"\\n\\n# Reply (automatically threads and sets In-Reply-To)\\n$GAPI gmail reply MESSAGE_ID --body \\\"Thanks, that works for me.\\\"\\n$GAPI gmail reply MESSAGE_ID --from '\\\"Support Bot\\\" <user@example.com>' --body \\\"Thanks\\\"\\n\\n# Labels\\n$GAPI gmail labels\\n$GAPI gmail modify MESSAGE_ID --add-labels LABEL_ID\\n$GAPI gmail modify MESSAGE_ID --remove-labels UNREAD\\n```\\n\\n### Calendar\\n\\n```bash\\n# List events (defaults to next 7 days)\\n$GAPI calendar list\\n$GAPI calendar list --start 2026-03-01T00:00:00Z --end 2026-03-07T23:59:59Z\\n\\n# Create event (ISO 8601 with timezone required)\\n$GAPI calendar create --summary \\\"Team Standup\\\" --start 2026-03-01T10:00:00-06:00 --end 2026-03-01T10:30:00-06:00\\n$GAPI calendar create --summary \\\"Lunch\\\" --start 2026-03-01T12:00:00Z --end 2026-03-01T13:00:00Z --location \\\"Cafe\\\"\\n$GAPI calendar create --summary \\\"Review\\\" --start 2026-03-01T14:00:00Z --end 2026-03-01T15:00:00Z --attendees \\\"alice@co.com,bob@co.com\\\"\\n\\n# Delete event\\n$GAPI calendar delete EVENT_ID\\n```\\n\\n### Drive\\n\\n```bash\\n# Search existing files\\n$GAPI drive search \\\"quarterly report\\\" --max 10\\n$GAPI drive search \\\"mimeType='application/pdf'\\\" --raw-query --max 5\\n\\n# Get metadata for a single file\\n$GAPI drive get FILE_ID\\n\\n# Upload a local file (auto-detects MIME type)\\n$GAPI drive upload /path/to/report.pdf\\n$GAPI drive upload /path/to/image.png --name \\\"Logo.png\\\" --parent FOLDER_ID\\n\\n# Download (binary files download as-is; Google-native files export to a\\n# sensible default \u2014 Docs\u2192pdf, Sheets\u2192csv, Slides\u2192pdf, Drawings\u2192png)\\n$GAPI drive download FILE_ID\\n$GAPI drive download DOC_ID --output ~/doc.pdf\\n$GAPI drive download DOC_ID --export-mime text/plain --output ~/doc.txt\\n\\n# Create a folder\\n$GAPI drive create-folder \\\"Reports\\\"\\n$GAPI drive create-folder \\\"Q4\\\" --parent FOLDER_ID\\n\\n# Share\\n$GAPI drive share FILE_ID --email alice@example.com --role reader\\n$GAPI drive share FILE_ID --email alice@example.com --role writer --notify\\n$GAPI drive share FILE_ID --type anyone --role reader        # anyone with link\\n$GAPI drive share FILE_ID --type domain --domain example.com --role reader\\n\\n# Delete \u2014 defaults to trash (reversible). Use --permanent to skip the trash.\\n$GAPI drive delete FILE_ID\\n$GAPI drive delete FILE_ID --permanent\\n```\\n\\n### Contacts\\n\\n```bash\\n$GAPI contacts list --max 20\\n```\\n\\n### Sheets\\n\\n```bash\\n# Create a new spreadsheet\\n$GAPI sheets create --title \\\"Q4 Budget\\\"\\n$GAPI sheets create --title \\\"Inventory\\\" --sheet-name \\\"Stock\\\"\\n\\n# Read\\n$GAPI sheets get SHEET_ID \\\"Sheet1!A1:D10\\\"\\n\\n# Write\\n$GAPI sheets update SHEET_ID \\\"Sheet1!A1:B2\\\" --values '[[\\\"Name\\\",\\\"Score\\\"],[\\\"Alice\\\",\\\"95\\\"]]'\\n\\n# Append rows\\n$GAPI sheets append SHEET_ID \\\"Sheet1!A:C\\\" --values '[[\\\"new\\\",\\\"row\\\",\\\"data\\\"]]'\\n```\\n\\n### Docs\\n### Docs\\n\\n# Read\\n$GAPI docs get DOC_ID\\n\\n# Create a new Doc (optionally seeded with body text)\\n$GAPI docs create --title \\\"Meeting Notes\\\"\\n$GAPI docs create --title \\\"Draft\\\" --body \\\"First paragraph...\\\"\\n\\n# Append text to the end of an existing Doc\\n$GAPI docs append DOC_ID --text \\\"Additional content to append\\\"\\n\\n\\n### Direct API Access via Python (Advanced/Custom Operations)\\n\\nWhen the CLI wrapper lacks subcommands for specific write or update actions (such as updating or patching calendar events), you can write a Python script that imports `google_api` and builds the service directly using `build_service(api, version)` to gain full access to the underlying API capabilities.\\n\\n```python\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\n\\n# Add google-workspace scripts directory to sys.path\\ngw_scripts_dir = \\\"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts\\\"\\nif gw_scripts_dir not in sys.path:\\n    sys.path.insert(0, gw_scripts_dir)\\n\\n# Set the active account\\nos.environ[\\\"GOOGLE_ACCOUNT\\\"] = \\\"personal-main\\\"\\n\\nimport google_api\\nservice = google_api.build_service(\\\"calendar\\\", \\\"v3\\\")\\n\\n# Example: Update/patch an existing event\\nservice.events().patch(\\n    calendarId=\\\"primary\\\",\\n    eventId=\\\"EVENT_ID\\\",\\n    body={\\n        \\\"summary\\\": \\\"New Summary\\\",\\n        \\\"description\\\": \\\"New Description\\\"\\n    }\\n).execute()\\n```\\n\\n\\n## Multi-Account Usage (Bes customization)\\n## Multi-Account Usage (Bes customization)\\n\\nJustin has multiple Google accounts. This installation supports per-account tokens\\nand cross-account read queries. Tokens are stored as\\n`~/.hermes/google_tokens/<account>.json`.\\n\\n### Listing registered accounts\\n\\n```bash\\nGWS_MULTI=\\\"python ${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/skills/productivity/google-workspace/scripts/gws_multi.py\\\"\\n$GWS_MULTI accounts\\n```\\n\\nReturns JSON like `{\\\"accounts\\\": [{\\\"account\\\": \\\"work\\\", \\\"auth_valid\\\": true}, ...]}`.\\n\\n### Running a command against a specific account\\n\\nUse `--account <name>` (BEFORE the gmail/calendar/etc. subcommand):\\n\\n```bash\\n$GWS_MULTI --account work gmail search \\\"is:unread newer_than:1d\\\" --max 5\\n$GWS_MULTI --account personal-main calendar list\\n$GWS_MULTI --account personal-junk gmail search \\\"from:newsletter@example.com\\\" --max 10\\n```\\n\\n### Running a command across ALL accounts (cross-account view)\\n\\nUse `--account all`:\\n\\n```bash\\n$GWS_MULTI --account all calendar list           # All events across work + personal accounts\\n$GWS_MULTI --account all gmail search \\\"is:unread newer_than:1d\\\" --max 5\\n```\\n\\nOr a subset:\\n\\n```bash\\n$GWS_MULTI --account work,personal-main calendar list\\n```\\n\\n### Extracting a single field as raw text (preferred over pipes)\\n\\nWhen you only need one field from a `gmail get` (typically `body`), use\\n`--field` \u2014 it prints the raw value with no JSON wrapping, no jq, and no\\n`python3 -c`. The `python3 -c` pattern trips Hermes' command-approval\\nsecurity scanner with a HIGH \\\"pipe to interpreter\\\" finding every time, so\\nprefer `--field` for routine work.\\n\\n```bash\\n# Single account, raw body text:\\n$GWS_MULTI --account personal-main gmail get MSG_ID --field body --max-len 2000\\n\\n# Multiple accounts: each account's output is prefixed with === <name> ===\\n$GWS_MULTI --account work,personal-main gmail get MSG_ID --field body\\n\\n# Reading several emails in one go (the original use case):\\nfor ID in 19e45975c0bdc35f 19e46218eb633052 19e457c4f0f6e434; do\\n  echo \\\"=== $ID ===\\\"\\n  $GWS_MULTI --account personal-main gmail get \\\"$ID\\\" --field body --max-len 2000\\ndone\\n```\\n\\nDocumented fallback when `--field` doesn't cover what you need (nested\\nfields, multiple fields in one call, transformations): use `jq`, not\\n`python3 -c`. The scanner doesn't flag jq.\\n\\n```bash\\n$GWS_MULTI --account personal-main gmail get MSG_ID \\\\\\n  | jq -r '.body | .[0:2000]'\\n$GWS_MULTI --account personal-main gmail get MSG_ID \\\\\\n  | jq -r '\\\"Subject: \\\\(.subject)\\\\nFrom: \\\\(.from)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\(.body[0:2000])\\\"'\\n```\\n\\n### Output format with --account\\n\\nEvery record in the response gets an `\\\"account\\\": \\\"<name>\\\"` field added so you can\\ntell which account contributed which row. Per-account errors don't abort the\\nwhole run \u2014 they're collected into a top-level `\\\"_errors\\\"` field on the response.\\n\\nWhen summarizing for Justin, **always preface results with the account tag**:\\n- \u2705 \\\"On `work`: 3 unread; on `personal-main`: 1 unread.\\\"\\n- \u274c \\\"4 unread emails.\\\" (loses the per-account view he wants)\\n\\n### Decision: when to use --account all vs. a single account\\n\\n- **Default to `--account all`** for time-sensitive queries (\\\"what's on my calendar\\n  today\\\", \\\"any urgent unread emails\\\") \u2014 Justin lives across all his accounts.\\n- Use a **specific account** when Justin explicitly names one (\\\"check work email\\n  from Alice\\\") or when the query is account-specific by topic (e.g. company-only\\n  Drive folders \u2192 `--account work`).\\n- For write operations (like creating calendar events, managing files, or editing docs/sheets): specify the correct target account using `--account <name>` (e.g. `gws_multi.py --account work calendar create ...` or `gws_multi.py --account personal-main calendar create ...`). Do not use `--account all` for write operations.\\n\\n### Legacy single-account compatibility\\n\\nIf `--account` is omitted, the script falls back to the legacy single-token path\\n(`~/.hermes/google_token.json`) which is now a symlink to\\n`google_tokens/work.json`. So old calls like\\n`python google_api.py gmail search ...` still work and resolve to the work\\naccount. Prefer the explicit `--account` form in new code.\\n\\n## Output Format\\n\\nAll commands return JSON. Parse with `jq` or read directly. Key fields:\\n\\n- **Gmail search**: `[{id, threadId, from, to, subject, date, snippet, labels}]`\\n- **Gmail get**: `{id, threadId, from, to, subject, date, labels, body}`\\n- **Gmail send/reply**: `{status: \\\"sent\\\", id, threadId}`\\n- **Calendar list**: `[{id, summary, start, end, location, description, htmlLink}]`\\n- **Calendar create**: `{status: \\\"created\\\", id, summary, htmlLink}`\\n- **Drive search**: `[{id, name, mimeType, modifiedTime, webViewLink}]`\\n- **Drive get**: `{id, name, mimeType, modifiedTime, size, webViewLink, parents, owners}`\\n- **Drive upload**: `{status: \\\"uploaded\\\", id, name, mimeType, webViewLink}`\\n- **Drive download**: `{status: \\\"downloaded\\\", id, name, path, mimeType}`\\n- **Drive create-folder**: `{status: \\\"created\\\", id, name, webViewLink}`\\n- **Drive share**: `{status: \\\"shared\\\", permissionId, fileId, role, type}`\\n- **Drive delete**: `{status: \\\"trashed\\\" | \\\"deleted\\\", fileId, permanent}`\\n- **Contacts list**: `[{name, emails: [...], phones: [...]}]`\\n- **Sheets get**: `[[cell, cell, ...], ...]`\\n- **Sheets create**: `{status: \\\"created\\\", spreadsheetId, title, spreadsheetUrl}`\\n- **Docs create**: `{status: \\\"created\\\", documentId, title, url}`\\n- **Docs append**: `{status: \\\"appended\\\", documentId, inserted_at, characters}`\\n\\n## Email noise filtering (for action-item capture)\\n\\nWhen scanning Gmail to extract action items for Todoist or similar:\\n\\n**Skip these automatically \u2014 they are never actionable for Justin:**\\n- Automated notifications: shipping/delivery (Amazon, etc.), parking receipts (PayByPhone), payment receipts\\n- App Store Connect issue alerts \u2014 Justin doesn't handle these; engineering does\\n- Calendar invite confirmations (accepted/declined auto-replies)\\n- Marketing newsletters (Mobbin, Loom, Figma announcements, Substack, Readwise summaries, Lenny's Newsletter, etc.)\\n- Todoist onboarding/tip emails\\n- Raymond James email-address-update confirmations\\n- Proud Haven / Hello Neighbor weekly newsletters (volunteer org updates, not personal asks)\\n- Guidepoint consultation requests \u2014 decline unless Justin explicitly says he wants to take them\\n\\n**Read the full body before deciding on:**\\n- Emails from schools (Waldorf Pittsburgh, Winchester-Thurston) \u2014 often contain hard deadlines\\n- Emails from family (Nana, teachers) \u2014 may require coordinated action\\n- Raymond James / financial advisor threads \u2014 may have pending decisions\\n- Emails from SignLab colleagues \u2014 check for asks, not just FYIs\\n\\n**Capture rule:** Only add to Todoist if there is a concrete next action for Justin. Informational emails, FYIs, and emails where Justin already replied are not actions.\\n\\n## Rules\\n\\n1. **Never send email, delete Drive files, share files, or modify Docs/Sheets without confirming with the user first.** Show what will be done (recipients, file IDs, content, share role) and ask for approval. For `drive delete`, prefer the default trash (reversible) over `--permanent`. For creating/deleting calendar events, confirm with Justin first unless he explicitly instructs you to create/schedule the events on his behalf (e.g. from forwarded emails, or during the morning briefing conversation).\\n2. **Check auth before first use** \u2014 run `setup.py --check`. If it fails, guide the user through setup.\\n3. **Use the Gmail search syntax reference** for complex queries \u2014 load it with `skill_view(\\\"google-workspace\\\", file_path=\\\"references/gmail-search-syntax.md\\\")`.\\n4. **Calendar times must include timezone** \u2014 always use ISO 8601 with offset (e.g., `2026-03-01T10:00:00-06:00`) or UTC (`Z`).\\n5. **Respect rate limits** \u2014 avoid rapid-fire sequential API calls. Batch reads when possible.\\n\\n## Pitfalls\\n\\n1. **`json.loads()` fails on Gmail search output with control characters.** Email snippets frequently contain Unicode control chars (e.g. `\\\\u034f`, zero-width spaces) that cause `json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Invalid control character`. Always use `json_parse()` from `hermes_tools` (which uses `strict=False`) instead of `json.loads()` when parsing gmail search/get output in `execute_code`.\\n\\n   ```python\\n   from hermes_tools import json_parse\\n   data = json_parse(result[\\\"output\\\"])  # not json.loads(result[\\\"output\\\"])\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Use `--field body --max-len N` for targeted email reads.** Avoids piping through `python3 -c` (trips the security scanner) and keeps output manageable. Fall back to `jq` (not `python3 -c`) when you need multiple fields or transformations.\\n\\n## Troubleshooting\\n\\n| Problem | Fix |\\n|---------|-----|\\n| `NOT_AUTHENTICATED` | Run setup Steps 2-5 above |\\n| `REFRESH_FAILED` | Token revoked or expired \u2014 redo Steps 3-5 |\\n| `HttpError 403: Insufficient Permission` | Missing API scope \u2014 `$GSETUP --revoke` then redo Steps 3-5 |\\n| `AUTHENTICATED (partial)` or \\\"Token missing scopes\\\" | New write capabilities (Drive write/delete, Docs create/edit) require re-authorization. `$GSETUP --revoke` then redo Steps 3-5 to grant the upgraded scopes. |\\n| `HttpError 403: Access Not Configured` | API not enabled \u2014 user needs to enable it in Google Cloud Console |\\n| `ModuleNotFoundError` | Run `$GSETUP --install-deps` |\\n| Advanced Protection blocks auth | Workspace admin must allowlist the OAuth client ID |\\n\\n## Revoking Access\\n\\n```bash\\n$GSETUP --revoke\\n```\\n\", \"path\": \"productivity/google-workspace/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/google-workspace\", \"linked_files\": {\"references\": [\"references/gmail-search-syntax.md\"], \"scripts\": [\"scripts/google_api.py\", \"scripts/setup.py\", \"scripts/gws_multi.py\", \"scripts/gws_bridge.py\", \"scripts/_hermes_home.py\"]}, \"usage_hint\": \"To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'\", \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\", \"metadata\": {\"hermes\": {\"tags\": [\"Google\", \"Gmail\", \"Calendar\", \"Drive\", \"Sheets\", \"Docs\", \"Contacts\", \"Email\", \"OAuth\"], \"homepage\": \"https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent\", \"related_skills\": [\"himalaya\"]}}}",

Small fluctuations are treated as neutral (\\\\u25ac)._\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*Anomaly Check*\\\\n2 anomalies detected\\\\n\\\\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Bright BSL - New User Onboarding` (ID: 49) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (787 vs 14-day avg: 1081.4, Z-score: -3.14)\\\\n\\\\u2022 [<http://Customer.io|Customer.io>] Volume Drop: Campaign `Auslan Wiz - New User Onboarding` (ID: 51) entries dropped significantly on *2026-06-04 (yesterday)* (520 vs 14-day avg: 675.6, Z-score: -2.86)\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780672270.617289\\\",\\n          \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U095LHMC4UW\\\",\\n          \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Justin\\\",\\n          \\\"text\\\": \\\"@Clio (bot) Does this data still include DAUs from older Android builds? (3 min elapsed \\\\u2014 iteration 22/90, waiting for provider response (streaming))\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"ts\\\": \\\"1780672571.542739\\\",\\n          \\\"user_id\\\": \\\"U0B21KU2J7Q\\\",\\n          \\\"user_name\\\": \\\"Clio (bot)\\\",\\n          \\\"text\\\": \\\"No, *we have not moved past that window.* The data still heavily relies on older, legacy Android builds where `properties.product_name` is null. This legacy cohort represents a massive, highly stable portion of our daily active users.\\\\n\\\\nHere is the direct evidence from the live PostHog SDK database (Project 118126) for yesterday, *Thursday, 2026-06-04*:\\\\n\\\\n*Yesterday's Snapshot (2026-06-04)*\\\\n* *Overall Impact:* Out of *25,858* total DAUs, *5,717 (22.1%)* had `product_name` unset. \\\\n* *ASL Bloom Sizing:* For ASL Bloom (reported at *20,707* DAU yesterday), *5,717 (27.6%)* of those users actually had a null `product_name` and were coalesced into the ASL Bloom metrics by the reporting script.\\\\n* *The Platform Split:*\\\\n  * *Android:* *5,552* out of *6,675* Android DAUs had `product_name` unset\\\\u2014meaning *83.2% of our active Android users* are still running these older builds.\\\\n  * *iOS / iPadOS:* Only *163* active users had `product_name` unset, which is less than 1% of iOS traffic.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*The 30-Day Trend: A Stable Plateau*\\\\nLooking at the trend over the last month, we appear to have hit a hard plateau rather than a gradual phase-out:\\\\n\\\\n* *In early May,* the legacy cohort represented roughly 35% of overall DAUs.\\\\n* *By late May,* that share settled to around *22.1%*.\\\\n* *However, the absolute volume has remained completely flat.* For the past three weeks, the number of active users on these older builds (almost entirely *Android version 1.36.38*, which predates the super-property implementation as noted in `tracking_schema.yaml L2772`) has hovered consistently between *5,300 and 5,600 DAUs* every single day. \\\\n\\\\nThis indicates that this cohort represents highly retained, highly active users who are opening the app daily but are simply not upgrading their apps. If we were to remove the coalesce fallback today, our reported ASL Bloom DAU would appear to immediately drop by more than a quarter.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*A Minor Allocation Caveat*\\\\nA tiny sliver of these nulls (typically 10 to 25 DAUs; exactly *11* yesterday) are actually legacy *PSL Isharay* users (`product_id: 'signlab-pakistan'`).           "content": "This is fully integrated now.\n\n1. **Vault & gbrain mapping done:** Created the physical directory `/home/justin.guest/vault/sources/email/` and registered it as an active prefix for the `email` page type in your `gbrain-personal` schema.\n2. **Unified Candidate Detection:** I wrote a new script, `fetch_source_candidates.py`. \"---\\nname: obsidian-projects\\ndescription: Use when creating or recording active projects, personal or team milestones, trips, or travel plans under Notes/ with category \\\"[[Projects]]\\\".\\nversion: 1.0.0\\nauthor: Bes\\nlicense: MIT\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [obsidian, notes, projects, travel, milestones]\\n    related_skills: [obsidian, obsidian-notes]\\n---\\n\\n# Obsidian: Projects & Travel Hubs\\n\\n## Overview\\nThis skill governs notes that act as central hubs for ongoing work initiatives, travel planning, or personal family milestones.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Folder & Category\\n- **Directory:** `/home/justin.guest/vault/Notes/`\\n- **Category link:** `category: \\\"[[Projects]]\\\"`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Note Layout & Structure\\n\\nA Project or Travel note organizes timelines, tasks, objectives, and related sub-notes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n---\\nid: 'YYYYMMDDHHmmss'\\ndaily_note: \\\"[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\"\\ncategory: \\\"[[Projects]]\\\"\\n---\\n\\n# Project Name / Travel Destination\\n\\n> Executive summary or purpose of the project/trip.\\n\\n## State\\n- **Status:** Active/On Hold/Planning/Completed\\n- **Timeline:** Expected Start/End dates\\n- **People Involved:** [[Person Name]], [[Another Person]]\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83c\udfaf Objectives & Milestones\\n- List of core goals or trip milestones.\\n\\n## \ud83d\uddd2 Task List\\n- [ ] Direct, actionable task\\n- [ ] Another task\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd17 Related Notes & Resources\\n- [[Related Note 1]]\\n- [[Related Note 2]]\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Naming Rules\\n- Filename format: `Title.md` (no timestamp prefix).\\n- Use CAPITALIZED normal-spaced names for project hubs (e.g. `SignLab Free to Play Shift.md`, `Telluride 2026.md`).\\n- Never prefix with dates/timestamps.\\n\", \"path\": \"note-taking/obsidian-projects/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\":           "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"obsidian\", \"description\": \"Core settings, paths, frontmatter schemas, baseline conventions, and note triage/sorting mapping rules for Justin's Obsidian vault.\", \"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"core\", \"conventions\", \"paths\", \"frontmatter\", \"triage\", \"sorting\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian-contacts\", \"obsidian-notes\", \"obsidian-logs\", \"obsidian-utilities\"], \"content\": \"---\\nname: obsidian\\ndescription: Core settings, paths, frontmatter schemas, baseline conventions, and note triage/sorting mapping rules for Justin's Obsidian vault.\\nversion: 1.4.0\\nauthor: Bes\\nlicense: MIT\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [obsidian, core, conventions, paths, frontmatter, triage, sorting]\\n    related_skills: [obsidian-contacts, obsidian-notes, obsidian-logs, obsidian-utilities]\\n---\\n\\n# Obsidian: Core Vault Conventions & Triage Routing\\n\\n## Overview\\nThis is the foundational skill for interacting with Justin's Obsidian vault. It defines paths, general note-creation rules, frontmatter fields, standard formatting conventions, and global note-triage routing rules.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Vault Path\\n- Vault root directory: `/home/justin.guest/vault`\\n- Environmental variable: `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` set in `~/.hermes/.env` (resolves to `/home/justin.guest/vault` inside VM).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Baseline Conventions\\n\\n### Frontmatter Schema\\nEvery manual note must have a YAML frontmatter block containing at least these fields:\\n```yaml\\n---\\nid: 'YYYYMMDDHHmmss'                 # Numerical string based on creation time\\ndaily_note: \\\"[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\" # Link to creation day\\ncategory: \\\"[[CategoryName]]\\\"         # Single category link (quoted wikilink)\\n---\\n```\\n- Use single quotes `'` or double quotes `\\\"` around IDs and wikilinks to ensure YAML parsing is safe.\\n- **`aliases`** (optional): YAML list of alternative names for easy lookup.\\n- **`project`** (optional): Quoted wikilink pointing to a parent project (placed last in frontmatter).\\n\\n### Formatting Rules\\n- **Horizontal Rules:** Always use exactly three hyphens `---` on a line by itself to represent a horizontal divider. Never use two hyphens or other symbols.\\n- **Filename Conventions:**\\n  - **Daily Notes:** Must be `YYYY-MM-DD Weekday.md` (e.g. `2026-06-09 Tuesday.md`).\\n  - **Notes, Decisions, Thoughts, Memories, Sources:** Must be `ID Title.md` (e.g. `20260609120000 Spaced Title.md`).\\n  - **References, Beliefs, Projects:** Must be `Title.md` (e.g. `Spaced Title.md`).\\n  - **Contacts (People/Organizations):** Must be `Title.md` (e.g. `Aly Lalji.md`, `SignLab.md`).\\n  - **Meetings:** Must be `YYYY-MM-DD - Spaced Meeting Title.md` (e.g. `2026-06-09 - SignLab Product Alignment.md`).\\n  - Do not use kebab-case, lowercase hyphenated, or otherwise incorrect filenames. All names must be capitalized, spaced titles following these rules.\\n  - **Rationale:** As notes become less ephemeral and more authoritative (moving up the hierarchy), the date and/or ID is de-emphasized or omitted in the filename. Ephemeral, lower-tier notes are highly temporal (their place in time and sequence matters more), whereas canonical/authoritative notes (projects, people, beliefs, references) should stand out visually and contextually by title alone.\\n\\n### Git & Synchronization\\n- Do NOT run `git` commands (add, commit, push) inside the vault. The background watcher `bes-vault-sync` handles commit and synchronization to GitHub automatically within seconds of filesystem writes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Global Note-Triage Routing Table\\n\\nNotes are categorized with a single `category` YAML property containing a quoted wikilink to its category note. Based on this category, they must be sorted into the corresponding folder (matching the `Type:` folder mapping in the category's definition file):\\n\\n| Target Folder | Target Category Link | Description / Type | Sub-skill Guidance |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `Contacts/` | `category: \\\"[[People]]\\\"` | Individual contacts, friends, family, collaborators | `obsidian-people` |\\n| `Contacts/` | `category: \\\"[[Organizations]]\\\"` | Companies, schools, institutions, legal entities | `obsidian-organizations` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Notes]]\\\"` | Default category for conceptual, structured, or raw notes | `obsidian-notes` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[References]]\\\"` | Useful facts, cheat sheets, guidelines, checklists | `obsidian-references-sources` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Sources]]\\\"` | Summaries, book reviews, articles, web clips | `obsidian-references-sources` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Thoughts]]\\\"` | Personal/ideas, current opinions, research questions | `obsidian-thoughts-beliefs` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Beliefs]]\\\"` | Trusted models, core guiding principles | `obsidian-thoughts-beliefs` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Decisions]]\\\"` | Team or individual decisions and reasoning logs | `obsidian-decisions` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Memory]]\\\"` | Journal-like personal notes of things I want to remember | |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Projects]]\\\"` | Hubs for notes about ongoing work, milestones, travel | `obsidian-projects` |\\n| `Daily Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Daily Notes]]\\\"` | Daily notes containing schedules and work logs | `obsidian-daily-notes` |\\n| `Logs/` | `category: \\\"[[Meetings]]\\\"` | Chronological meeting agendas, summaries, outcomes | `obsidian-meetings` |\\n| `Logs/` | `category: \\\"[[Readwise]]\\\"` | Raw logs of what I've been reading (plus highlights) | |\\n| `Utilities/` | `category: \\\"[[Categories]]\\\"` | Category representation notes themselves (in `Utilities/Categories/`) | `obsidian-utilities` |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Action Steps for Triage\\n1. **Determine Category:** Read note content and title. Map it to exactly one category above. Convert legacy inline tags (e.g. `#meeting`) to the property and remove from body.\\n2. **Update YAML:** Set `category: \\\"[[<CategoryName>]]\\\"` using targeted `patch`.\\n3. **Move File:** Relocate the file from `Inbox/` to its corresponding target folder. Create folders with `mkdir -p` if missing.\\n\", \"path\": \"note-taking/obsidian/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/note-taking/obsidian\", \"linked_files\": {\"references\": [\"references/vault-audit-2026-05-22.md\", \"references/vault-hygiene-design.md\", \"references/obsidian-flavored-markdown.md\"], \"scripts\": [\"scripts/rename_kebab_notes.py\", \"scripts/reconcile_granola.py\", \"scripts/migrate_notes.py\", \"scripts/migrate_logseq_json.py\"]}, \"usage_hint\": \"To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'\", \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\", \"metadata\": {\"hermes\": {\"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"core\", \"conventions\", \"paths\", \"frontmatter\", \"triage\", \"sorting\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian-contacts\", \"obsidian-notes\", \"obsidian-logs\", \"obsidian-utilities\"]}}}",

          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"content\": \"1|#!/usr/bin/env python3\\n2|\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3|vault_hygiene.py \u2014 Obsidian vault hygiene check + auto-fix.\\n4|Leverages `gbrain lint --fix` for content fixes and misplaced file moving,\\n5|and runs local checks for ID conflicts, missing IDs, and missing daily notes.\\n6|\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n7|\\n8|import os\\n9|import re\\n10|import subprocess\\n11|import datetime\\n12|from collections import defaultdict\\n13|from pathlib import Path\\n14|\\n15|VAULT = Path(\\\"/home/justin.guest/vault\\\")\\n16|\\n17|def reconcile_granola_meetings(vault_path):\\n18|    meetings_dir = Path(vault_path) / \\\"meetings\\\"\\n19|    meetings_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n20|    \\n21|    dest_dir = Path(vault_path) / \\\"Logs\\\" / \\\"Meetings\\\"\\n22|    dest_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n23|        \\n24|    print(\\\"Reconciling meetings from Granola...\\\")\\n25|    \\n26|    # Pre-scan vault for existing IDs to avoid collisions\\n27|    existing_ids = set()\\n28|    skip_dirs = {\\\"Readwise\\\", \\\"Utilities\\\", \\\".git\\\", \\\".trash\\\", \\\".cursor\\\", \\\".claude\\\", \\\"sources\\\", \\\"Daily Notes\\\"}\\n29|    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(vault_path):\\n30|        dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not d.startswith(\\\".\\\") and d not in skip_dirs]\\n31|        for f in files:\\n32|            if not f.endswith(\\\".md\\\"):\\n33|                continue\\n34|            p = Path(root) / f\\n35|            try:\\n36|                content = p.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\", errors=\\\"replace\\\")\\n37|                if content.startswith(\\\"---\\\"):\\n38|                    end = content.find(\\\"\\\\n---\\\", 3)\\n39|                    if end > 0:\\n40|                        fm = content[3:end]\\n41|                        id_match = re.search(r\\\"^id:\\\\s*[\\\\\\\"']?(\\\\d+)[\\\\\\\"']?\\\", fm, re.MULTILINE)\\n42|                        if id_match:\\n43|                            existing_ids.add(id_match.group(1).strip())\\n44|            except Exception:\\n45|                pass\\n46|\\n47|    for src_dir, is_raw in [(meetings_dir, True), (dest_dir, False)]:\\n48|        for path in sorted(src_dir.glob(\\\"*.md\\\")):\\n49|            filename = path.name\\n50|            # Match YYYY-MM-DD at start of filename\\n51|            date_match = re.match(r\\\"^(\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2})\\\", filename)\\n52|            if not date_match:\\n53|                continue\\n54|                \\n55|            date_str = date_match.group(1)\\n56|            try:\\n57|                dt = datetime.date.fromisoformat(date_str)\\n58|                weekday_lower = dt.strftime(\\\"%A\\\").lower()\\n59|                weekday_cap = dt.strftime(\\\"%A\\\")\\n60|            except ValueError:\\n61|                continue\\n62|                \\n63|            text = path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\", errors=\\\"replace\\\")\\n64|            \\n65|            # Check if the file has standard frontmatter\\n66|            has_frontmatter = False\\n67|            fm_content = \\\"\\\"\\n68|            body_content = text\\n69|            \\n70|            if text.startswith(\\\"---\\\"):\\n71|                fm_end = text.find(\\\"\\\\n---\\\", 3)\\n72|                if fm_end > 0:\\n73|                    has_frontmatter = True\\n74|                    fm_content = text[3:fm_end]\\n75|                    body_content = text[fm_end+4:]\\n76|                    \\n77|            # Parse fields from frontmatter if it exists\\n78|            note_id = \\\"\\\"\\n79|            daily_note = \\\"\\\"\\n80|            \\n81|            if has_frontmatter:\\n82|                id_match = re.search(r\\\"^id:\\\\s*[\\\\\\\"']?(\\\\d+)[\\\\\\\"']?\\\", fm_content, re.MULTILINE)\\n83|                dn_match = re.search(r\\\"^daily_note:\\\\s*[\\\\\\\"']?([^\\\\\\\"'\\\\n]+)[\\\\\\\"']?\\\", fm_content, re.MULTILINE)\\n84|                if id_match:\\n85|                    note_id = id_match.group(1).strip()\\n86|                if dn_match:\\n87|                    daily_note = dn_match.group(1).strip()\\n88|                    \\n89|            needs_update = False\\n90|            \\n91|            if not note_id:\\n92|                mtime = path.stat().st_mtime\\n93|                mtime_dt = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(mtime)\\n94|                if mtime_dt.strftime(\\\"%Y-%m-%d\\\") == date_str:\\n95|                    time_part = mtime_dt.strftime(\\\"%H%M%S\\\")\\n96|                else:\\n97|                    time_part = \\\"120000\\\"\\n98|                    \\n99|                candidate_id = f\\\"{date_str.replace('-', '')}{time_part}\\\"\\n100|                while candidate_id in existing_ids:\\n101|\", \"total_lines\": 217, \"file_size\": 8549, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=101 to continue reading (showing 1-100 of 217 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

          "content": "{\"content\": \"101|                    # Increment the ID to make it unique\\n102|                    candidate_id = str(int(candidate_id) + 1)\\n103|                \\n104|                note_id = candidate_id\\n105|                existing_ids.add(note_id)\\n106|                needs_update = True\\n107|                \\n108|            if not daily_note or \\\"[[\\\" not in daily_note:\\n109|                daily_note = f\\\"[[Daily Notes/{date_str}-{weekday_lower}|{date_str} {weekday_cap}]]\\\"\\n110|                needs_update = True\\n111|                \\n112|            # Clean body: strip leading whitespace and redundant separators\\n113|            original_body = body_content\\n114|            body_content = body_content.lstrip()\\n115|            \\n116|            # Clean up double hyphens / horizontal rules at top of body (Granola artifact)\\n117|            if body_content.startswith(\\\"--\\\\n\\\") or body_content.startswith(\\\"--\\\\r\\\\n\\\"):\\n118|                body_content = body_content.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\", 1)[1].lstrip()\\n119|                needs_update = True\\n120|            elif body_content.startswith(\\\"-- \\\") or body_content.startswith(\\\"--\\\\t\\\"):\\n121|                body_content = body_content.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\", 1)[1].lstrip()\\n122|                needs_update = True\\n123|            elif body_content.startswith(\\\"---\\\\n\\\") or body_content.startswith(\\\"---\\\\r\\\\n\\\"):\\n124|                body_content = body_content.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\", 1)[1].lstrip()\\n125|                needs_update = True\\n126|                \\n127|            if body_content != original_body:\\n128|                needs_update = True\\n129|                \\n130|            if is_raw or needs_update:\\n131|                # Construct clean frontmatter and write to dest_dir\\n132|                new_fm = f\\\"---\\\\nid: {note_id}\\\\ndaily_note: '{daily_note}'\\\\n---\\\\n\\\"\\n133|                new_text = new_fm + body_content\\n134|                dest_path = dest_dir / filename\\n135|                dest_path.write_text(new_text, encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n136|                \\n137|                if needs_update:\\n138|                    print(f\\\"  Fixed/Reconciled: {filename}\\\")\\n139|                else:\\n140|                    print(f\\\"  Processed and moved: {filename}\\\")\\n141|                    \\n142|                if is_raw:\\n143|                    # Delete original file\\n144|                    try:\\n145|                        path.unlink()\\n146|                    except Exception as e:\\n147|                        print(f\\\"  Error deleting original raw file {filename}: {e}\\\")\\n148|\\n149|# 1. Reconcile raw Granola meetings from external syncing\\n150|reconcile_granola_meetings(VAULT)\\n151|\\n152|# 2. Walk the vault to detect ID conflicts, missing ID, missing daily_note\\n153|id_to_paths = defaultdict(list)\\n154|missing_ids = []\\n155|missing_daily_notes = []\\n156|\\n157|# Folders to skip for manual checks\\n158|skip_dirs = {\\\"Readwise\\\", \\\"Utilities\\\", \\\".git\\\", \\\".trash\\\", \\\".cursor\\\", \\\".claude\\\", \\\"sources\\\", \\\"Daily Notes\\\"}\\n159|\\n160|for root, dirs, files in os.walk(VAULT):\\n161|    dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not d.startswith(\\\".\\\") and d not in skip_dirs]\\n162|    for f in sorted(files):\\n163|        if not f.endswith(\\\".md\\\") or f == \\\"RESOLVER.md\\\":\\n164|            continue\\n165|        path = Path(root) / f\\n166|        text = path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\", errors=\\\"replace\\\")\\n167|        \\n168|        # Parse frontmatter\\n169|        note_id = \\\"\\\"\\n170|        daily_note = \\\"\\\"\\n171|        if text.startswith(\\\"---\\\"):\\n172|            end = text.find(\\\"\\\\n---\\\", 3)\\n173|            if end > 0:\\n174|                fm_raw = text[3:end]\\n175|                id_match = re.search(r\\\"^id:\\\\s*[\\\\\\\"']?(\\\\d+)[\\\\\\\"']?\\\", fm_raw, re.MULTILINE)\\n176|                dn_match = re.search(r\\\"^daily_note:\\\\s*[\\\\\\\"']?([^\\\\\\\"'\\\\n]+)[\\\\\\\"']?\\\", fm_raw, re.MULTILINE)\\n177|                if id_match:\\n178|                    note_id = id_match.group(1).strip()\\n179|                if dn_match:\\n180|                    daily_note = dn_match.group(1).strip()\\n181|        \\n182|        rel_path = path.relative_to(VAULT)\\n183|        if note_id:\\n184|            id_to_paths[note_id].append(rel_path)\\n185|        else:\\n186|            missing_ids.append(rel_path)\\n187|            \\n188|        if not daily_note or \\\"[[\\\" not in daily_note:\\n189|            missing_daily_notes.append(rel_path)\\n190|\\n191|# 3. Format output for vault_hygiene_cron.py\\n192|lines = []\\n193|\\n194|# ID Conflicts\\n195|id_conflicts = {nid: paths for nid, paths in id_to_paths.items() if len(paths) > 1}\\n196|if id_conflicts:\\n197|    lines.append(\\\"## \ud83d\udd34 ID conflicts\\\")\\n198|    for nid, paths in sorted(id_conflicts.items()):\\n199|        for p in paths:\\n200|            lines.append(f\\\"  - {p}: id={nid} shared by {len(paths)} notes\\\")\\n201|\", \"total_lines\": 217, \"file_size\": 8549, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=201 to continue reading (showing 101-200 of 217 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

        {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"obsidian-categorize-and-sort\", \"description\": \"Use when triaging, organizing, or sorting a new or incoming note (e.g. in Inbox/) by determining its category, formatting its template via sub-skills, and moving it to its correct directory.\", \"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"operations\", \"triage\", \"sorting\", \"taxonomy\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian\", \"obsidian-people\", \"obsidian-organizations\", \"obsidian-daily-notes\", \"obsidian-meetings\", \"obsidian-decisions\", \"obsidian-thoughts-beliefs\", \"obsidian-projects\", \"obsidian-references-sources\"], \"content\": \"---\\nname: obsidian-categorize-and-sort\\ndescription: Use when triaging, organizing, or sorting a new or incoming note (e.g. in Inbox/) by determining its category, formatting its template via sub-skills, and moving it to its correct directory.\\nversion: 1.3.0\\nauthor: Bes\\nlicense: MIT\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [obsidian, operations, triage, sorting, taxonomy]\\n    related_skills: [obsidian, obsidian-people, obsidian-organizations, obsidian-daily-notes, obsidian-meetings, obsidian-decisions, obsidian-thoughts-beliefs, obsidian-projects, obsidian-references-sources]\\n---\\n\\n# Obsidian Operation: Categorize and Sort Notes\\n\\n## Overview\\nThis operational skill handles the workflow of triaging new, raw, or incoming notes. It guides determining the category, formatting the note structure using category-specific sub-skills, and moving the file to its designated physical folder.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## The Triage Process\\n\\n### Step 1 \u2014 Determine the Category\\nRead the note content and title. Map the note to **exactly one** category from the table below. Convert legacy inline tags (e.g. `#people`) to formal category properties.\\n\\n| Category Link | Destination Folder | Category-Level Formatting Sub-skill |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `category: \\\"[[People]]\\\"` | `Contacts/` | **`obsidian-people`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Organizations]]\\\"` | `Contacts/` | **`obsidian-organizations`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Notes]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-notes`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[References]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-references-sources`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Sources]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-references-sources`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Thoughts]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-thoughts-beliefs`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Beliefs]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-thoughts-beliefs`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Decisions]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-decisions`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Projects]]\\\"` | `Notes/` | **`obsidian-projects`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Daily Notes]]\\\"` | `Daily Notes/` | **`obsidian-daily-notes`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Meetings]]\\\"` | `Logs/Meetings/` | **`obsidian-meetings`** |\\n| `category: \\\"[[Categories]]\\\"` | `Utilities/Categories/` | **`obsidian-utilities`** |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2 \u2014 Format the Content (Sub-Skill Handoff)\\nOnce the category is identified:\\n1. Ensure the note has standard frontmatter (`id`, `daily_note`, `category`).\\n2. Load the corresponding category-specific sub-skill (e.g. `obsidian-people` for a biography note, or `obsidian-decisions` for a choice log).\\n3. Format or scaffold the note body according to that category's standard template.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3 \u2014 Move the File to its Target Folder\\nIdentify the target folder from the table above. Move the file from its source folder (such as `Inbox/`) to the target destination folder.\\n```bash\\nmv \\\"/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/Some Note.md\\\" \\\"/home/justin.guest/vault/Notes/Some Note.md\\\"\\n```\\n*Note: Always verify or create the target directory using `mkdir -p` before moving.*\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Classification Guidelines & Ambiguities\\n\\nTo avoid incorrect or naive categorization, always differentiate between the following note types:\\n\\n### 1. Conceptual/Methodology Notes vs. Logs\\n* **Linguistic Overlap:** Notes discussing \\\"meetings,\\\" \\\"interviews,\\\" \\\"syncs,\\\" or \\\"conversations\\\" as general methodologies or abstract concepts (e.g., *Zettelkasten for product development*, *Continuous interviewing*) must stay in `/Notes/` under `category: \\\"[[Notes]]\\\"`. Do not naively sort them into `/Logs/Meetings/` just because their title matches keywords.\\n* **Meeting Prep/Agendas:** Personal talking points or draft agendas written *prior* to a meeting represent personal planning files and should reside in `/Notes/` (as `[[Notes]]` or `[[Memory]]`). Only actual collaborative records of conversations/outcomes belong in `/Logs/Meetings/` as `[[Meetings]]`.\\n\\n### 2. Interaction Logs vs. Contact Profiles\\n* **Interaction Logs:** A quick note recording a specific interaction (e.g., \\\"Emailed Andrew Novak about room design\\\") contains a person's name in the title but is transactional and belongs in `/Notes/` or `/Memory/` (under `category: \\\"[[Notes]]\\\"` or `category: \\\"[[Memory]]\\\"`).\\n* **Contact Profiles:** Only actual profiles, bios, or contact directories (e.g., `/Contacts/Andrew novak.md`) belong in `/Contacts/` under `category: \\\"[[People]]\\\"`.\\n* *Exception:* Contact details imported or synced into `/sources/` from external systems should be migrated to `/Contacts/` under `category: \\\"[[People]]\\\"`.\\n\\n### 3. Subjective Thoughts vs. Objective Concepts\\n* **Personal Reflections:** Notes containing opinions, subjective philosophies, or career decision logs (e.g., *Leave raptor*, *Thoughts on fatphobia*) should be categorized as `[[Thoughts]]` or `[[Beliefs]]` (using sub-skill `obsidian-thoughts-beliefs`), even if their legacy metadata includes `type: concept`.\\n\\n### 4. Guides/Checklists vs. Default Notes\\n* **Guides and Handbooks:** Technical procedures, step-by-step migration guides, or checklists (e.g., *Mixpanel to Posthog migration guide*) should be categorized as `[[References]]` (using sub-skill `obsidian-references-sources`) rather than the default `[[Notes]]`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Common Pitfalls\\n## Common Pitfalls\\n- **Multiple Categories:** Never assign multiple categories to a single note. Every note gets **exactly one** category.\\n- **Leaking Inbox:** Do not leave notes in `Inbox/` once categorized. All processed notes must reside in their taxonomy folders.\\n- **Ephemeral Project Guides as References:** Do not categorize temporary checklists or migration guides tied to a specific project as `[[References]]`. References are reserved for permanent, long-term lookup documents. Project-bound checklists/guides belong under `[[Notes]]` or `[[Projects]]`.\\n- **Links Verification:** moves are generally safe since Obsidian auto-resolves links by note name regardless of folder structure.\\n\", \"path\": \"note-taking/obsidian-categorize-and-sort/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/note-taking/obsidian-categorize-and-sort\", \"linked_files\": null, \"usage_hint\": null, \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\", \"metadata\": {\"hermes\": {\"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"operations\", \"triage\", \"sorting\", \"taxonomy\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian\", \"obsidian-people\", \"obsidian-organizations\", \"obsidian-daily-notes\", \"obsidian-meetings\", \"obsidian-decisions\", \"obsidian-thoughts-beliefs\", \"obsidian-projects\", \"obsidian-references-sources\"]}}}",

Timeline\\n121|            tail = content[idx:]\\n122|            \\n123|            # Find all bullets under ## Timeline\\n124|            lines = tail.split('\\\\n')\\n125|            bullets = []\\n126|            other_lines = []\\n127|            reached_non_timeline = False\\n128|            \\n129|            for line in lines:\\n130|                striped = line.strip()\\n131|                if reached_non_timeline:\\n132|                    other_lines.append(line)\\n133|                elif striped.startswith('-'):\\n134|                    bullets.append(line)\\n135|                elif striped == '':\\n136|                    continue # skip extra blank lines under Timeline\\n137|                elif striped.startswith('---') or striped.startswith('#'):\\n138|                    # Reached divider or another heading, exit timeline section\\n139|                    reached_non_timeline = True\\n140|                    other_lines.append(line)\\n141|                else:\\n142|                    # Some other non-bullet content, means we exited the timeline section\\n143|                    reached_non_timeline = True\\n144|                    other_lines.append(line)\\n145|                    \\n146|            # Add the new bullet at the top of the bullets list (reverse chronological)\\n147|            new_bullet = f\\\"- {event_date} | Mentioned in [[{slug}|{source_title}]]\\\"\\n148|            bullets.insert(0, new_bullet)\\n149|            \\n150|\", \"total_lines\": 378, \"file_size\": 15193, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=150 to continue reading (showing 100-149 of 378 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"content\": \"150|            # Reconstruct tail: one blank line after ## Timeline, then bullets, then one blank line, then the rest\\n151|            new_tail_parts = []\\n152|            new_tail_parts.append(\\\"\\\") # exactly one empty line after heading\\n153|            for b in bullets:\\n154|                new_tail_parts.append(b)\\n155|                \\n156|            if other_lines:\\n157|                new_tail_parts.append(\\\"\\\")\\n158|                new_tail_parts.extend(other_lines)\\n159|            else:\\n160|                new_tail_parts.append(\\\"\\\") # trailing newline\\n161|                \\n162|            content = content[:idx] + '\\\\n'.join(new_tail_parts)\\n163|            \\n164|        with open(entity_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\\n165|            f.write(content)\\n166|        return True\\n167|    except Exception as e:\\n168|        print(f\\\"Error enriching {entity_path}: {e}\\\")\\n169|        return False\\n170|\\n171|def scan_file_for_signals(file_path, content, entities, vault_path, event_date, enriched_counts):\\n172|    rel_path = os.path.relpath(file_path, vault_path)\\n173|    file_title = os.path.basename(file_path)[:-3]\\n174|    \\n175|    # Don't enrich the file with itself\\n176|    file_key = file_title.lower()\\n177|    \\n178|    # Scanned content to search\\n179|    scanned_text = content.lower()\\n180|\", \"total_lines\": 378, \"file_size\": 15193, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=180 to continue reading (showing 150-179 of 378 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

The `add_timeline_entry` function was inserting a hardcoded `\\n` at the start of each new bullet string, which caused redundant blank lines to accumulate after joining.\n\nI have completed the following:\n\n1. **Fixed the Script Logic:** Patched both `/home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/check_vault_signals.py` and its backup copy. The function now cleanly processes the timeline block:\n   * Strips all duplicate and trailing blank lines immediately below the `## Timeline` header.\n   * Restructures the list with exactly one blank line after the heading and zero blank lines between the individual bullets.\n2. **Cleaned Existing Vault Notes:** Ran a sweep across the entire vault to fix already-affected files. Spacing was successfully restored and compacted under `## Timeline` in 10 project and travel notes (including `Bes setup.md`, `Emerald Isle 2026.md`, and `B2c expansion strategy.md`).",
