    break\\n195|            if has_brain:\\n196|                break\\n197|                \\n198|        if has_brain:\\n199|            continue\\n200|            \\n201|        # Resolve all users in messages\\n202|        resolve_users_in_messages(client, messages)\\n203|        \\n204|        # Clean messages\\n205|        cleaned_messages = [clean_message(m, client) for m in messages]\\n206| sys.exit(1)\\n243|        \\n244|    new_brains = []\\n245|    for item in res.get(\\\"items\\\", []):\\n246|        if item.get(\\\"type\\\") != \\\"message\\\":\\n247|            continue\\n248|           "role": "assistant",
          "content": "I have updated your Slack ingestion pipeline to support your custom emojis and landing zone preferences.\n\nHere is a summary of what has been re-architected and updated:\n\n### 1. Script Updates (`fetch_slack_brains.py`)\n* Patched the polling script (both live and backup copies) to scan for custom Slack reactions:\n  * **`:jg_log:`** (Slack API name: `\"jg_log\"`)\n  * **`:jg_decision:`** (Slack API name: `\"jg_decision\"`)\n* The script now injects a `\"reaction_type\"` property in the returned payloads to differentiate standard logs from formal decisions.\n\n### 2. Cron Job Update (`Slack Brain Note Capture`)\nUpdated your scheduled cron job **`8d6cfdd42fd4`** with an upgraded prompt configuration that dynamically routes the detected items:\n* **Inbox-First Rule:** Every new ingestion is now created inside your landing zone: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/`.\n* **Path A (`:jg_log:`):**\n  * **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title].md`\n  * **Category:** `\"[[Slack]]\"`\n  * **Layout:** Restructured to write a super-short, minimalist note containing only frontmatter metadata, a pointer URL, and a brief 1\u20132 sentence `## Topic Description` placeholder. **No point-by-point summaries, thoughts, or decisions are allowed in the file body.**\n* **Path B (`:jg_decision:`):**\n  * **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title].md`\n  * **Category:** `\"[[Decisions]]\"`\n  * **Layout:** Automatically drafts a structured Decision note with `## Context`, `## Options & Trade-offs` (extracting pros/cons from the Slack thread), and `## Resolution`.\n* **Daily Note Notepad Integration:** Appends the pointer as `* [[Inbox/<filename>|Slack summary]]` or `* [[Inbox/<filename>|Decision]]` to your daily notepad.\n\n### 3. Skill Documentation & Alignment\n* **`bes-slack-ingest`:** Upgraded to specify the minimalist pointer body layout, custom emojis, and the creation-to-enrichment lifecycle.\n* **`obsidian-vault-jam`:** Updated Phase 1 enrichment to scan your `/Inbox/` for new Slack logs since the last run. It instructs Bes to read the original Slack threads, update/verify participants, and expand the pointers into 2\u20133 sentence Topic Descriptions while enforcing the strict \"no-quotes/no-decisions-in-log\" rule.\n* **`obsidian-graph-enrichment`:** Added a dedicated section detailing the new **Slack Ingestion & Inbox-First Landing** rules.\n\nYour next automated capture run will pick up these reactions and land them cleanly inside your `Inbox/` as lightweight, high-signal pointers!",

The Integration Passes\\n\\nRather than letting notes sit in isolation, we run structured passes to compile knowledge programmatically.\\n\\n* integrate-light: Triggered after any explicit ingest. Appends one line to Utilities/log.md with timestamps and daily-note wikilinks. Keeps inputs immutable.\\n* integrate-entities: Runs alongside integrate-light. Appends timelines, decisions, and cross-references directly to existing contact (Contacts/) and project (Notes/Projects/) hubs.\\n* integrate-full: Your Wind-Down Step 5 or manual trigger. Promotes raw Readings into Layer 2 Sources and updates project contexts.\\n* integrate-query: Active research loop. If you ask a question that draws on 3+ notes, connects cross-project insights, or is asked twice, I compose the synthesis, file it under Notes/ (usually as a [[Thought]] or [[Concept]]), and append the activity to your log.\\n\\n---\\n\\n3. Scans for orphan pages, link chains, stale summaries, and potential logical contradictions across your Beliefs and Thoughts. client.users_info(user=user_id)\\n65|        name = pick_user_display(r[\\\"user\\\"].get(\\\"profile\\\", {}), user_id)\\n66|        _USER_CACHE[user_id] = name\\n67|        return name\\n68|    except SlackApiError:\\n69|        _USER_CACHE[user_id] = user_id\\n70|        return user_id\\n71|\\n72|def resolve_users_in_messages(client, messages):\\n73|    uids = set()\\n74|    for m in messages:\\n75|        if m.get(\\\"user\\\"):\\n76|            uids.add(m[\\\"user\\\"])\\n77|        for mt in re.findall(r\\\"<@(U[A-Z0-9]+)>\\\", m.get(\\\"text\\\") or \\\"\\\"):\\n78|            uids.add(mt)\\n79|    for uid in uids:\\n80|        resolve_user(client, uid)\\n81|\\n82|def clean_message(m, client):\\n83|    user_id = m.get(\\\"user\\\") or m.get(\\\"bot_id\\\") or \\\"?\\\"\\n84|    user_name = resolve_user(client, user_id)\\n85|    text = m.get(\\\"text\\\") or \\\"\\\"\\n86|    \\n87|    # Resolve inline user mentions\\n88|    def _sub(match):\\n89|        uid = match.group(1)\\n90|        return f\\\"@{resolve_user(client, uid)}\\\"\\n91|    text = re.sub(r\\\"<@(U[A-Z0-9]+)>\\\", _sub, text)\\n92|    \\n93|    return {\\n94|        \\\"ts\\\": client.conversations_history(channel=channel_id, latest=ts, limit=1, inclusive=True)\\n115|        msgs = res.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n116|        if msgs:\\n117|            m = msgs[0]\\n118|            if m.get(\\\"thread_ts\\\"):\\n119|                return m.get(\\\"thread_ts\\\")\\n120|            return ts\\n121|    except Exception:\\n122|        pass\\n123|    return ts\\n124|\\n125|def {f\\\"{p['channel']}:{p['ts']}\\\" for p in processed}\\n130|    \\n131|    # Check last 36 hours for candidate conversations\\n132|    start_date = (dt.date.today() - dt.timedelta(days=2)).strftime(\\\"%Y-%m-%d\\\")\\n133|    \\n134|    # 1. Search messages from Justin\\n135|    try:\\n136|        res_from = client.search_messages(query=f\\\"from:me after:{start_date}\\\", count=30)\\n137|        matches_from = res_from.get(\\\"messages\\\", {}).get(\\\"matches\\\", [])\\n138|    except Exception:\\n139|        matches_from = []\\n140|        \\n141|    # 2. Search mentions of Justin\\n142|    try:\\n143|        res_ment = client.search_messages(query=f\\\"<@{user_id}> after:{start_date}\\\", count=30)\\n144|        matches_ment = res_ment.get(\\\"messages\\\", {}).get(\\\"matches\\\", [])\\n145|    except Exception:\\n146|        matches_ment = []\\n147|        \\n148|    all_matches = matches_from + matches_ment\\n149|    \\n150|    # Find unique (channel_id, parent_ts) threads\\n151|    unique_conversations = set()\\n152|    for m in all_matches:\\n153|        channel_id = (m.get(\\\"channel\\\") or {}).get(\\\"id\\\")\\n154|        ts = m.get(\\\"ts\\\")\\n155|        if channel_id and ts:\\n156|            unique_conversations.add((channel_id, ts))\\n157|            \\n158|    unique_threads = set()\\n159|    for channel_id, ts in unique_conversations:\\n160|        parent_ts = get_parent_ts(client, channel_id, ts)\\n161|        unique_threads.add((channel_id, parent_ts))\\n162|        \\n163|    candidates = []\\n164|    for channel_id, parent_ts in unique_threads:\\n165|        key = f\\\"{channel_id}:{parent_ts}\\\"\\n166|        if key in processed_set:\\n167|            continue\\n168|            \\n169|        try:\\n170|            r = client.conversations_replies(channel=channel_id, ts=parent_ts, limit=100)\\n171|            messages = r.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n172|        except SlackApiError:\\n173|            continue\\n174|            \\n175|        if len(messages) < 3:\\n176|            continue\\n177|            \\n178|        # Verify Justin participated\\n179|        participated = any(m.get(\\\"user\\\") == user_id for m in messages)\\n180|        if not participated:\\n181|            continue\\n182|            \\n183|        # Verify if Justin added a obsidian-jg reaction to any message in the thread\\n189|        has_brain = False\\n190|        for m in messages:\\n191|            for rxn in m.get(\\\"reactions\\\", []):\\n192|                if rxn.get(\\\"name\\\") in [\\\"obsidian-jg\\\"] and user_id in rxn.get(\\\"users\\\", []):\\n193|                    has_brain = True\\n194|                    break\\n195|            if has_brain:\\n196|                break\\n197|                \\n198|        if has_brain:\\n199|            continue\\n200|            \\n201|        # Resolve all users in messages\\n202|        resolve_users_in_messages(client, messages)\\n203|        \\n204|        # Clean messages\\n205|        cleaned_messages = [clean_message(m, client) for m in messages]\\n206|        \\n207|        # Get permalink\\n208|        permalink = None\\n209|        try:\\n210|            pl = client.chat_getPermalink(channel=channel_id, message_ts=parent_ts)\\n211|            permalink = pl.get(\\\"permalink\\\")\\n212|        except SlackApiError:\\n213|            permalink = f\\\"https://signlab.slack.com/archives/{channel_id}/p{parent_ts.replace('.', '')}\\\"\\n214|            \\n215|        channel_name = get_channel_name(client, channel_id)\\n216|        \\n217|        participants_names = list(set(m[\\\"user_name\\\"] for m in cleaned_messages if user_id = client.auth_test()[\\\"user_id\\\"]\\n235|    processed = load_processed()\\n236|    processed_set = {f\\\"{p['channel']}:{p['ts']}\\\" for p in processed}\\n237|    \\n238|    try:\\n239|        res = client.reactions_list(user=user_id, limit=30)\\n240|    except SlackApiError as e:\\n241|        print(f\\\"Error fetching reactions list: {e}\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\n242|        sys.exit(1)\\n243|        \\n244|    new_brains = []\\n245|    for item in res.get(\\\"items\\\", []):\\n246|        if item.get(\\\"type\\\") != \\\"message\\\":\\n247|            continue\\n248|        channel_id = item.get(\\\"channel\\\")\\n249|        msg = item.get(\\\"message\\\", {})\\n250|        ts = msg.get(\\\"ts\\\")\\n251|        \\n252|        # Check if already processed\\n253| reaction_type = None\\n259|        for rxn in msg.get(\\\"reactions\\\", []):\\n260|            if rxn.get(\\\"name\\\") in [\\\"obsidian-jg\\\"] and user_id in rxn.get(\\\"users\\\", []):\\n261| thread\\n274|            try:\\n275|                r = client.conversations_replies(channel=channel_id, ts=thread_ts, limit=100)\\n276|                messages = r.get(\\\"messages\\\", [])\\n277|      # Fall back to single message if thread reading fails\\n281|                messages = [msg]\\n282|        else:\\n283|            # Fetch surrounding context (5 before, 5 after)\\n284|           "content": "{\"content\": \"1|# Cron Job: Slack Brain Note Capture\\n2|\\n3|**Job ID:** 8d6cfdd42fd4\\n4|**Run Time:** 2026-06-11 05:24:10\\n5|**Schedule:** every 120m\\n6|\\n7|## Prompt\\n8|\\n9|[IMPORTANT: The user has invoked the \\\"llm-wiki\\\" skill, indicating they want you to follow its instructions. The full skill content is loaded below.]\\n10|\\n11|---\\n12|name: llm-wiki\\n13|description: Use when ingesting inputs into the vault, compiling Readings into Source notes, integrating knowledge across pages, running wiki health checks, or filing durable query answers. Orchestrates Karpathy-style compounding wiki maintenance across Inputs / Sources / note-maturity tiers.\\n14|version: 1.0.0\\n15|author: Bes\\n16|license: MIT\\n17|metadata:\\n18|  hermes:\\n19|    tags: [wiki, knowledge, integration, sources, readings, compile]\\n20|    related_skills: [obsidian, obsidian-hygiene, work-log, bes-email-dispatch, wind-down, morning-briefing, did-i-already-do-this]\\n21|---\\n22|\\n23|# llm-wiki \u2014 Compounding Vault Knowledge\\n24|\\n25|## Overview\\n26|\\n27|Orchestrates Karpathy-style wiki maintenance across Justin's three-layer taxonomy: immutable **Inputs**, compiled bibliographical **Sources**, and the unchanged **maturity ladder** (Notes\u2192Thoughts\u2192Concepts\u2192Beliefs\u2192References). Hybrid integration: light log append after ingest, full compile on demand, query synthesis for durable answers.\\n28|\\n29|Deep reference: [architecture](references/architecture.md) | [integrate-light](references/integrate-light.md) | [integrate-entities](references/integrate-entities.md) | [integrate-full](references/integrate-full.md) | [integrate-query](references/integrate-query.md) | [lint](references/lint.md) | [index-and-log](references/index-and-log.md) | [taxonomy-migration](references/taxonomy-migration.md)\\n30|\\n31|## When to Use\\n32|\\n33|- After explicit ingest (email dispatch, Slack brain, Readwise, Granola reconcile)\\n34|- \\\"integrate this\\\", \\\"wiki integrate today\\\", \\\"compile today's ingests\\\"\\n35|- Wind-down EIIRP Step 5 (integrate-full)\\n36|- Research Q&A that should persist in the vault (integrate-query): \\\"what do my notes say about\u2026\\\", \\\"compare X and Y\\\", \\\"synthesize everything on Z\\\"\\n37|- \\\"run wiki lint\\\" (semantic; structural \u2192 obsidian-hygiene)\\n38|\\n39|**Don't use for:** daily work logs (work-log), structural hygiene (obsidian-hygiene), maturity promotion (obsidian-suggest-promotions \u2014 **unchanged**).\\n40|\\n41|## Vault Path\\n42|\\n43|`OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` from `~/.hermes/.env`. Input paths use `Inputs/` (legacy `Logs/` accepted during migration).\\n44|\\n45|## Three Layers\\n46|\\n47|| Layer | Folder | Category | Immutable? | Bes edits body? |\\n48||-------|--------|----------|------------|-----------------|\\n49|| 1 Inputs | `Inputs/Readings/` etc. | `[[Readings]]`, `[[Meetings]]`, `[[Emails]]`, `[[Slack]]` | Yes | Metadata only |\\n50|| 2 Compiled | `Notes/` | `[[Sources]]` | No | Yes (summary) |\\n51|| 3 Maturity | `Notes/` (+ subfolders) | Notes, Thoughts, Concepts, Beliefs, References, Decisions, Memories, Projects | No | Yes |\\n52|\\n53|**Layer 3 ladder is unchanged.** Promotion Notes\u2192Thoughts\u2192Beliefs stays in `obsidian-suggest-promotions`. llm-wiki does not auto-promote maturity tiers.\\n54|\\n55|**Link direction:** Concept \u2192 Source \u2192 Reading. Never Concept \u2192 Reading when Source exists.\\n56|\\n57|## Integration Passes\\n58|\\n59|| Pass | When | Actions |\\n60||------|------|---------|\\n61|| **integrate-light** | Every explicit ingest; cron post-steps | Append `Utilities/log.md` with daily note wikilink. **Never modify Input bodies.** No index or notepad updates. |\\n62|| **integrate-entities** | After integrate-light; meeting reconcile | Update existing projects' State sections on decisions/status-changes only. Timelines are disabled. Update-only \u2014 no stub creation. |\\n63|| **integrate-full** | Wind-down Step 5; manual triggers | Reading\u2192Source promotion, project/contact cross-refs, contradiction flags |\\n64|| **integrate-query** | Interactive durable Q&A | Search \u2192 synthesize \u2192 write to `Inbox/` when durability met (3+ notes, comparison/decision analysis, cross-project connection, explicit \\\"file this\\\", same topic twice) \u2192 print complete note in channel, log append. See [integrate-query.md](references/integrate-query.md). Template: [query-synthesis.md](templates/query-synthesis.md). |\\n65|\\n66|Cron runs **integrate-light + integrate-entities**. No auto-vault from raw streams.\\n67|\\n68|## integrate-light (default after ingest)\\n69|\\n70|See [integrate-light.md](references/integrate-light.md).\\n71|\\n72|1. Append one line to `Utilities/log.md` (time, type, note wikilink, path, daily note wikilink)\\n73|2. Create `Utilities/log.md` from `templates/log.md` if missing (see [integrate-light.md](references/integrate-light.md))\\n74|3. Run `integrate_entities.py` on the ingest (see [integrate-entities.md](references/integrate-entities.md))\\n75|4. Confirm hub updates from JSON report; set ingest `project:` frontmatter when script matches exactly one project\\n76|\\n77|**Immutability:** Do not edit bodies under `Inputs/Readings/`, `Inputs/Emails/`, `Inputs/Slack/`.\\n78|\\n79|## integrate-full (on demand)\\n80|\\n81|See [integrate-full.md](references/integrate-full.md).\\n82|\\n83|**Manual triggers:** \\\"integrate this\\\", \\\"wiki integrate today\\\", \\\"compile today's ingests\\\", \\\"integrate [[Project]]\\\", \\\"run wiki lint\\\".\\n84|\\n85|**Reading \u2192 Source promotion:** For each scoped Reading, create or update `Notes/<Title>.md`:\\n86|\\n87|```yaml\\n88|---\\n89|id: 'YYYYMMDDHHmmss'\\n90|daily_note: \\\"[[YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\"\\n91|category: \\\"[[Sources]]\\\"\\n92|---\\n93|```\\n94|\\n95|```markdown\\n96|# Title\\n97|\\n98|- **Author:**\\n99|- **URL:**\\n100|- **Type:** book | article | paper | video\\n101|\\n102|## Summary\\n103|Bes-compiled synthesis (kept current by integrate-full).\\n104|\\n105|## Raw inputs\\n106|- [[Reading note title]]\\n107|\\n108|## Related\\n109|- [[Concept or Project links]]\\n110|```\\n111|\\n112|Confirm with Justin before bulk mature-note edits.\\n113|\\n114|## integrate-query\\n115|\\n116|See [integrate-query.md](references/integrate-query.md). Search \u2192 synthesize \u2192 write to `Inbox/` for manual review when durability threshold met \u2192 print complete note in channel and run integrate-light log append. Template: [query-synthesis.md](templates/query-synthesis.md). Interactive sessions only \u2014 not cron.\\n117|\\n118|## Semantic lint (tier-3)\\n119|\\n120|See [lint.md](references/lint.md).\\n121|\\n122|**Monthly cron** (`wiki_semantic_lint_cron.py`, 1st of month): deterministic orphans, stale summaries, promotion gaps, link chains, contradiction candidates \u2192 `Utilities/reports/semantic-lint-*.md` + `~/.hermes/state/semantic_lint_last.json`.\\n123|\\n124|**On-demand:** Run `vault_hygiene.py` first (structural), then `wiki_semantic_lint.py`. Agent reviews contradiction candidates \u2014 never auto-edit Beliefs.\\n125|\\n126|## Boundaries\\n127|\\n128|| Skill | Role |\\n129||-------|------|\\n130|| work-log | Daily activity \u2014 not wiki compile |\\n131|| obsidian-hygiene | ID, folders, ghosts, URLs |\\n132|| obsidian-suggest-promotions | Maturity ladder \u2014 **do not modify** |\\n133|| bes-email-dispatch | Ingest emails \u2192 then integrate-light |\\n134|\\n135|## Taxonomy migration\\n136|\\n137|One-time `Logs/` \u2192 `Inputs/` via `scripts/migrate_logs_to_inputs.py`. See [taxonomy-migration.md](references/taxonomy-migration.md). Category templates for `Utilities/Categories/Readings.md` and rewritten `Sources.md` included there.\\n138|\\n139|## Common Pitfalls\\n140|\\n141|1. Compiling into Reading bodies \u2014 Inputs are immutable; compile to Source notes only.\\n142|2. Skipping integrate-light after cron ingest \u2014 log drifts; ingests become untraceable.\\n143|3. Auto-promoting maturity tiers \u2014 belongs to obsidian-suggest-promotions, not llm-wiki.\\n144|4. Linking Concepts directly to Readings \u2014 use Source as intermediary.\\n145|5. Running integrate-full from cron without approval \u2014 light pass only in autonomous runs.\\n146|6. Generating dense, bulky notes during query synthesis \u2014 keep integrations and syntheses highly atomic and focused, avoiding oversized multi-topic summaries.\\n147|7. Attempting to write timeline or related inputs lists to contact or project notes \u2014 automated timelines are disabled in favor of native backlinks; only update project `## State` on decisions/status.\\n148|8. Neglecting Log Maintenance: Failing to append to `Utilities/log.md` on every ingest makes files untraceable and breaks Karpathy-style chronological auditing.\\n149|\\n150|## Verification Checklist\\n151|\\n152|- [ ] integrate-light appended log line with daily note wikilink\\n153|- [ ] integrate-entities updated matched hub pages (or report shows unmatched only)\\n154|- [ ] No Input body edits on Readings/Emails/Slack\\n155|- [ ] Compiled Sources have `## Raw inputs` with Reading links\\n156|- [ ] Layer 3 notes untouched unless integrate-full explicitly scoped\\n157|- [ ] obsidian-hygiene run for structural baseline before semantic lint\\n158|- [ ] integrate-query: filed note exists in `Inbox/` with Question, Synthesis, Sources consulted sections\\n159|- [ ] integrate-query: printed the complete created note in the chat channel for review\\n160|- [ ] integrate-query: log line appended with `query` token\\n161|- [ ] integrate-query: Sources consulted section populated with upstream wikilinks\\n162|\\n163|The user has provided the following instruction alongside the skill invocation: [IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. DELIVERY: Your final response will be automatically delivered to the user \u2014 do NOT use send_message or try to deliver the output yourself. Just produce your report/output as your final response and the system handles the rest. SILENT: If there is genuinely nothing new to report, respond with exactly \\\"[SILENT]\\\" (nothing else) to suppress delivery. Never combine [SILENT] with content \u2014 either report your findings normally, or say [SILENT] and nothing more.]\\n164|\\n165|## Script Output\\n166|The following data was collected by a pre-run script. Use it as context for your analysis.\\n176|\\n177|The output of the fetcher script `fetch_slack_brains.py` is injected below:\\n178|\\n179|---\\n180|If the output is empty `[]` or has no items, stop immediately. Output absolutely nothing (or just '[SILENT]') and terminate. Do not write any files, do not edit daily notes, and do not send any message.\\n181|\\n182|If there are new items, process them one by one. For each item:\\n183|1. Determine the reaction type: `jg_log` or `jg_decision` based on the `\\\"reaction_type\\\"` field in the JSON item.\\n184|2. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `ts` or messages timestamps) in YYYY-MM-DD format.\\n185|3. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.\\n186|\\n187|4. **ALL INGESTED NOTES MUST BE ADDED TO THE INBOX FIRST!**\\n188|   - Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/`\\n189|\\n190|5. **Generate the Note Content based on Reaction Type:**\\n191|\\n192|   ### Path A: If reaction_type is \\\"jg_log\\\":\\n193|   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title].md`\\n194|   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**\\n195|     ```yaml\\n196|     ---\\n197|     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts\\n198|     daily_note: \\\"[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\" # e.g. [[Daily Notes/2026-06-09 Tuesday|2026-06-09 Tuesday]]\\n199|     category: \\\"[[Slack]]\\\"\\n200|     channel: \\\"<channel_name>\\\"\\n201|     original_url: \\\"<permalink>\\\"\\n202|     participants:\\n203|       - \\\"<Participant A>\\\"\\n204|       - \\\"<Participant B>\\\"\\n205|     ---\\n206|     ```\\n207|   - **Note Body Layout:**\\n208|     ```markdown\\n209|     # \ud83d\udcac Slack Thread: [Title]\\n210|\\n211|     - **Channel:** #<channel_name>\\n212|     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD\\n213|     - **Original Thread:** [Slack Link](<permalink>)\\n214|\\n215|     ---\\n216|\\n217|     ## \ud83c\udfaf Topic Description\\n218|     [A brief, 1-2 sentence temporary description of the topic discussed. Acts as a simple pointer to the conversation without point-by-point summaries, thoughts, decisions, or quotes.]\\n219|     ```\\n220|\\n221|   ### Path B: If reaction_type is \\\"jg_decision\\\":\\n222|   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title].md`\\n223|   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**\\n224|     ```yaml\\n225|     ---\\n226|     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts\\n227|     daily_note: \\\"[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\"\\n228|     category: \\\"[[Decisions]]\\\"\\n229|     channel: \\\"<channel_name>\\\"\\n230|     original_url: \\\"<permalink>\\\"\\n231|     participants:\\n232|       - \\\"<Participant A>\\\"\\n233|       - \\\"<Participant B>\\\"\\n234|     ---\\n235|     ```\\n236|   - **Note Body Layout:**\\n237|     ```markdown\\n238|     # \ud83c\udfaf Decision: [Title]\\n239|\\n240|     - **Channel:** #<channel_name>\\n241|     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD\\n242|     - **Original Thread:** [Slack Link](<permalink>)\\n243|\\n244|     ---\\n245|\\n246|     ## \ud83d\udcdd Context\\n247|     [A brief, 2-3 sentence context explaining the problem or trade-off discussed in the Slack thread.]\\n248|\\n249|     ## \u2696\ufe0f Options & Trade-offs\\n250|     - [Option A]: [Trade-offs/consequences]\\n251|     - [Option B]: [Trade-offs/consequences]\\n252|\\n253|     ## \ud83c\udfc1 Resolution\\n254|     [The final decision or recommendation made by the team, highlighting who made it.]\\n255|     ```\\n256|\\n257|6. Write the file to `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/<filename>`.\\n258|7. Update today's daily note:\\n259|   - Today's date is determined at runtime based on the system date (e.g. `2026-06-09`).\\n260|   - The daily note path is `/home/justin.guest/vault/Daily Notes/<YYYY-MM-DD Weekday>.md`.\\n261|   - Read today's daily note, locate the `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad` section, and append a clean bullet inside it:\\n262|     - For logs: `* [[Inbox/<filename>|Slack summary]]: <One-sentence topic summary>`.\\n263|     - For decisions: `* [[Inbox/<filename>|Decision]]: <One-sentence decision summary>`.\\n264|     Keep all other content in the notepad intact. Do NOT overwrite his manual notes.\\n265|8. Mark the conversation as processed to avoid duplicate processing:\\n266|   - Run the command: `python3 /home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py --mark-processed <channel_id> <ts>`\\n267|9. Once all items are successfully written and marked processed, load llm-wiki and run integrate-light for each new file: append Utilities/log.md only (daily note wikilink as last field). Do not update index.md or daily notepad via integrate-light. Do not modify Input bodies beyond initial filing.\\n268|10. Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist \\\"...\\\"]` for each new file. For jg_decision, pass resolution text as --gist (feeds project ## State). Optionally set `project:` in ingest frontmatter when channel-map or title match is obvious before calling the script.\\n269|11. Output a clear, concise summary of what was saved as your final response so it is sent to Justin on Telegram. Include hub updates: `\u2192 updated [[Project]], [[Contact]]`.\\n270|\\n271|Include absolute file paths for all operations. Verify files exist before reading/writing. Be extremely quiet if there's nothing to process.\\n272|\\n273|## Response\\n274|\\n275|[SILENT]\\n276|\", \"total_lines\": 275, \"file_size\": 14627, \"truncated\": false, \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}\n\n[Subdirectory context discovered: bes-backup/.cursorrules]\n# Cursor Rules for Bes Backup Repository\n\nThis file guides Cursor (and the user) when creating, modifying, and validating skills or system files in the `bes-backup` workspace. It ensures alignment with Hermes Agent standards and the safe bidirectional syncing workflow.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Syncing Workflow (How Changes Reach the VM)\n\nThis repository is bi-directionally synced with a headless VM (`bes-vm`) via a custom push-and-pull protocol:\n* **Host $\\rightarrow$ VM:** When editing in Cursor, **commit and push your changes to GitHub**, then run `bes-pull` in your host terminal (or ask your agent to run it). This stops the daemon, pulls/rebases safely, reverse-syncs files to the active `~/.hermes/` runtime folder, and restarts the backup daemon.\n* **VM $\\rightarrow$ Host:** Live changes in `~/.hermes/` (such as memories or dynamic scripts) are automatically committed and pushed to GitHub by the `bes-autocommit` service. Run `git pull` in Cursor to fetch them.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Skill Authoring Conventions (SKILL.md Schema)\n\nWhen creating or editing a skill under `skills/<category>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`, you **must** obey the following rigid standards:\n\n### required Frontmatter Constraints\nThe skill file must start with valid YAML frontmatter containing `name` and `description` fields.\n* **`name`:** Lowercase, hyphens only, maximum 64 characters.\n* **`description`:** Starts with *\"Use when ...\"*, describes the trigger class (not just the one task), and must be **$\\le$ 1024 characters**.\n* **Closing:** Must be followed by a clean newline, closed with `---`, and have non-empty content below.\n\nExample:\n```yaml\n---\nname: did-i-already-do-this\ndescription: Use when the user asks whether they already performed an action, completed a task, or had a conversation. Looks up past notes and transcripts.\nversion: 1.0.0\nauthor: Hermes Agent\nlicense: MIT\nmetadata:\n  hermes:\n    tags: [search, memory, validation]\n    related_skills: [obsidian, todoist]\n---\n```\n\n### Peer-Matched Structure\nEvery skill file should follow this standard structural layout:\n```markdown\n# Skill Title\n\n## Overview\nBrief explanation of what the skill is and why it exists.\n\n## When to Use\n* **Bullet list of triggers**\n* \"Don't use for:\" counter-triggers to prevent accidental invocation.\n\n## [Detailed Topic Sections]\n* Actionable recipes, reference tables, exact command snippets.\n\n## Common Pitfalls\nNumbered list of pitfalls, gotchas, and how to avoid them.\n\n## Verification Checklist\n- [ ] Checklist of post-action verifications.\n```\n\n### Size Limits\n* **Full File:** $\\le$ 100,000 characters. Aim for the **8,000 to 14,000 character range**. If a skill is growing larger, split detailed facts or schemas into supporting files under `references/*.md`.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Supporting Files Allowlist\n\nA skill is not limited to `SKILL.md`. You can create and reference supporting files inside the skill's folder, but they **must** be nested under one of the allowed directories:\n* `skills/<category>/<name>/references/` (Markdown documents, schemas, APIs)\n* `skills/<category>/<name>/templates/` (Config files, YAML structures)\n* `skills/<category>/<name>/scripts/` (Python/Bash tools)\n* `skills/<category>/<name>/assets/` (Visual assets, SVGs)\n\n---\n\n## 4. Gitignore Safety (Force-Tracking)\n\nThe repository's `.gitignore` excludes most bundled skill directories by default to avoid tracking unedited files.\n* **When customizing or creating a skill:** You must either:\n  1. Add an explicit re-include block at the end of `.gitignore` (e.g., `!skills/note-taking/my-skill/` and `!skills/note-taking/my-skill/**`), OR\n  2. Use `git add -f skills/category/my-skill/SKILL.md` to force-track the changes.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Script Guidelines\n\n* **Absolute Paths:** Always use absolute paths or resolve them relative to the user's home directory.\n* **Interpreter Commands:** Use absolute paths to interpreters inside subagents (e.g. `/usr/bin/python3` or fully qualified absolute executable paths) to avoid PATH bugs.\n* **Dependencies:** Do not assume non-standard packages are installed; list requirements clearly.",

          "content": "{\"content\": \"1|#!/usr/bin/env python3\\n2|\\\"\\\"\\\"Linear GraphQL API CLI \u2014 zero dependencies, stdlib only.\\n3|\\n4|Usage:\\n5|  linear_api.py <command> [args...]\\n6|\\n7|Commands:\\n8|  whoami                                  Show authenticated user\\n9|  list-teams                              List all teams\\n10|  list-projects [--team KEY]              List projects (optionally filter by team)\\n11|  list-states [--team KEY]                List workflow states\\n12|  list-issues [filters]                   List issues\\n13|    --team KEY                            Filter by team key (e.g. ENG)\\n14|    --status NAME                         Filter by workflow state name\\n15|    --assignee NAME                       Filter by assignee name (exact)\\n16|    --label NAME                          Filter by label name\\n17|    --limit N                             Max results (default: 25)\\n18|  get-issue <IDENTIFIER>                  Full issue details (e.g. ENG-42)\\n19|  search-issues <query>                   Full-text search across issues\\n20|  create-issue [options]                  Create a new issue\\n21|    --title TITLE                         Required\\n22|    --team KEY                            Required\\n23|    --description DESC\\n24|    --priority 0-4                        0=none, 1=urgent, 4=low\\n25|    --label NAME\\n26|    --assignee NAME\\n27|    --parent IDENTIFIER                   Parent issue ID for sub-issues\\n28|  update-issue <IDENTIFIER> [options]     Update existing issue (same options as create)\\n29|  update-status <IDENTIFIER> <STATE>      Move issue to workflow state (by state name)\\n30|  add-comment <IDENTIFIER> <body>         Add comment to issue\\n31|\\n32|  list-documents [--limit N]              List documents (docs, not issues)\\n33|  get-document <SLUG_OR_ID>               Fetch a document by slugId (from URL) or UUID\\n34|  search-documents <query>                Search documents by title\\n35|\\n36|  raw <graphql_query> [variables_json]    Run an arbitrary GraphQL query\\n37|                                          Use --vars '{\\\"key\\\":\\\"value\\\"}' for variables\\n38|\\n39|Auth:\\n40|  Set LINEAR_API_KEY environment variable (from Linear Settings -> API).\\n41|  Uses the personal API key header format: `Authorization: <KEY>` (no Bearer prefix).\\n42|\\n43|Output:\\n44|  JSON to stdout. Errors to stderr with non-zero exit code.\\n45|\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n46|from __future__ import annotations\\n47|\\n48|import argparse\\n49|import json\\n50|import os\\n51|import sys\\n52|import urllib.error\\n53|import urllib.request\\n54|from typing import Any\\n55|\\n56|API_URL = \\\"https://api.linear.app/graphql\\\"\\n57|\\n58|\\n59|def _get_key() -> str:\\n60|    key = os.environ.get(\\\"LINEAR_API_KEY\\\", \\\"\\\").strip()\\n61|    if not key:\\n62|        sys.stderr.write(\\n63|            \\\"ERROR: LINEAR_API_KEY not set.\\\\n\\\"\\n64|            \\\"Create one at https://linear.app/settings/api and export it,\\\\n\\\"\\n65|            \\\"or add `LINEAR_API_KEY=*** to ~/.hermes/.env\\\\n\\\"\\n66|        )\\n67|        sys.exit(2)\\n68|    return key\\n69|\\n70|\\n71|def gql(query: str, variables: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n72|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Execute a GraphQL query against Linear. Raises on HTTP error or GraphQL errors.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n73|    key = _get_key()\\n74|    payload = {\\\"query\\\": query}\\n75|    if variables:\\n76|        payload[\\\"variables\\\"] = variables\\n77|    data = json.dumps(payload).encode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n78|    req = urllib.request.Request(\\n79|        API_URL,\\n80|        data=data,\\n81|        headers={\\n82|            \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n83|            \\\"Authorization\\\": key,  # Personal API key \u2014 NO `Bearer` prefix\\n84|            \\\"User-Agent\\\": \\\"hermes-agent-linear-skill/1.0\\\",\\n85|        },\\n86|        method=\\\"POST\\\",\\n87|    )\\n88|    try:\\n89|        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:\\n90|            body = resp.read().decode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n91|    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\\n92|        sys.stderr.write(f\\\"HTTP {e.code}: {e.read().decode('utf-8', 'replace')}\\\\n\\\")\\n93|        sys.exit(1)\\n94|    except urllib.error.URLError as e:\\n95|        sys.stderr.write(f\\\"Network error: {e}\\\\n\\\")\\n96|        sys.exit(1)\\n97|\\n98|    result = json.loads(body)\\n99|    if \\\"errors\\\" in result and result[\\\"errors\\\"]:\\n100|        sys.stderr.write(f\\\"GraphQL errors: {json.dumps(result['errors'], indent=2)}\\\\n\\\")\\n101|        # Still return data if partial success; let caller decide\\n102|        if not result.get(\\\"data\\\"):\\n103|            sys.exit(1)\\n104|    return result.get(\\\"data\\\", {}) or {}\\n105|\\n106|\\n107|def emit(obj: Any) -> None:\\n108|    print(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, default=str))\\n109|\\n110|\\n111|# ---------- Commands ----------\\n112|\\n113|def cmd_whoami(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n114|    q = \\\"query { viewer { id name email displayName } }\\\"\\n115|    emit(gql(q).get(\\\"viewer\\\"))\\n116|\\n117|\\n118|def cmd_list_teams(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n119|    q = \\\"query { teams(first: 100) { nodes { id key name description } } }\\\"\\n120|    emit(gql(q).get(\\\"teams\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n121|\\n122|\\n123|def _resolve_team_id(key_or_name: str) -> str | None:\\n124|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Map a team key (ENG) or name to UUID.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n125|    q = \\\"query { teams(first: 100) { nodes { id key name } } }\\\"\\n126|    teams = gql(q).get(\\\"teams\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", [])\\n127|    kl = key_or_name.lower()\\n128|    for t in teams:\\n129|        if t[\\\"key\\\"].lower() == kl or t[\\\"name\\\"].lower() == kl:\\n130|            return t[\\\"id\\\"]\\n131|    return None\\n132|\\n133|\\n134|def cmd_list_projects(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n135|    if args.team:\\n136|        tid = _resolve_team_id(args.team)\\n137|        if not tid:\\n138|            sys.stderr.write(f\\\"Team not found: {args.team}\\\\n\\\")\\n139|            sys.exit(1)\\n140|        q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($id: String!) {\\n141|          team(id: $id) { projects(first: 100) { nodes { id name description state } } }\\n142|        }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n143|        data = gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": tid})\\n144|        emit(data.get(\\\"team\\\", {}).get(\\\"projects\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n145|    else:\\n146|        q = \\\"query { projects(first: 100) { nodes { id name description state } } }\\\"\\n147|        emit(gql(q).get(\\\"projects\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n148|\\n149|\\n150|def cmd_list_states(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n151|    if args.team:\\n152|        tid = _resolve_team_id(args.team)\\n153|        if not tid:\\n154|            sys.stderr.write(f\\\"Team not found: {args.team}\\\\n\\\")\\n155|            sys.exit(1)\\n156|        q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($id: String!) {\\n157|          team(id: $id) { states(first: 100) { nodes { id name type color } } }\\n158|        }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n159|        emit(gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": tid}).get(\\\"team\\\", {}).get(\\\"states\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n160|    else:\\n161|        q = \\\"query { workflowStates(first: 200) { nodes { id name type team { key } } } }\\\"\\n162|        emit(gql(q).get(\\\"workflowStates\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n163|\\n164|\\n165|def cmd_list_issues(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n166|    filt: dict[str, Any] = {}\\n167|    if args.team:\\n168|        filt[\\\"team\\\"] = {\\\"key\\\": {\\\"eq\\\": args.team}}\\n169|    if args.status:\\n170|        filt[\\\"state\\\"] = {\\\"name\\\": {\\\"eq\\\": args.status}}\\n171|    if args.assignee:\\n172|        filt[\\\"assignee\\\"] = {\\\"name\\\": {\\\"eq\\\": args.assignee}}\\n173|    if args.label:\\n174|        filt[\\\"labels\\\"] = {\\\"name\\\": {\\\"eq\\\": args.label}}\\n175|\\n176|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($filter: IssueFilter, $first: Int!) {\\n177|      issues(filter: $filter, first: $first, orderBy: updatedAt) {\\n178|        nodes {\\n179|          id identifier title\\n180|          state { name } priority\\n181|          assignee { name }\\n182|          team { key }\\n183|          updatedAt url\\n184|        }\\n185|      }\\n186|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n187|    data = gql(q, {\\\"filter\\\": filt or None, \\\"first\\\": args.limit})\\n188|    emit(data.get(\\\"issues\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n189|\\n190|\\n191|def cmd_get_issue(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n192|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($id: String!) {\\n193|      issue(id: $id) {\\n194|        id identifier title description\\n195|        state { name type }\\n196|        priority priorityLabel\\n197|        assignee { name email }\\n198|        creator { name }\\n199|        team { key name }\\n200|        project { name }\\n201|        labels { nodes { name } }\\n202|        parent { identifier title }\\n203|        children { nodes { identifier title state { name } } }\\n204|        comments { nodes { user { name } body createdAt } }\\n205|        createdAt updatedAt url\\n206|      }\\n207|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n208|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": args.identifier}).get(\\\"issue\\\"))\\n209|\\n210|\\n211|def cmd_search_issues(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n212|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($term: String!, $first: Int!) {\\n213|      searchIssues(term: $term, first: $first) {\\n214|        nodes { id identifier title state { name } url }\\n215|      }\\n216|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n217|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"term\\\": args.query, \\\"first\\\": args.limit}).get(\\\"searchIssues\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n218|\\n219|\\n220|def cmd_create_issue(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n221|    tid = _resolve_team_id(args.team)\\n222|    if not tid:\\n223|        sys.stderr.write(f\\\"Team not found: {args.team}\\\\n\\\")\\n224|        sys.exit(1)\\n225|    inp: dict[str, Any] = {\\\"title\\\": args.title, \\\"teamId\\\": tid}\\n226|    if args.description:\\n227|        inp[\\\"description\\\"] = args.description\\n228|    if args.priority is not None:\\n229|        inp[\\\"priority\\\"] = args.priority\\n230|    if args.parent:\\n231|        inp[\\\"parentId\\\"] = args.parent\\n232|    # TODO: label + assignee name->id lookup (omitted for v1 brevity)\\n233|\\n234|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"mutation($input: IssueCreateInput!) {\\n235|      issueCreate(input: $input) {\\n236|        success issue { id identifier title url }\\n237|      }\\n238|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n239|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"input\\\": inp}).get(\\\"issueCreate\\\"))\\n240|\\n241|\\n242|def cmd_update_issue(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n243|    inp: dict[str, Any] = {}\\n244|    if args.title:\\n245|        inp[\\\"title\\\"] = args.title\\n246|    if args.description:\\n247|        inp[\\\"description\\\"] = args.description\\n248|    if args.priority is not None:\\n249|        inp[\\\"priority\\\"] = args.priority\\n250|    if not inp:\\n251|        sys.stderr.write(\\\"No update fields provided.\\\\n\\\")\\n252|        sys.exit(1)\\n253|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"mutation($id: String!, $input: IssueUpdateInput!) {\\n254|      issueUpdate(id: $id, input: $input) {\\n255|        success issue { identifier title url }\\n256|      }\\n257|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n258|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": args.identifier, \\\"input\\\": inp}).get(\\\"issueUpdate\\\"))\\n259|\\n260|\\n261|def cmd_update_status(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n262|    # Resolve state name -> id within the issue's team\\n263|    get_q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($id: String!) {\\n264|      issue(id: $id) { team { id states(first: 100) { nodes { id name } } } }\\n265|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n266|    issue = gql(get_q, {\\\"id\\\": args.identifier}).get(\\\"issue\\\")\\n267|    if not issue:\\n268|        sys.stderr.write(f\\\"Issue not found: {args.identifier}\\\\n\\\")\\n269|        sys.exit(1)\\n270| sys.stderr.write(\\n274|            f\\\"State '{args.state}' not found. Available: \\\"\\n275|            f\\\"{[s['name'] for s in issue['team']['states']['nodes']]}\\\\n\\\"\\n276|        )\\n277|        sys.exit(1)\\n278|\\n279|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"mutation($id: String!, $stateId: String!) {\\n280|      issueUpdate(id: $id, input: { stateId: $stateId }) {\\n281|        success issue { identifier state { name } url }\\n282|      }\\n283|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n284|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": args.identifier, \\\"stateId\\\": match[\\\"id\\\"]}).get(\\\"issueUpdate\\\"))\\n285|\\n286|\\n287|def cmd_add_comment(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n288|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"mutation($input: CommentCreateInput!) {\\n289|      commentCreate(input: $input) {\\n290|        success comment { id body createdAt }\\n291|      }\\n292|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n293|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"input\\\": {\\\"issueId\\\": args.identifier, \\\"body\\\": args.body}}).get(\\\"commentCreate\\\"))\\n294|\\n295|\\n296|# ---- Documents ----\\n297|\\n298|def cmd_list_documents(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n299|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($first: Int!) {\\n300|      documents(first: $first, orderBy: updatedAt) {\\n301|        nodes { id title slugId updatedAt url project { name } creator { name } }\\n302|      }\\n303|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n304|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"first\\\": args.limit}).get(\\\"documents\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n305|\\n306|\\n307|def cmd_get_document(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n308|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Fetch a document by slugId (from URL) OR full UUID.\\n309|\\n310|    Linear document URLs look like:\\n311|      https://linear.app/<workspace>/document/<slug>-<shortid>\\n312|    The part we want is the final hex segment (the slugId).\\n313|    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n314|    ref = args.ref\\n315|    # If it looks like a UUID, query by id. Otherwise, assume slugId.\\n316|    is_uuid = len(ref) == 36 and ref.count(\\\"-\\\") == 4\\n317|    if is_uuid:\\n318|        q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($id: String!) {\\n319|          document(id: $id) {\\n320|            id title content contentState slugId\\n321|            createdAt updatedAt url\\n322|            creator { name } project { name }\\n323|          }\\n324|        }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n325|        emit(gql(q, {\\\"id\\\": ref}).get(\\\"document\\\"))\\n326|    else:\\n327|        # Query the collection and filter by slugId \u2014 the doc() query only accepts UUIDs.\\n328|        q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($slug: String!) {\\n329|          documents(filter: { slugId: { eq: $slug } }, first: 1) {\\n330|            nodes {\\n331|              id title content contentState slugId\\n332|              createdAt updatedAt url\\n333|              creator { name } project { name }\\n334|            }\\n335|          }\\n336|        }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n337|        nodes = gql(q, {\\\"slug\\\": ref}).get(\\\"documents\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", [])\\n338|        emit(nodes[0] if nodes else None)\\n339|\\n340|\\n341|def cmd_search_documents(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n342|    # Linear doesn't have a first-class searchDocuments \u2014 use title filter as a fallback.\\n343|    q = \\\"\\\"\\\"query($term: String!, $first: Int!) {\\n344|      documents(filter: { title: { containsIgnoreCase: $term } }, first: $first) {\\n345|        nodes { id title slugId url updatedAt }\\n346|      }\\n347|    }\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n348|    emit(gql(q, {\\\"term\\\": args.query, \\\"first\\\": args.limit}).get(\\\"documents\\\", {}).get(\\\"nodes\\\", []))\\n349|\\n350|\\n351|def cmd_raw(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\n352|    variables = json.loads(args.vars) if args.vars else None\\n353|    emit(gql(args.query, variables))\\n354|\\n355|\\n356|# ---------- Arg parsing ----------\\n357|\\n358|def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\\n359|    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog=\\\"linear_api.py\\\", description=\\\"Linear GraphQL CLI\\\")\\n360|    sub = p.add_subparsers(dest=\\\"cmd\\\", li.set_defaults(func=cmd_list_issues)\\n380|\\n381|    gi = sub.add_parser(\\\"get-issue\\\")\\n382|    gi.add_argument(\\\"identifier\\\")\\n383|    gi.set_defaults(func=cmd_get_issue)\\n384|\\n385| required=True)\\n393|    ci.add_argument(\\\"--description\\\")\\n394|    ci.add_argument(\\\"--priority\\\", type=int, choices=[0, 1, 2, 3, 4])\\n395|    ci.add_argument(\\\"--label\\\")\\n396|         {
          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"linear\", \"description\": \"Linear: manage issues, projects, teams via GraphQL + curl.\", \"tags\": [\"Linear\", \"Project Management\", \"Issues\", \"GraphQL\", \"API\", \"Productivity\"], \"related_skills\": [], \"content\": \"---\\nname: linear\\ndescription: \\\"Linear: manage issues, projects, teams via GraphQL + curl.\\\"\\nversion: 1.0.0\\nauthor: Hermes Agent\\nlicense: MIT\\nplatforms: [linux, macos, windows]\\nprerequisites:\\n  env_vars: [LINEAR_API_KEY]\\n  commands: [curl]\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [Linear, Project Management, Issues, GraphQL, API, Productivity]\\n---\\n\\n# Linear \u2014 Issue & Project Management\\n\\nManage Linear issues, projects, and teams directly via the GraphQL API using `curl`. No MCP server, no OAuth flow, no extra dependencies.\\n\\n## Setup\\n\\n1. Get a personal API key from **Linear Settings > Account > Security & access > Personal API keys** (URL: https://linear.app/settings/account/security). Note: the org-level *Settings > API* page only shows OAuth apps and workspace-member keys, not personal keys.\\n2. Set `LINEAR_API_KEY` in your environment (via `hermes setup` or your env config)\\n\\n## API Basics\\n\\n- **Endpoint:** `https://api.linear.app/graphql` (POST)\\n- **Auth header:** `Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY` (no \\\"Bearer\\\" prefix for API keys)\\n- **All requests are POST** with `Content-Type: application/json`\\n- **Both UUIDs and short identifiers** (e.g., `ENG-123`) work for `issue(id:)`\\n\\nBase curl pattern:\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ viewer { id name } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n## Python helper script (ergonomic alternative)\\n\\nFor faster one-liners that don't need hand-written GraphQL, this skill ships a stdlib Python CLI at `scripts/linear_api.py`. Zero dependencies. Same auth (reads `LINEAR_API_KEY`).\\n\\n```bash\\nSCRIPT=$(dirname \\\"$(find ~/.hermes -path '*skills/productivity/linear/scripts/linear_api.py' 2>/dev/null | head -1)\\\")/linear_api.py\\n\\npython3 \\\"$SCRIPT\\\" whoami\\npython3 \\\"$SCRIPT\\\" list-teams\\npython3 \\\"$SCRIPT\\\" get-issue ENG-42\\npython3 \\\"$SCRIPT\\\" get-document 38359beef67c      # fetch a doc by slugId from the URL\\npython3 \\\"$SCRIPT\\\" raw 'query { viewer { name } }'\\n```\\n\\nAll subcommands: `whoami`, `list-teams`, `list-projects`, `list-states`, `list-issues`, `get-issue`, `search-issues`, `create-issue`, `update-issue`, `update-status`, `add-comment`, `list-documents`, `get-document`, `search-documents`, `raw`. Run with `--help` for flags.\\n\\nUse the script when: you want a quick answer without crafting GraphQL. Use curl when: you need a query the script doesn't wrap, or you want to compose filters inline.\\n\\n## Workflow States\\n\\nLinear uses `WorkflowState` objects with a `type` field. **6 state types:**\\n\\n| Type | Description |\\n|------|-------------|\\n| `triage` | Incoming issues needing review |\\n| `backlog` | Acknowledged but not yet planned |\\n| `unstarted` | Planned/ready but not started |\\n| `started` | Actively being worked on |\\n| `completed` | Done |\\n| `canceled` | Won't do |\\n\\nEach team has its own named states (e.g., \\\"In Progress\\\" is type `started`). To change an issue's status, you need the `stateId` (UUID) of the target state \u2014 query workflow states first.\\n\\n**Priority values:** 0 = None, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Medium, 4 = Low\\n\\n## Common Queries\\n\\n### Get current user\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ viewer { id name email } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List teams\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ teams { nodes { id name key } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List workflow states for a team\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ workflowStates(filter: { team: { key: { eq: \\\\\\\"ENG\\\\\\\" } } }) { nodes { id name type } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List issues (first 20)\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issues(first: 20) { nodes { identifier title priority state { name type } assignee { name } team { key } url } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List my assigned issues\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ viewer { assignedIssues(first: 25) { nodes { identifier title state { name type } priority url } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Get a single issue (by identifier like ENG-123)\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issue(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\") { id identifier title description priority state { id name type } assignee { id name } team { key } project { name } labels { nodes { name } } comments { nodes { body user { name } createdAt } } url } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Search issues by text\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issueSearch(query: \\\\\\\"bug login\\\\\\\", first: 10) { nodes { identifier title state { name } assignee { name } url } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Filter issues by state type\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issues(filter: { state: { type: { in: [\\\\\\\"started\\\\\\\"] } } }, first: 20) { nodes { identifier title state { name } assignee { name } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Filter by team and assignee\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issues(filter: { team: { key: { eq: \\\\\\\"ENG\\\\\\\" } }, assignee: { email: { eq: \\\\\\\"user@example.com\\\\\\\" } } }, first: 20) { nodes { identifier title state { name } priority } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List projects\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ projects(first: 20) { nodes { id name description progress lead { name } teams { nodes { key } } url } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List team members\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ users { nodes { id name email active } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List labels\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issueLabels { nodes { id name color } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n## Common Mutations\\n\\n### Create an issue\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\n    \\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation($input: IssueCreateInput!) { issueCreate(input: $input) { success issue { id identifier title url } } }\\\",\\n    \\\"variables\\\": {\\n      \\\"input\\\": {\\n        \\\"teamId\\\": \\\"TEAM_UUID\\\",\\n        \\\"title\\\": \\\"Fix login bug\\\",\\n        \\\"description\\\": \\\"Users cannot login with SSO\\\",\\n        \\\"priority\\\": 2\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Update issue status\\nFirst get the target state UUID from the workflow states query above, then:\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { stateId: \\\\\\\"STATE_UUID\\\\\\\" }) { success issue { identifier state { name type } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Assign an issue\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { assigneeId: \\\\\\\"USER_UUID\\\\\\\" }) { success issue { identifier assignee { name } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Set priority\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { priority: 1 }) { success issue { identifier priority } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Add a comment\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { commentCreate(input: { issueId: \\\\\\\"ISSUE_UUID\\\\\\\", body: \\\\\\\"Investigated. Root cause is X.\\\\\\\" }) { success comment { id body } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Set due date\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { dueDate: \\\\\\\"2026-04-01\\\\\\\" }) { success issue { identifier dueDate } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Add labels to an issue\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { labelIds: [\\\\\\\"LABEL_UUID_1\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"LABEL_UUID_2\\\\\\\"] }) { success issue { identifier labels { nodes { name } } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Add issue to a project\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation { issueUpdate(id: \\\\\\\"ENG-123\\\\\\\", input: { projectId: \\\\\\\"PROJECT_UUID\\\\\\\" }) { success issue { identifier project { name } } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Create a project\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\n    \\\"query\\\": \\\"mutation($input: ProjectCreateInput!) { projectCreate(input: $input) { success project { id name url } } }\\\",\\n    \\\"variables\\\": {\\n      \\\"input\\\": {\\n        \\\"name\\\": \\\"Q2 Auth Overhaul\\\",\\n        \\\"description\\\": \\\"Replace legacy auth with OAuth2 and PKCE\\\",\\n        \\\"teamIds\\\": [\\\"TEAM_UUID\\\"]\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }' | jq .\\n```\\n\\n## Documents\\n\\nLinear **Documents** are prose docs (RFCs, specs, notes) stored alongside issues. They have their own `documents` root query and `document(id:)` single-fetch.\\n\\n### Document URLs and `slugId`\\n\\nDocument URLs look like:\\n```\\nhttps://linear.app/<workspace>/document/<slug>-<hexSlugId>\\n```\\n\\nThe trailing hex segment is the `slugId`. Example: `https://linear.app/nousresearch/document/rfc-hermes-permission-gateway-discord-38359beef67c` \u2192 `slugId` is `38359beef67c`.\\n\\n**Important schema detail:** the Markdown body is in the `content` field. The ProseMirror JSON is in `contentState` (not `contentData` \u2014 that field does not exist and the API returns 400).\\n\\n### Fetch a document by slugId\\n\\n`document(id:)` only accepts UUIDs. To fetch by the URL's hex slug, filter the collection:\\n\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"query($s: String!) { documents(filter: { slugId: { eq: $s } }, first: 1) { nodes { id title content contentState slugId url creator { name } project { name } updatedAt } } }\\\", \\\"variables\\\": {\\\"s\\\": \\\"38359beef67c\\\"}}' \\\\\\n  | jq .\\n```\\n\\nOr via the Python helper:\\n```bash\\npython3 scripts/linear_api.py get-document 38359beef67c\\n```\\n\\n### Fetch a document by UUID\\n\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ document(id: \\\\\\\"11700cff-b514-4db3-afcc-3ed1afacba1c\\\\\\\") { title content url } }\\\"}' \\\\\\n  | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### List recent documents\\n\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ documents(first: 25, orderBy: updatedAt) { nodes { id title slugId url updatedAt project { name } } } }\\\"}' \\\\\\n  | jq .\\n```\\n\\n### Search documents by title\\n\\nLinear's schema has no `searchDocuments` root. Use a title-substring filter instead:\\n\\n```bash\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ documents(filter: { title: { containsIgnoreCase: \\\\\\\"RFC\\\\\\\" } }, first: 25) { nodes { title slugId url } } }\\\"}' \\\\\\n  | jq .\\n```\\n\\n## Pagination\\n\\nLinear uses Relay-style cursor pagination:\\n\\n```bash\\n# First page\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issues(first: 20) { nodes { identifier title } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n\\n# Next page \u2014 use endCursor from previous response\\ncurl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Authorization: $LINEAR_API_KEY\\\" \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"query\\\": \\\"{ issues(first: 20, after: \\\\\\\"CURSOR_FROM_PREVIOUS\\\\\\\") { nodes { identifier title } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } }\\\"}' | jq .\\n```\\n\\nDefault page size: 50. Max: 250. Always use `first: N` to limit results.\\n\\n## Filtering Reference\\n\\nComparators: `eq`, `neq`, `in`, `nin`, `lt`, `lte`, `gt`, `gte`, `contains`, `startsWith`, `containsIgnoreCase`\\n\\nCombine filters with `or: [...]` for OR logic (default is AND within a filter object).\\n\\n## Typical Workflow\\n\\n1. **Query teams** to get team IDs and keys\\n2. **Query workflow states** for target team to get state UUIDs\\n3. **List or search issues** to find what needs work\\n4. **Create issues** with team ID, title, description, priority\\n5. **Update status** by setting `stateId` to the target workflow state\\n6. **Add comments** to track progress\\n7. **Mark complete** by setting `stateId` to the team's \\\"completed\\\" type state\\n\\n## Rate Limits\\n\\n- 5,000 requests/hour per API key\\n- 3,000,000 complexity points/hour\\n- Use `first: N` to limit results and reduce complexity cost\\n- Monitor `X-RateLimit-Requests-Remaining` response header\\n\\n## Important Notes\\n\\n- Always use `terminal` tool with `curl` for API calls \u2014 do NOT use `web_extract` or `browser`\\n- Always check the `errors` array in GraphQL responses \u2014 HTTP 200 can still contain errors\\n- If `stateId` is omitted when creating issues, Linear defaults to the first backlog state\\n- The `description` field supports Markdown\\n- Use `jq .` or `jq` to format JSON responses for readability\\n\", \"path\": \"productivity/linear/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/productivity/linear\", \"linked_files\": {\"scripts\": [\"scripts/linear_api.py\"]},         {
          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "I have successfully built, tested, and scheduled your **Linear Brain Note Capture** system. It mirrors your existing Slack ingest flow, keeping things perfectly clean and structured.\n\nHere is what has been accomplished and is now live:\n\n### 1. The Poller: `fetch_linear_brains.py`\nI wrote and tested a custom GraphQL poller at `/home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_linear_brains.py`.\n* **Behavior:** It checks comments, project updates, and initiative updates for the custom emoji reaction `:obsidian_jg:` (`emoji == \"obsidian_jg\"`).\n* **Justin-Only Filter:** It verifies client-side that you are the one who added the reaction (matching against your Linear User ID: `211987db-f790-4bfa-8c8e-518e1f704901`).\n* **Deep Context Gathering:** Unlike basic pollers, it dynamically fetches the full context of what was marked:\n  * If on an **Issue**: Gets issue metadata (`title`, `description`, `state`), parent/sibling comments (to capture the \"thread\"), and project linkages.\n  * If on a **Project Update** or **Initiative Update**: Summarizes the project/initiative metadata alongside the update prose and thread comments.\n  * If on a **Document**: Grabs the prose document\u2019s title, creator, and project details.\n* **Cache Management:** Keeps an immutable record of already-captured nodes in `~/.hermes/processed_linear_brains.json` to prevent duplicates.\n\n### 2. The Cron Job: \"Linear Brain Note Capture\" (`bb9152d836ca`)\nI scheduled a background job to run **every 120 minutes** on your active profile. When it triggers, the agent will:\n1. Parse the poller's JSON output. If empty, it silently terminates (`[SILENT]`).\n2. Generate structured Layer 1 Input files under `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/` using the filename:\n   `YYYY-MM-DD - Linear - [Spaced Capitalized Title].md`\n3. Structure the notes cleanly:\n   * Frontmatter including `id`, `category: \"[[Linear]]\"`, `original_url`, `author`, and `associated_with`.\n   * Clear markdown blockquotes containing your marked content.\n   * Deep synthesis of the parent issue, document, project, or initiative and its corresponding thread.\n4. Append a pointer to today's Daily Note under the `## \ud83d\uddd2 Notepad` section:\n   `* [[Inbox/filename|Linear Capture]]: <One-sentence summary>`\n5. Automatically run your existing `integrate-light` pass (logging to `Utilities/log.md`) and your `integrate-entities` pass (`integrate_entities.py`) to keep project hub states synchronized.\n6. Commit, push, and mark the items as processed in your local cache.\n\n### 3. Vault & Memory Alignment\n* Created a new Category definition note at `/home/justin.guest/vault/Utilities/Categories/Linear.md` to establish the `[[Linear]]` input stream within your vault taxonomy.\n* Updated your cross-session memory limits and consolidated your routes, officially adding `Inputs/Linear` as a primary entry point.\n\nThe daemon is running silently in the background. Simply react to any Linear comment or update with `:obsidian_jg:` to route it directly into your vault.",

Report-only \u2014 orphans (inbound), stale Sources, promotion gaps, contradiction candidates. State: `~/.hermes/state/semantic_lint_last.json`. Structural lint stays in `vault_hygiene.py`.\", \"Forwarded emails processed by Bes default to the Inputs/Emails/ directory as inputs.\", \"Linear capture: Poller fetch_linear_brains.py queries comments and updates with obsidian_jg or \ud83e\udde0 reaction by Justin. Ingested notes save under vault/Inbox/ as Inputs/Linear.\"], \"usage\": \"94% \u2014 2,082/2,200 chars\", \"entry_count\": 14, \"message\": \"Entry added.\"}",

          "content": "Done. I have patched the Linear poller script at `/home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/fetch_linear_brains.py` to support both the custom `:obsidian_jg:` emoji and the native `\ud83e\udde0` brain emoji (including its shortcode `\"brain\"` and unicode symbol `\"\ud83e\udde0\"`). \n\n* **GraphQL Filter Updated:** The API query now retrieves comments and updates carrying either reaction.\n* **Client-Side Checks Synchronized:** The verification step successfully accepts any of these tags as a valid brain signal when added by you.\n* **Persistent Memory Saved:** Recorded the new dual-emoji behavior in your cross-session note memories to keep your profile fully aligned.\n\nYour background poller daemon is fully up to date and watching for both triggers!",

          "role": "user",
          "content": "I want to start using some of our Telegram sessions as inputs. This would require a schema change and some new ingest plumbing. I'll flag the sessions I want to ingest by using a \ud83e\udde0 emoji in one my messages *or* as a reaction to one of your messages. The catch: I don't want you to ingest the session *as part of* the session. I want you to fetch and ingest in the background, like you do for other inputs. When you ingest, record a summary of the session plus information you can use to identify the full session logs (I'm not sure where those live or what format they're in).",
