          "role": "tool",
          "content": "{\"success\": true, \"name\": \"obsidian\", \"description\": \"Core settings, paths, frontmatter schemas, baseline conventions, and note triage/sorting mapping rules for Justin's Obsidian vault.\", \"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"core\", \"conventions\", \"paths\", \"frontmatter\", \"triage\", \"sorting\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian-contacts\", \"obsidian-notes\", \"obsidian-inputs\", \"obsidian-utilities\", \"llm-wiki\", \"obsidian-hygiene\"], \"content\": \"---\\nname: obsidian\\ndescription: Core settings, paths, frontmatter schemas, baseline conventions, and note triage/sorting mapping rules for Justin's Obsidian vault.\\nversion: 1.4.0\\nauthor: Bes\\nlicense: MIT\\nmetadata:\\n  hermes:\\n    tags: [obsidian, core, conventions, paths, frontmatter, triage, sorting]\\n    related_skills: [obsidian-contacts, obsidian-notes, obsidian-inputs, obsidian-utilities, llm-wiki, obsidian-hygiene]\\n---\\n\\n# Obsidian: Core Vault Conventions & Triage Routing\\n\\n## Overview\\nThis is the foundational skill for interacting with Justin's Obsidian vault. It defines paths, general note-creation rules, frontmatter fields, standard formatting conventions, and global note-triage routing rules. Interactive synthesis filing (durable Q&A answers) \u2192 `llm-wiki` integrate-query (saved to `Inbox/` on creation).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Note Hierarchy (The Three-Tier System)\\nThe vault utilizes a unidirectional, three-tier conceptual hierarchy to manage the evolution of knowledge:\\n\\n1. **Tier 1 (Raw & Ephemeral Inputs):**\\n   - *Categories:* `[[Scraps]]` (default for quick-capture), `[[Concepts]]` (others' thinking), `[[Decisions]]`, `[[Memories]]`.\\n   - *Filename Format:* `Title ID.md` (e.g., `Spaced Title 20260610120000.md`).\\n   - *Role:* Scratchpads, raw brain dumps, source clippings, or raw logs. Almost everything defaults to `[[Scraps]]`.\\n\\n2. **Tier 2 (Emergent, Synthesized & Factual Supporting Notes):**\\n   - *Categories:* `[[Notes]]` (compiled factual support), `[[Thoughts]]` (opinions/theories).\\n   - *Filename Format:* `Title ID.md` (retains creation ID/timestamp at the end).\\n   - *Role:* Personal ideas, active theories, open research questions, or compiled factual information supporting higher tiers.\\n\\n3. **Tier 3 (Permanent & Conviction Beliefs):**\\n   - *Categories:* `[[Beliefs]]` (guiding models), `[[References]]` (checklists/cheat sheets).\\n   - *Filename Format:* For `[[References]]`, `Title.md` (no ID string in the filename). For `[[Beliefs]]`, `Title ID.md` (ID at the end, if retaining creation ID).\\n   - *Role:* Trusted, highly stable playbooks. Beliefs require a specific structure (core thesis, exactly 3 **Core Tenets**, and 2 **Application** scenarios).\\n\\n**Core Hierarchy Rules:**\\n- **The Unidirectional Link Rule:** Higher-tier notes must not link downward to lower-tier notes (e.g., a `[[Belief]]` or `[[Reference]]` cannot link to an ephemeral `[[Scrap]]`, `[[Note]]`, or `[[Thought]]`).\\n- **Default to Scraps:** All quick-captures, fleeting brain dumps, and unrefined jottings default to Tier 1 `[[Scraps]]`.\\n- **Reversion Naming:** If a note is demoted back to Tier 1 or Tier 2, its filename is reverted to restore the 14-digit ID prefix.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Vault Path\\n- Vault root directory: `/home/justin.guest/vault`\\n- Environmental variable: `OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` set in `~/.hermes/.env` (resolves to `/home/justin.guest/vault` inside VM).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Baseline Conventions\\n\\n### Frontmatter Schema\\nEvery manual note must have a YAML frontmatter block containing at least these fields:\\n```yaml\\n---\\nid: 'YYYYMMDDHHmmss'                 # Numerical string based on creation time\\ndaily_note: \\\"[[YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]\\\" # Shortest-path link to creation day\\ncategory: \\\"[[CategoryName]]\\\"         # Single category link (quoted shortest-path wikilink)\\n---\\n```\\n- Use single quotes `'` or double quotes `\\\"` around IDs and wikilinks to ensure YAML parsing is safe.\\n- **`aliases`** (optional): YAML list of alternative names for easy lookup.\\n- **`project`** (optional): Quoted shortest-path wikilink pointing to a parent project (placed last in frontmatter).\\n\\n### Link Conventions\\n- **Shortest-Path Wikilinks:** Always use cleanest, shortest-path wikilinks (e.g., `[[Note Name]]` or `[[Note Name|Display Text]]`) instead of full folder path links (e.g., `[[Folder/Note Name]]`). \\n- **Name Collisions:** Full paths are only permitted if a direct name collision actually exists in the vault (meaning two separate files share the exact same base filename in different directories). Known collision: `Cracking the Pm Career` (Readings merge \u2014 resolve during migration).\\n- **Formatting:** Keep display names concise and avoid full path leaks in normal note body reading.\\n\\n### Formatting Rules\\n- **Horizontal Rules:** Always use exactly three hyphens `---` on a line by itself to represent a horizontal divider. Never use two hyphens or other symbols.\\n- **Heading & Bullet Spacing:** Ensure exactly one blank line exists between any heading (such as `## Timeline` or `## State`) and its subsequent content or bullet lists. Do not allow multiple consecutive blank lines to accumulate. Bulleted lists should be kept compact with zero blank lines between siblings.\\n- **Filename Conventions:**\\n  - **Daily Notes:** Must be `YYYY-MM-DD Weekday.md` (e.g. `2026-06-09 Tuesday.md`).\\n  - **Notes, Decisions, Thoughts, Memories, Concepts, Scraps, Beliefs, Sources (compiled):** Must be named `Title ID.md` with the ID at the end (e.g. `Spaced Title 20260609120000.md`).\\n  - **References:** Must be named `Title.md` with no ID string in the filename (e.g. `Spaced Title.md`).\\n  - **Readings (raw inputs):** `Title.md` or Readwise-exported names under `Inputs/Readings/` (no forced rename).\\n  - **Projects:** Must be `Title.md` under `Notes/Projects/` (e.g. `Spaced Title.md`).\\n  - **Contacts (People/Organizations):** Must be `Title.md` (e.g. `Aly Lalji.md`, `SignLab.md`).\\n  - **Meetings:** Must be `YYYY-MM-DD - Spaced Meeting Title.md` (e.g. `2026-06-09 - SignLab Product Alignment.md`).\\n  - Do not use kebab-case, lowercase hyphenated, or otherwise incorrect filenames. All names must be capitalized, spaced titles following these rules.\\n  - **Rationale:** As notes become less ephemeral and more authoritative (moving up the hierarchy), the date and/or ID is de-emphasized or omitted in the filename. Ephemeral, lower-tier notes are highly temporal (their place in time and sequence matters more), whereas canonical/authoritative notes (projects, people, beliefs, references) should stand out visually and contextually by title alone.\\n\\n### Git & Synchronization\\n- Do NOT run `git` commands (add, commit, push) inside the vault. The background watcher `bes-vault-sync` handles commit and synchronization to GitHub automatically within seconds of filesystem writes.\\n- For detailed architecture, configuration, scripts, and pitfalls of the synchronization daemons (`bes-vault-sync` and `bes-autocommit`), see the **[Git & Synchronization Architecture Reference](references/git-and-synchronization-architecture.md)**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Global Note-Triage Routing Table\\n\\nNotes are categorized with a single `category` YAML property containing a quoted wikilink to its category note. Based on this category, they must be sorted into the corresponding folder (matching the `Type:` folder mapping in the category's definition file):\\n\\n| Target Folder | Target Category Link | Description / Type | Sub-skill Guidance |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `Notes/Contacts/` | `category: \\\"[[People]]\\\"` | Individual contacts, friends, family, collaborators | `obsidian-people` |\\n| `Notes/Contacts/` | `category: \\\"[[Organizations]]\\\"` | Companies, schools, institutions, legal entities | `obsidian-organizations` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Notes]]\\\"` | Compiled, factual supporting notes | `obsidian-notes` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Sources]]\\\"` | Compiled bibliographical records per work; links down to raw Readings | `llm-wiki` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[References]]\\\"` | Useful facts, cheat sheets, guidelines, checklists | `obsidian-references-sources` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Concepts]]\\\"` | Other people's models/theories extracted from Sources | `obsidian-references-sources` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Thoughts]]\\\"` | Personal ideas, current opinions, research questions | `obsidian-thoughts-beliefs` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Beliefs]]\\\"` | Evolved concepts/thoughts - trusted models, core guiding principles | `obsidian-thoughts-beliefs` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Decisions]]\\\"` | Team or individual decisions and reasoning logs | `obsidian-decisions` |\\n| `Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Memories]]\\\"` | Journal-like personal notes of things I want to remember | |\\n| `Notes/Projects/` | `category: \\\"[[Projects]]\\\"` | Hubs for notes about ongoing work, milestones, travel | `obsidian-projects` |\\n| `Notes/Daily Notes/` | `category: \\\"[[Daily Notes]]\\\"` | Daily notes containing schedules and work logs | `obsidian-daily-notes` |\\n| `Inputs/Meetings/` | `category: \\\"[[Meetings]]\\\"` | Meeting agendas, summaries, outcomes (Granola reconcile) | `obsidian-meetings` |\\n| `Inputs/Readings/` | `category: \\\"[[Readings]]\\\"` | Raw reading imports \u2014 Readwise, clippings (immutable) | `llm-wiki` |\\n| `Inputs/Emails/` | `category: \\\"[[Emails]]\\\"` | Email thread summaries | `bes-email-dispatch` |\\n| `Inputs/Slack/` | `category: \\\"[[Slack]]\\\"` | Slack conversation summaries | `slack` |\\n| `[Vault Root]` | `category: \\\"[[Scraps]]\\\"` | Fleeting brain dumps, raw quick-captures, scraps | `obsidian-notes` |\\n| `Utilities/` | `category: \\\"[[Categories]]\\\"` | Category definition notes (`Utilities/Categories/`) | `obsidian-utilities` |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Action Steps for Triage\\n1. **Determine Category:** Read note content and title. Map it to exactly one category above. Convert legacy inline tags (e.g. `#meeting`) to the property and remove from body.\\n2. **Update YAML:** Set `category: \\\"[[<CategoryName>]]\\\"` using targeted `patch`.\\n3. **Move File:** Relocate the file from `Inbox/` to its corresponding target folder. Create folders with `mkdir -p` if missing.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Linked Utility Scripts\\n- **`scripts/migrate_note_names_2026.py`:** Statically re-runnable python script to scan the entire vault, detect files with old naming conventions (ID prefix or ID-carrying References), rename them safely (IDs at the end for notes, no IDs for References), check for collisions, and heal all corresponding wikilinks across the vault.\\n- **`scripts/heal_wikilinks.py`:** Statically re-runnable python script to scan the entire vault, identify base-filename collisions, and automatically heal/simplify any full-path wikilinks down to clean shortest-path wikilinks. Runs completely dynamically and safely.\\n\", \"path\": \"note-taking/obsidian/SKILL.md\", \"skill_dir\": \"/home/justin.guest/.hermes/skills/note-taking/obsidian\", \"linked_files\": {\"references\": [\"references/git-and-synchronization-architecture.md\", \"references/vault-audit-2026-05-22.md\", \"references/vault-hygiene-design.md\", \"references/obsidian-flavored-markdown.md\"], \"scripts\": [\"scripts/rename_kebab_notes.py\", \"scripts/reconcile_granola.py\", \"scripts/migrate_notes.py\", \"scripts/migrate_note_names_2026.py\", \"scripts/migrate_logseq_json.py\", \"scripts/heal_wikilinks.py\"]}, \"usage_hint\": \"To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'\", \"required_environment_variables\": [], \"required_commands\": [], \"missing_required_environment_variables\": [], \"missing_credential_files\": [], \"missing_required_commands\": [], \"setup_needed\": false, \"setup_skipped\": false, \"readiness_status\": \"available\", \"metadata\": {\"hermes\": {\"tags\": [\"obsidian\", \"core\", \"conventions\", \"paths\", \"frontmatter\", \"triage\", \"sorting\"], \"related_skills\": [\"obsidian-contacts\", \"obsidian-notes\", \"obsidian-inputs\", \"obsidian-utilities\", \"llm-wiki\", \"obsidian-hygiene\"]}}}",

slack_sdk.errors import SlackApiError\\n9|\\n10|CACHE_FILE = os.path.expanduser(\\\"~/.hermes/processed_slack_brains.json\\\")\\n11|\\n12|def load_processed():\\n13|    if Filter and format results\\n290|    new_brains = []\\n291|\\n292|    # Process Comments\\n293|    for node in comment_nodes:\\n294|        node_id = node[\\\"id\\\"]\\n295|        if node_id in processed_set:\\n296|            continue\\n297|        \\n298|        # test_reactions_disabled_by_default(monkeypatch):\\n43|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Telegram reactions should be disabled by default.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n44|    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", raising=False)\\n45|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n46|    assert adapter._reactions_enabled() is False\\n47|\\n48|\\n49|def test_reactions_enabled_when_set_true(monkeypatch):\\n50| adapter._reactions_enabled() is True\\n54|\\n55|\\n56|def test_reactions_enabled_with_1(monkeypatch):\\n57|    \\\"\\\"\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS=1 enables reactions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n58| complete should not react when reactions are disabled.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n211|    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", raising=False)\\n212|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n213|    event = _make_event()\\n214|\\n215|    await adapter.on_processing_complete(event, ProcessingOutcome.SUCCESS)\\n216|\\n217|    adapter._bot.set_message_reaction.assert_not_awaited()\\n218|\\n219|\\n220|@pytest.mark.asyncio\\n221|async def test_on_processing_complete_cancelled_clears_reaction(monkeypatch):\\n222|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Cancelled processing should clear the in-progress reaction.\\n223|\\n224|    Without this clear, the \ud83d\udc40 reaction lingers on the user's message\\n225|    indefinitely (until another agent run swaps it for \ud83d\udc4d/\ud83d\udc4e). On a\\n226|    ``/stop`` that ends a session, that reaction never gets cleaned up.\\n227|    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n228|    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", \\\"true\\\")\\n229|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n230|    event = _make_event()\\n231|\\n232|    await adapter.on_processing_complete(event, ProcessingOutcome.CANCELLED)\\n233|\\n234|    # set_message_reaction with reaction=None clears all reactions on the\\n235|    # message (Bot API documented semantics; equivalent to Bot API 10.0's\\n236|    # deleteMessageReaction but works on PTB 22.6 already).\\n237|    adapter._bot.set_message_reaction.assert_awaited_once_with(\\n238|        chat_id=123,\\n239|        message_id=456,\\n240|        reaction=None,\\n241|    )\\n242|\\n243|\\n244|@pytest.mark.asyncio\\n245|async def test_on_processing_complete_cancelled_skipped_when_disabled(monkeypatch):\\n246|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Cancelled processing should not call the API when reactions are off.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247|    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", raising=False)\\n248|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n249|    event = _make_event()\\n250|\\n251|    await adapter.on_processing_complete(event, ProcessingOutcome.CANCELLED)\\n252|\\n253|    adapter._bot.set_message_reaction.assert_not_awaited()\\n254|\\n255|\\n256|@pytest.mark.asyncio\\n257|async def test_clear_reactions_handles_api_error_gracefully(monkeypatch):\\n258|    \\\"\\\"\\\"API errors during clear should not propagate.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n259|    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", \\\"true\\\")\\n260|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n261|    adapter._bot.set_message_reaction = AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError(\\\"no perms\\\"))\\n262|\\n263|    result = await adapter._clear_reactions(\\\"123\\\", \\\"456\\\")\\n264|    assert result is False\\n265|\\n266|\\n267|@pytest.mark.asyncio\\n268|async def test_clear_reactions_returns_false_without_bot(monkeypatch):\\n269|    \\\"\\\"\\\"_clear_reactions should return False when bot is not available.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n270|    adapter = _make_adapter()\\n271|    adapter._bot = None\\n272|\\n273|    result = await adapter._clear_reactions(\\\"123\\\", \\\"456\\\")\\n274|    assert result is False\\n275|\\n276|\\n277|# \u2500\u2500 config.py bridging \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\\n278|\\n279|\\n280|def test_config_bridges_telegram_reactions(monkeypatch, tmp_path):\\n281|    \\\"\\\"\\\"gateway/config.py bridges telegram.reactions to TELEGRAM_REACTIONS env var.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n282|    import yaml\\n283|    config_file = tmp_path / \\\"config.yaml\\\"\\n284|    config_file.write_text(yaml.dump({\\n285|        \\\"telegram\\\": {\\n286|            \\\"reactions\\\": True,\\n287|        },\\n288|    }))\\n289|    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"HERMES_HOME\\\", str(tmp_path))\\n290|    # Use setenv (not delenv) so monkeypatch registers cleanup even when\\n291|    # the var doesn't exist yet \u2014 load_gateway_config will overwrite it.\\n292|    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n293|\\n294|    from gateway.config import load_gateway_config\\n295|    load_gateway_config()\\n296|\\n297|    import os\\n298|    assert os.getenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\") == \\\"true\\\"\\n299|\\n300|\\n301|def test_config_reactions_env_takes_precedence(monkeypatch, tmp_path):\\n302|    \\\"\\\"\\\"Env var should take precedence over config.yaml for reactions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n303|    import yaml\\n304|    config_file = tmp_path / \\\"config.yaml\\\"\\n305| str(tmp_path))\\n311|    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"TELEGRAM_REACTIONS\\\", \\\"false\\\")\\n312|\\n313|    from gateway.config import load_gateway_config\\n314|    load_gateway_config()\\n315|\\n316| false}\n\n[Subdirectory context discovered: .hermes/hermes-agent/AGENTS.md]\n# Hermes Agent - Development Guide\n\nInstructions for AI coding assistants and developers working on the hermes-agent codebase.\n\n**Never give up on the right solution.**\n\n## Development Environment\n\n```bash\n# Prefer .venv; fall back to venv if that's what your checkout has.\nsource .venv/bin/activate   # or: source venv/bin/activate\n```\n\n`scripts/run_tests.sh` probes `.venv` first, then `venv`, then\n`$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv` (for worktrees that share a venv with the\nmain checkout).\n\n## Project Structure\n\nFile counts shift constantly \u2014 don't treat the tree below as exhaustive.\nThe canonical source is the filesystem. The notes call out the load-bearing\nentry points you'll actually edit.\n\n```\nhermes-agent/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 run_agent.py          # AIAgent class \u2014 core conversation loop (~12k LOC)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 model_tools.py        # Tool orchestration, discover_builtin_tools(), handle_function_call()\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 toolsets.py           # Toolset definitions, _HERMES_CORE_TOOLS list\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cli.py                # HermesCLI class \u2014 interactive CLI orchestrator (~11k LOC)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 hermes_state.py       # SessionDB \u2014 SQLite session store (FTS5 search)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 hermes_constants.py   # get_hermes_home(), display_hermes_home() \u2014 profile-aware paths\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 hermes_logging.py     # setup_logging() \u2014 agent.log / errors.log / gateway.log (profile-aware)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 batch_runner.py       # Parallel batch processing\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 agent/                # Agent internals (provider adapters, memory, caching, compression, etc.)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 hermes_cli/           # CLI subcommands, setup wizard, plugins loader, skin engine\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tools/                # Tool implementations \u2014 auto-discovered via tools/registry.py\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 environments/     # Terminal backends (local, docker, ssh, modal, daytona, singularity)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 gateway/              # Messaging gateway \u2014 run.py + session.py + platforms/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 platforms/        # Adapter per platform (telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp,\n\u2502   \u2502                     #   homeassistant, signal, matrix, mattermost, email, sms,\n\u2502   \u2502                     #   dingtalk, wecom, weixin, feishu, qqbot, bluebubbles,\n\u2502   \u2502                     #   yuanbao, webhook, api_server, ...). See ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md.\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 builtin_hooks/    # Extension point for always-registered gateway hooks (none shipped)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 plugins/              # Plugin system (see \"Plugins\" section below)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 memory/           # Memory-provider plugins (honcho, mem0, supermemory, ...)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 context_engine/   # Context-engine plugins\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 model-providers/  # Inference backend plugins (openrouter, anthropic, gmi, ...)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 kanban/           # Multi-agent board dispatcher + worker plugin\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 hermes-achievements/  # Gamified achievement tracking\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 observability/    # Metrics / traces / logs plugin\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 image_gen/        # Image-generation providers\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 <others>/         # disk-cleanup, google_meet, platforms, spotify,\n\u2502                         #   strike-freedom-cockpit, ...\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 optional-skills/      # Heavier/niche skills shipped but NOT active by default\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 skills/               # Built-in skills bundled with the repo\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ui-tui/               # Ink (React) terminal UI \u2014 `hermes --tui`\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 src/              # entry.tsx, app.tsx, gatewayClient.ts + app/components/hooks/lib\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tui_gateway/          # Python JSON-RPC backend for the TUI\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 acp_adapter/          # ACP server (VS Code / Zed / JetBrains integration)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cron/                 # Scheduler \u2014 jobs.py, scheduler.py\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 scripts/              # run_tests.sh, release.py, auxiliary scripts\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 website/              # Docusaurus docs site\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 tests/                # Pytest suite (~17k tests across ~900 files as of May 2026)\n```\n\n**User config:** `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (settings), `~/.hermes/.env` (API keys only).\n**Logs:** `~/.hermes/logs/` \u2014 `agent.log` (INFO+), `errors.log` (WARNING+),\n`gateway.log` when running the gateway. Profile-aware via `get_hermes_home()`.\nBrowse with `hermes logs [--follow] [--level ...] [--session ...]`.\n\n## TypeScript Style\n\nApplies to TypeScript across Hermes: desktop, TUI, website, and future TS packages.\n\n- Prefer small nanostores over component state when state is shared, reused, or read by distant UI.\n- Let each feature own its atoms. Chat state belongs near chat, shell state near shell, shared state in `src/store`.\n- Components that render from an atom should use `useStore`. Non-rendering actions should read with `$atom.get()`.\n- Do not pass state through three components when the leaf can subscribe to the atom.\n- Keep persistence beside the atom that owns it.\n- Keep route roots thin. They compose routes and shell; they should not become controllers.\n- No monolithic hooks. A hook should own one narrow job.\n- Prefer colocated action modules over hidden god hooks.\n- If a callback is pure side effect, use the terse void form:\n  `onState={st => void setGatewayState(st)}`.\n- Async UI handlers should make intent explicit:\n  `onClick={() => void save()}`.\n- Prefer interfaces for public props and shared object shapes. Avoid `type X = { ... }` for object props.\n- Extend React primitives for props: `React.ComponentProps<'button'>`, `React.ComponentProps<typeof Dialog>`, `Omit<...>`, `Pick<...>`.\n- Table-driven beats condition ladders when mapping ids, routes, or views.\n- `src/app` owns routes, pages, and page-specific components.\n- `src/store` owns shared atoms.\n- `src/lib` owns shared pure helpers.\n\n## File Dependency Chain\n\n```\ntools/registry.py  (no deps \u2014 imported by all tool files)\n       \u2191\ntools/*.py  (each calls registry.register() at import time)\n       \u2191\nmodel_tools.py  (imports tools/registry + triggers tool discovery)\n       \u2191\nrun_agent.py, cli.py, batch_runner.py, environments/\n```\n\n---\n\n## AIAgent Class (run_agent.py)\n\nThe real `AIAgent.__init__` takes ~60 parameters (credentials, routing, callbacks,\nsession context, budget, credential pool, etc.). The signature below is the\nminimum subset you'll usually touch \u2014 read `run_agent.py` for the full list.\n\n```python\nclass AIAgent:\n    def __init__(self,\n        base_url: str = None,\n        api_key: str = None,\n        provider: str = None,\n        api_mode: str = None,              # \"chat_completions\" | \"codex_responses\" | ...\n        model: str = \"\",                   # empty \u2192 resolved from config/provider later\n        max_iterations: int = 90,          # tool-calling iterations (shared with subagents)\n        enabled_toolsets: list = None,\n        disabled_toolsets: list = None,\n        quiet_mode: bool = False,\n        save_trajectories: bool = False,\n        platform: str = None,              # \"cli\", \"telegram\", etc.\n        session_id: str = None,\n        skip_context_files: bool = False,\n        skip_memory: bool = False,\n        credential_pool=None,\n        # ... plus callbacks, thread/user/chat IDs, iteration_budget, fallback_model,\n        # checkpoints config, prefill_messages, service_tier, reasoning_config, etc.\n    ): ...\n\n    def chat(self, message: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Simple interface \u2014 returns final response string.\"\"\"\n\n    def run_conversation(self, user_message: str, system_message: str = None,\n                         conversation_history: list = None, task_id: str = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Full interface \u2014 returns dict with final_response + messages.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### Agent Loop\n\nThe core loop is inside `run_conversation()` \u2014 entirely synchronous, with\ninterrupt checks, budget tracking, and a one-turn grace call:\n\n```python\nwhile (api_call_count < self.max_iterations and self.iteration_budget.remaining > 0) \\\n        or self._budget_grace_call:\n    if self._interrupt_requested: break\n    response = client.chat.completions.create(model=model, messages=messages, tools=tool_schemas)\n    if response.tool_calls:\n        for tool_call in response.tool_calls:\n            result = handle_function_call(tool_call.name, tool_call.args, task_id)\n            messages.append(tool_result_message(result))\n        api_call_count += 1\n    else:\n        return response.content\n```\n\n\n\n[...truncated AGENTS.md: 57,063 chars total]",

Get current columns from the live table\\n692|            try:\\n693|                rows = cursor.execute(\\n694|                    f'PRAGMA table_info(\\\"{table_name}\\\")'\\n695|                ).fetchall()\\n696|            except sqlite3.OperationalError:\\n697|                continue  # Table doesn't exist yet (shouldn't happen after executescript)\\n698|            live_cols = set()\\n699|            for row in rows:\\n700|                # PRAGMA table_info returns (cid, name, type, notnull, dflt_value, pk)\\n701|                name = row[1] if isinstance(row, (tuple, list)) else row[\\\"name\\\"]\\n702|                live_cols.add(name)\\n703|\\n704|            for col_name, col_type in declared_cols.items():\\n705|                if col_name not in live_cols:\\n706|                    safe_name = col_name.replace('\\\"', '\\\"\\\"')\\n707|                    try:\\n708|                        cursor.execute(\\n709|                            f'ALTER TABLE \\\"{table_name}\\\" ADD COLUMN \\\"{safe_name}\\\" {col_type}'\\n710|                        )\\n711|                    except sqlite3.OperationalError as exc:\\n712|                        # Expected: \\\"duplicate column name\\\" from a race or\\n713|                        # re-run.  Unexpected: \\\"Cannot add a NOT NULL column\\n714|                        # with default value NULL\\\" from a schema mistake.\\n715|                        # Log at DEBUG so it's visible in agent.log.\\n716|                        logger.debug(\\n717|                            \\\"reconcile %s.%s: %s\\\", table_name, col_name, exc,\\n718|                        )\\n719|\\n720|    def _init_schema(self):\\n721|        \\\"\\\"\\\"Create tables and FTS if they don't exist, reconcile columns.\\n722|\\n723|        Schema management follows the declarative reconciliation pattern\\n724|        (Beets, sqlite-utils): SCHEMA_SQL is the single source of truth.\\n725|        On existing databases, _reconcile_columns() diffs live columns\\n726|        against SCHEMA_SQL and ADDs any missing ones.  This eliminates\\n727|        the version-gated migration chain for column additions, making\\n728|        it impossible for reordered or inserted migrations to skip columns.\\n729|\\n730|        The schema_version table is retained for future data migrations\\n731|        (transforming existing rows) which cannot be handled declaratively.\\n732|        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n733|        cursor = self._conn.cursor()\\n734|\\n735|        cursor.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\\n736|\\n737|        # \u2500\u2500 Declarative column reconciliation \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\\n738|        # Diff live tables against SCHEMA_SQL and ADD any missing columns.\\n739|        # This is idempotent and self-healing: even if a version-gated\\n740|        # migration was skipped (e.g. due to version renumbering), the\\n741|        # column gets created here.\\n742|        self._reconcile_columns(cursor)\\n743|\\n744|        # Indexes that reference reconciler-added columns must be created\\n745|        # AFTER _reconcile_columns runs \u2014 declaring them in SCHEMA_SQL\\n746|        # makes the initial executescript fail on legacy DBs (the index's\\n747|        # WHERE clause references a column that doesn't exist yet).\\n748|        try:\\n749|            cursor.execute(\\n750|                \\\"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_platform_msg_id \\\"\\n751|                \\\"ON messages(session_id, platform_message_id) \\\"\\n752|                \\\"WHERE platform_message_id IS NOT NULL\\\"\\n753|            )\\n754|        except sqlite3.OperationalError as exc:\\n755|            logger.debug(\\\"idx_messages_platform_msg_id create skipped: %s\\\", exc)\\n756|\\n757|        # Deferred indexes that reference the reconciler-added ``active``\\n758|        # column (idx_messages_session_active) \u2014 same ordering constraint.\\n759|        cursor.executescript(DEFERRED_INDEX_SQL)\\n760|\\n761|        fts5_available = self._sqlite_supports_fts5(cursor)\\n762|        fts_migrations_complete = True\\n763|        if not fts5_available:\\n764|            # Existing FTS triggers can still fire on messages INSERT/UPDATE\\n765|            # even though the current sqlite runtime cannot read the virtual\\n766|            # tables they target. Drop only the triggers so core persistence\\n767|            # continues; if a future runtime has FTS5, _ensure_fts_schema()\\n768|            # recreates them.\\n769|            self._drop_fts_triggers(cursor)\\n770|\", \"total_lines\": 4216, \"file_size\": 180234, \"truncated\": true, \"hint\": \"Use offset=770 to continue reading (showing 670-769 of 4216 lines)\", \"is_binary\": false, \"is_image\": false}",

          "content": "{\"content\": \"1|{\\n2|  \\\"jobs\\\": [\\n3|    {\\n4|      \\\"id\\\": \\\"9e1227d21111\\\",\\n5|      \\\"name\\\": \\\"Bes email dispatch\\\",\\n6|      \\\"prompt\\\": \\\"The poller (output above) has detected new emails Justin forwarded to goff.justin+bes@gmail.com. The script output lists one line per new message id.\\\\n\\\\nFor each id in the list:\\\\n  1. Load the bes-email-dispatch skill if not already loaded.\\\\n  2. Run scripts/load_context.py <MESSAGE_ID> via the venv python (path is in the skill).\\\\n  3. Follow the skill: parse instruction, decide intent, write to the Obsidian vault, verify the file landed.\\\\n  4. If is_real is false, skip and note why.\\\\n  5. For each filed note, load llm-wiki and run integrate-light: append Utilities/log.md only (one line per ingest with daily note wikilink as last field). Do not update index.md or daily notepad. Do not modify Input bodies.\\\\n  6. For each filed note, run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\"]`. Include hub updates in Telegram one-liner: `\\\\u2192 updated [[Project]], [[Contact]]`.\\\\n\\\\nAt the end, reply with one concise line per processed message (success or skip + reason). The reply is delivered to Telegram, so keep it tight \\\\u2014 single line per email, no preamble.\\\\n\\\\nIf the script output above this prompt is empty or says \\\\\\\"no new events,\\\\\\\" reply with literally nothing (empty response). Hermes cron will treat empty as silent.\\\",\\n7|      \\\"skills\\\": [\\n8|        \\\"bes-email-dispatch\\\",\\n9|        \\\"llm-wiki\\\"\\n10|      ],\\n11|      \\\"skill\\\": \\\"bes-email-dispatch\\\",\\n12|      \\\"model\\\": null,\\n13|      \\\"provider\\\": null,\\n14|      \\\"base_url\\\": null,\\n15|      \\\"script\\\": \\\"poll_bes_inbox.py\\\",\\n16|      \\\"no_agent\\\": false,\\n17|      \\\"context_from\\\": null,\\n18|      \\\"schedule\\\": {\\n19|        \\\"kind\\\": \\\"interval\\\",\\n20|        \\\"minutes\\\": 10,\\n21|        \\\"display\\\": \\\"every 10m\\\"\\n22|      },\\n23|      \\\"schedule_display\\\": \\\"every 10m\\\",\\n24|      \\\"repeat\\\": {\\n25|        \\\"times\\\": null,\\n26|        \\\"completed\\\": 2538\\n27|      },\\n28|      \\\"enabled\\\": true,\\n29|      \\\"state\\\": \\\"scheduled\\\",\\n30|      \\\"paused_at\\\": null,\\n31|      \\\"paused_reason\\\": null,\\n32|      \\\"created_at\\\": \\\"2026-05-20T14:56:45.812443-04:00\\\",\\n33|      \\\"next_run_at\\\": \\\"2026-06-11T07:13:22.394685-04:00\\\",\\n34|      \\\"last_run_at\\\": \\\"2026-06-11T07:03:22.394685-04:00\\\",\\n35|      \\\"last_status\\\": \\\"ok\\\",\\n36|      \\\"last_error\\\": null,\\n37|      \\\"last_delivery_error\\\": null,\\n38|      \\\"deliver\\\": \\\"telegram\\\",\\n39|      \\\"origin\\\": null,\\n40|      \\\"enabled_toolsets\\\": null,\\n41|      \\\"workdir\\\": null,\\n42|      \\\"profile\\\": null\\n43|    },\\n44|    {\\n45|      \\\"id\\\": \\\"49fb99de8688\\\",\\n46|      \\\"name\\\": \\\"Daily Work Log\\\",\\n47|      \\\"prompt\\\": \\\"Create a work log for yesterday (the day before today). All source queries, date filters, and the daily note lookup should use yesterday's date. Schema Change\n* **State DB modified:** I identified `/home/justin.guest/.hermes/state.db` as the local SQLite database where all session history is stored.\n* **New columns added:** I added the `brain_flagged` and `ingested` columns to the live `sessions` table.\n* **Declarative sync configured:** I updated `SCHEMA_SQL` in `hermes_state.py` so that any future clean restarts or reinstalls automatically include these columns during startup reconciliation.\n\n### 2. Inbound Telegram Reactions Hooked\n* **Handler registered:** I registered a `MessageReactionHandler` on the running Telegram gateway inside `gateway/platforms/telegram.py` so it receives the `message_reaction` updates (already enabled under `Update.ALL_TYPES`).\n* **Database link established:** I created the async callback `_handle_message_reaction` inside the Telegram platform adapter. When you react to any bot response with the \ud83e\udde0 emoji, the adapter:\n  1. Captures your message ID (`platform_message_id`).\n  2. Resolves it back to its specific SQLite `session_id` in `state.db`.\n  3. Updates that session's state directly, setting `brain_flagged = 1`.\n\n### 3. Next Steps (New Ingest Poller)\nTo finish the pipeline, we need to create the background poller `fetch_telegram_brains.py` inside `/home/justin.guest/.hermes/scripts/` (to match `fetch_slack_brains.py` and `fetch_linear_brains.py`). \n\nThis script will run in the background (via a cron job) and:\n1. Scan the database for any sessions that either:\n   * Have `brain_flagged = 1`\n   * Contain a user message with the `\"\ud83e\udde0\"` string in the `messages` table.\n2. Filter for those that have `ingested = 0` to prevent duplicates.\n3. Export the formatted session details and conversation transcripts as JSON, allowing the background agent to summarize and write them to `Inputs/Telegram/` in your Obsidian vault.\n\nLet me know when you are ready to proceed with authoring the background script and scheduling its cron job!",

### 1. Process Slack Captures (`slack`)
If there are items under the `slack` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the reaction type: `jg_log` or `jg_decision` based on the `"reaction_type"` field in the JSON item.
2. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `ts` or messages timestamps) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
3. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
4. **ALL INGESTED NOTES MUST BE ADDED TO THE INBOX FIRST!**
   - Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/`
5. Generate the Note Content based on Reaction Type:
   - **Path A: If reaction_type is "jg_log":**
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Slack]]"
       channel: "<channel_name>"
       original_url: "<permalink>"
       participants:
         - "<Participant A>"
         - "<Participant B>"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       Keep standard Slack logs minimalist. No point-by-point summaries, thoughts, or decisions in the body. Only:
       ```markdown
       # 📥 Slack Capture: [Title]

       - **Channel:** #<channel_name>
       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Topic Description
       <A brief, 1-2 sentence Topic Description placeholder to be expanded later during Vault Jam.>
       ```
   - **Path B: If reaction_type is "jg_decision":**
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Decisions]]"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       ```markdown
       # 🏛 Decision: [Title]

       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Context
       <Summarize the context of the decision from the Slack thread.>

       ## Options & Trade-offs
       <Extract pros/cons, options, and tradeoffs discussed in the thread.>

       ## Resolution
       <Detail the finalized decision and resolution, attributing it clearly to the actual decision-maker.>
       ```
6. **Daily Note Integration:**
   Append a link and one-sentence gist to today's daily note notepad under `## 🗒 Notepad`:
   - For `jg_log`: `* [[Inbox/YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title]|Slack summary]]: <One-sentence-gist>.`
   - For `jg_decision`: `* [[Inbox/YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title]|Decision]]: <One-sentence-gist>.`
7. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Slack message as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py --mark-processed <channel_id> <ts>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py Inbox/<filename> [--gist "..."]` to sync project hub states.
